questionTextSource: Is it irresponsible to overtake a truck and trailer a short distance before a crossroads?
explanationSource: Under no circumstances should you start an overtaking manoeuvre just before a crossing. There is an increased risk of accidents. This applies especially to overtaking trucks. You may not be able to see cross-traffic or traffic signs.
answerASource: Yes, because the truck and trailer can obscure the view of important traffic signs
answerBSource: Yes, because the truck and trailer can obscure the view of the crossing traffic
answerCSource: No, because trucks and trailers mostly drive at low speed
questionTextTarget: Chorrahaga ozgina qolganda yuk mashinasi va tirkamasini quvib o‘tish mas’uliyatsizlikmi?
explanationTarget: Hech qanday holatda kesishma oldidan quvib o‘tishni boshlamaslik kerak. Avariya xavfi ortadi. Bu ayniqsa yuk mashinalarini quvib o‘tishda to‘g‘ri. Siz yonlama harakatni yoki yo‘l belgilarini ko‘rmay qolishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Ha, chunki yuk mashinasi va tirkama muhim yo‘l belgilarini to‘sib qo‘yishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Ha, chunki yuk mashinasi va tirkama kesishayotgan transport oqimini to‘sib qo‘yishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Yo‘q, chunki yuk mashinalari va tirkamalar odatda past tezlikda yuradi

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to overtake now?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to overtake because the cyclist could turn and the oncoming traffic is not detected in time.
answerASource: Because [the cyclist] could turn off to the left
answerBSource: Because I am not able to see possible oncoming traffic in good enough time
answerCSource: Because of the very poor condition of the left-side road surface
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir quvib o‘tish mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish mumkin emas, chunki velosipedchi burilib ketishi mumkin va qarama-qarshi harakatni vaqtida ko‘rib bo‘lmaydi.
answerBTarget: Chunki qarshidan kelayotgan ehtimoliy transportni vaqtida ko‘ra olmayman
answerCTarget: Chunki yo‘lning chap tomondagi qoplamasi juda yomon

questionTextSource: What mistakes make overtaking dangerous even on clear and straight roads?
explanationSource: Overtaking maneuvers are dangerous even on straight and open roads. This is especially true if oncoming traffic approaches you. The speed of oncoming vehicles is difficult to assess. Often the duration of the overtaking maneuver is underestimated. If you take too long, dangerous situations for you and for others can arise. Before you start overtaking another vehicle, make sure that the opposite lane is clear and you can finish the overtaking maneuver quickly. Otherwise you wait.
answerASource: The distance of an oncoming vehicle is overestimated
answerBSource: You underestimate your overtaking distance
answerCSource: You overestimate your overtaking distance
questionTextTarget: Aniq va to‘g‘ri yo‘llarda ham quvib o‘tishni xavfli qiladigan xatolar qaysilar?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish to‘g‘ri va yaxshi ko‘rinadigan yo‘llarda ham xavfli. Ayniqsa, qarshidan transport yaqinlashganda. Qarshidan kelayotgan transport tezligini baholash qiyin. Ko‘pincha quvib o‘tish davomiyligi kam baholanadi. Agar bu juda uzoq cho‘zilsa, siz va boshqalar uchun xavfli vaziyat yuzaga keladi. Quvib o‘tishni boshlashdan oldin qarshi yo‘lak bo‘sh ekaniga va manevrni tez yakunlay olishingizga ishonch hosil qiling. Aks holda kuting.
answerATarget: Qarshidan kelayotgan transportgacha bo‘lgan masofa ortiqcha baholanadi
answerBTarget: Siz quvib o‘tish masofangizni kam baholaysiz
answerCTarget: Siz quvib o‘tish masofangizni ortiqcha baholaysiz

questionTextSource: When are you in danger when overtaking?
explanationSource: If you notice that the vehicle behind you wants to overtake you, do not increase your own speed under any circumstances. This prolongs the overtaking manoeuvre and can lead to dangerous situations. Before you start to overtake, make sure that the vehicle behind you is not about to overtake you. Otherwise they will have to brake heavily.
answerASource: - suddenly accelerates
answerBSource: - swerves out to overtake
answerCSource: - reduces his speed considerably
questionTextTarget: Quvib o‘tishda qachon xavf ostida qolasiz?
explanationTarget: Agar ortingizdagi transport sizni quvib o‘tmoqchi ekanini sezsangiz, hech qanday holatda o‘zingizning tezligingizni oshirmang. Bu quvib o‘tishni uzaytiradi va xavfli vaziyatlarga olib kelishi mumkin. Quvib o‘tishni boshlashdan oldin ortingizdagi transport sizni quvib o‘tishga tayyor emasligiga ishonch hosil qiling. Aks holda u qattiq tormoz berishga majbur bo‘ladi.

questionTextSource: In which cases must you abandon overtaking?
explanationSource: Sudden oncoming traffic requires that an overtaking manoeuvre be stopped immediately. The same applies if the vehicle to be overtaken accelerates. This means that you can no longer estimate the overtaking distance and endanger oncoming traffic if you continue. If the vehicle being overtaken reduces its speed, there is no danger.
answerASource: When the road user being overtaken suddenly accelerates
answerBSource: When there is danger from unexpected oncoming traffic
answerCSource: When the road user being overtaken considerably reduces his speed
questionTextTarget: Qaysi hollarda quvib o‘tishni to‘xtatishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: To‘satdan paydo bo‘lgan qarama-qarshi harakat quvib o‘tish manevrini darhol to‘xtatishni talab qiladi. Quvib o‘tilayotgan transport tezlashsa ham xuddi shunday. Bu holda quvib o‘tish masofasini endi aniq baholab bo‘lmaydi va davom etsangiz qarshidan kelayotgan transportga xavf tug‘dirasiz. Agar quvib o‘tilayotgan transport tezligini kamaytirsa, xavf yo‘q.
answerATarget: Quvib o‘tilayotgan harakat ishtirokchisi to‘satdan tezlashganda
answerCTarget: Quvib o‘tilayotgan harakat ishtirokchisi tezligini ancha kamaytirganda

questionTextSource: When is overtaking forbidden?
explanationSource:
If you encounter an unclear traffic situation, overtaking is prohibited. This applies in particular:
- On bends
- On unclear stretches of road
- Immediately before and at junctions where the right of way is unclear
- When the traffic situation is unclear
- If you cannot judge exactly what the vehicle in front is doing.
- If it would take you too long to overtake because your vehicle is not fast enough.
Before overtaking, make sure that you do not endanger other road users and oncoming traffic.
answerASource: If you are unable to drive considerably faster than the road user being overtaken
answerBSource: If you do not have a clear view of the road ahead
answerCSource: If the traffic situation is not clear
questionTextTarget: Qachon quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Agar vaziyat noaniq bo‘lsa, quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi. Bu, ayniqsa: burilishlarda; ko‘rinishi noaniq yo‘l qismlarida; ustunlik noaniq bo‘lgan kesishma va chorrahalar oldida va ularda; umumiy vaziyat noaniq bo‘lsa; oldingizdagi transport nima qilayotganini aniq baholay olmasangiz; sizning transport vositangiz yetarlicha tez bo‘lmagani uchun quvib o‘tish juda uzoq cho‘zilsa amal qiladi. Quvib o‘tishdan oldin boshqa harakat ishtirokchilariga va qarama-qarshi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasligingizga ishonch hosil qiling.
answerATarget: Agar quvib o‘tilayotgan harakat ishtirokchisidan ancha tez yura olmasam
answerBTarget: Agar oldindagi yo‘lni aniq ko‘ra olmasam
answerCTarget: Agar yo‘l harakati vaziyati aniq bo‘lmasa

questionTextSource: Why can it be dangerous to overtake even on clearly visible, straight roads?
explanationSource: On a straight stretch of road, you may be able to see oncoming traffic from a distance, but often underestimate their speed. If an overtaking manoeuvre takes too long, dangerous situations can arise. If there is oncoming traffic, it is better to wait until the road is clear before overtaking.
answerASource: - oncoming traffic is travelling faster than first thought
answerBSource: - the overtaking distance is longer than first thought
answerCSource: - oncoming traffic is travelling slower than first thought
questionTextTarget: Nega yaxshi ko‘rinadigan, to‘g‘ri yo‘llarda ham quvib o‘tish xavfli bo‘lishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Yo‘lning to‘g‘ri qismida siz qarshidan kelayotgan transportni uzoqdan ko‘rishingiz mumkin, ammo uning tezligini ko‘pincha kam baholaysiz. Agar quvib o‘tish manevri juda uzoq davom etsa, xavfli vaziyatlar yuzaga kelishi mumkin. Agar qarshidan transport bo‘lsa, yo‘l butunlay bo‘shaguncha kutganingiz ma’qul.
answerATarget: - qarshidan kelayotgan transport kutilgandan tezroq harakatlanmoqda
answerCTarget: - qarshidan kelayotgan transport kutilgandan sekinroq harakatlanmoqda

questionTextSource: What should you do in this situation?
explanationSource: You must not overtake the cyclist: you must not cross the continuous centre line under any circumstances, not even partially. Likewise, you must not use a horn here.
answerASource: I remain behind the cyclist
answerBSource: I overtake the cyclist
answerCSource: I sound my horn, so that the cyclist makes way
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz velosipedchini quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak: uzluksiz markaziy chiziqni hech qanday holatda, hatto qisman ham bosib o‘tish mumkin emas. Xuddi shuningdek, bu yerda signal ham berish mumkin emas.
answerCTarget: Velosipedchi yo‘l bersin deb signal chalaman

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: You just want to start overtaking the cyclist, when the blue car suddenly drives out of its parking space. Apparently, he has not noticed the cyclist yet. It might happen that the cyclist now moves to the left, without looking back. Immediately stop the overtaking maneuver to avoid endangering the cyclist.
answerASource: I abort the overtaking manoeuvre
answerBSource: I perform the overtaking manoeuvre quickly
answerCSource: I perform the overtaking manoeuvre cautiously
questionTextTarget: Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz velosipedchini quvib o‘tishni endi boshlamoqchi bo‘lganingizda, ko‘k avtomobil to‘satdan to‘xtash joyidan chiqib ketadi. Ko‘rinishidan u velosipedchini hali payqamagan. Velosipedchi orqasiga qaramasdan chapga siljishi mumkin. Velosipedchiga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun quvib o‘tish manevrini darhol to‘xtating.
answerCTarget: Quvib o‘tish manevrini ehtiyotkorlik bilan bajaraman

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: Your lane is blocked by a stopped vehicle. You must swerve into the oncoming lane to get around it. Pay particular attention to oncoming traffic. The cyclist in front of you will also go around the obstacle and may not indicate this by hand signals.
answerASource: The cyclist will drive past to the left of the parked car
answerBSource: Oncoming traffic
answerCSource: The cyclist will dismount to allow me to pass
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Sizning yo‘lagingiz to‘xtab turgan transport vositasi bilan to‘silgan. Uni aylanib o‘tish uchun qarama-qarshi yo‘lakka chiqishingiz kerak. Qarshidan kelayotgan transportga alohida e’tibor bering. Oldingizdagi velosipedchi ham to‘siqni aylanib o‘tadi va buni qo‘l ishorasi bilan bildirmasligi mumkin.
answerATarget: Velosipedchi to‘xtab turgan avtomobilning chap tomonidan o‘tadi
answerCTarget: Velosipedchi men o‘tishim uchun tushib qoladi

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate, if you want to overtake?
explanationSource: On snow-covered roads, the braking and stopping distance is longer and there is also an increased risk of slipping. Cyclists in particular have difficulty riding safely. Their risk of falling is significantly increased. Keep a greater distance from the cyclist, also because his stopping distance is very long.
answerASource: With the unsafe riding manner of the cyclist
answerBSource: With an extended stopping distance
answerCSource: With the risk of skidding
questionTextTarget: Agar quvib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qor bilan qoplangan yo‘llarda tormoz va to‘xtash masofasi uzunroq bo‘ladi hamda sirpanish xavfi oshadi. Ayniqsa velosipedchilar xavfsiz yurishda qiynaladi. Ularning yiqilish xavfi sezilarli darajada ortadi. Velosipedchidan kattaroq masofa saqlang, chunki uning to‘xtash masofasi ham juda uzun.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What should you prepare yourself for?
explanationSource: A cyclist might shake as one-handed driving and the heavy load on the rack make his bike unstable. If the front cyclist turns around, it will likely make him swing to the left and take up more space than usual. You should only drive past the cyclists with a safe distance of at least two meters.
answerASource: The cyclist will come to a sudden stop right in front of me
answerBSource: The cyclist farther in front will swerve to the left
answerCSource: Oncoming traffic will appear suddenly
questionTextTarget: Siz quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Nimaga tayyor turishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchi bir qo‘l bilan boshqarayotgani va yuk ortilgani sabab velosiped beqaror bo‘lishi mumkin. Oldindagi velosipedchi orqasiga qarasa, ehtimol chapga og‘ib, odatdagidan ko‘proq joy egallaydi. Velosipedchilar yonidan kamida ikki metr xavfsiz yon masofa bilan o‘ting.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake cyclists on a narrow road. The width of the road means that the maximum possible space between them and the car is 50 cm. What should you do?
explanationSource: To avoid danger, as a vehicle driver you must always keep a sufficient safety distance of at least 1.50 metres when overtaking cyclists. If it is not possible to pass the cyclist at such a distance, you must not overtake him under any circumstances.
answerASource: I refrain from overtaking
answerBSource: I give a short warning signal before overtaking
answerCSource: I overtake at a moderate speed
questionTextTarget: Siz tor yo‘lda velosipedchilarni quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Yo‘lning kengligi sabab avtomobil bilan ular orasidagi eng katta masofa 50 sm bo‘lishi mumkin. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Xavfni oldini olish uchun haydovchi velosipedchini quvib o‘tishda har doim kamida 1,50 metr yetarli xavfsiz yon masofani saqlashi kerak. Agar bunday masofada o‘tishning imkoni bo‘lmasa, hech qanday holatda quvib o‘tmang.
answerBTarget: Quvib o‘tishdan oldin qisqa ogohlantiruvchi signal beraman

questionTextSource: You want to overtake cyclists. What should be noted when selecting the distance between the cyclists and the car?
explanationSource: If you want to overtake a cyclist, you must consider the following with regard to the lateral distance: The side distance must be at least 1.5 m, more if the cyclist's behaviour is conspicuous. The reasons for this are possible oscillating movements of the cyclist, but also the possible suction effect of your vehicle at higher speed. Honking as well as overtaking at a lower lateral distance are considered gross negligence.
answerASource: How the cyclists are acting
answerBSource: The speed of my vehicle
answerCSource: 1.0 m between the cyclist and the car is always sufficient
questionTextTarget: Siz velosipedchilarni quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Velosipedchi bilan avtomobil orasidagi masofani tanlashda nimaga e’tibor berish kerak?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchini quvib o‘tishda yon masofa bo‘yicha quyidagilarni hisobga olish kerak: yon masofa kamida 1,5 m bo‘lishi kerak, velosipedchining harakati beqaror bo‘lsa bundan ham ko‘proq. Buning sababi velosipedchining tebranma harakatlari va sizning avtomobilingiz yuqori tezlikda hosil qiladigan havo tortishi bo‘lishi mumkin. Signal berish va juda kam yon masofada quvib o‘tish qo‘pol beparvolik hisoblanadi.
answerCTarget: Velosipedchi bilan avtomobil orasidagi 1,0 m doim yetarli

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: The cyclist will overtake the lorry and pull out to do so. It is too dangerous to overtake him before the bend, as you may not yet be able to see oncoming vehicles. Only overtake the cyclist after the bend. As a general rule, you may only overtake if you can see that there is no danger to or obstruction of oncoming traffic during the entire overtaking process.
answerASource: - after the bend
answerBSource: - before drawing level with the [truck]
answerCSource: - between [truck] and bend
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday bo‘ladi?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchi yuk mashinasini quvib o‘tadi va buning uchun chapga chiqadi. Burilishdan oldin uni quvib o‘tish juda xavfli, chunki qarshidan kelayotgan transportni hali ko‘ra olmasligingiz mumkin. Velosipedchini faqat burilishdan keyin quvib o‘ting. Umuman olganda, quvib o‘tish faqat butun manevr davomida qarshidan transportga xavf yoki to‘sqinlik bo‘lmasligiga amin bo‘lsangizgina mumkin.

questionTextSource: In which situation is overtaking prohibited?
explanationSource: Overtaking is prohibited here, because in these situations you greatly increase the risk of an accident as the person overtaking.
answerASource: - cannot complete the overtaking manoeuvre before the start of a no-overtaking section
answerBSource: - cannot complete the overtaking manoeuvre before a hill crest
answerCSource: - am unable to maintain the necessary space to the side
questionTextTarget: Qaysi vaziyatda quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Bu yerda quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi, chunki bunday vaziyatlarda quvib o‘tuvchi sifatida avariya xavfini keskin oshirasiz.
answerATarget: - quvib o‘tish manevrini quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan hudud boshlanishidan oldin yakunlay olmasam
answerBTarget: - quvib o‘tish manevrini tepalik cho‘qqisigacha yakunlay olmasam

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What should you be prepared for now?
explanationSource: The cyclist directly in front of you wants to cross onto the pedestrian path, in order to cross the kerb the cyclist will most likely stop first. The distant cyclist shows by his hand gesture that he wants to move into the left turn lane.
answerASource: [The cyclist] directly in front of me, stops on the roadway
answerBSource: [The] [cyclist] in the distance pulls out to the left
answerCSource: [The blue car] overtakes me
questionTextTarget: Siz quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Hozir nimaga tayyor turishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Sizga eng yaqin velosipedchi bordyurdan oshib piyodalar yo‘lagiga o‘tmoqchi, buning uchun ehtimol avval to‘xtaydi. Uzoqroqda ketayotgan velosipedchi esa qo‘l ishorasi bilan chapga burilish yo‘lagiga chiqmoqchi ekanini ko‘rsatmoqda.

questionTextSource: Why can it be dangerous to overtake on an unobscured, straight road?
explanationSource: If you misjudge the speed and distance of an oncoming vehicle, your overtaking distance is longer than expected. This can cause accidents.
answerASource: - estimate the distance away of an oncoming vehicle as being too great
answerBSource: - estimate the speed of an oncoming vehicle as being too low
answerCSource: - estimate my overtaking distance to be shorter than it is
questionTextTarget: Ko‘rinishi ochiq, to‘g‘ri yo‘lda ham nega quvib o‘tish xavfli bo‘lishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Agar qarshidan kelayotgan transportning tezligi va masofasini noto‘g‘ri baholasangiz, quvib o‘tish masofangiz kutilgandan uzunroq bo‘ladi. Bu avariyaga sabab bo‘lishi mumkin.
answerATarget: - qarshidan kelayotgan transportgacha bo‘lgan masofani haddan ortiq katta deb baholasam
answerBTarget: - qarshidan kelayotgan transport tezligini juda past deb baholasam
answerCTarget: - quvib o‘tish masofamni haqiqatdagidan qisqaroq deb baholasam

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The vehicle overtaking you has estimated its overtaking path too short. You should reduce your speed so that the overtaking person can re-enter earlier and that the risk of an accident is reduced.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I move to the right, onto the parking strip
answerCSource: I maintain my current speed
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday bo‘ladi?
explanationTarget: Sizni quvib o‘tayotgan transport quvib o‘tish yo‘lini yetarlicha baholamagan. U tezroq o‘z yo‘lagiga qayta kirishi va avariya xavfi kamayishi uchun siz tezligingizni pasaytirishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why may you not overtake in this situation?
explanationSource: The overtaking lane is not visible due to the cyclists and the course of the road. If you cannot see the overtaking lane, you must not overtake. As you cannot go much faster than the cyclists, the overtaking lane becomes much longer and therefore more dangerous.
answerASource: Because the overtaking distance is obscured
answerBSource: Because I am travelling only a little faster than the cyclists
answerCSource: Because there is a no-overtaking sign
questionTextTarget: Nega bu vaziyatda quvib o‘tishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchilar va yo‘lning holati sabab quvib o‘tish yo‘lagi ko‘rinmayapti. Agar quvib o‘tish yo‘lagini ko‘rmasangiz, quvib o‘tishingiz mumkin emas. Shuningdek, siz velosipedchilardan uncha tez yurmayotganingiz uchun quvib o‘tish yo‘li ancha uzayadi va shu sabab yanada xavfli bo‘ladi.
answerBTarget: Chunki men velosipedchilardan uncha tez emasman
answerCTarget: Chunki quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan belgi bor

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to overtake now?
explanationSource: The cyclist swings to the left and looks over his shoulder. He will probably turn left. Reduce your speed to give the cyclist enough space and ensure that he does not feel pressurised. In this way, you will also avoid endangering the cyclist in the event of an abrupt braking manoeuvre and possible rear-end collisions due to a lack of distance.
answerASource: Because [the cyclist] could turn off to the left
answerBSource: Because it will impede oncoming traffic
answerCSource: Because there is a no-overtaking sign
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir quvib o‘tishga ruxsat yo‘q?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchi chapga og‘ib, orqasiga qarayapti. Ehtimol u chapga buriladi. Velosipedchiga yetarli joy berish va u o‘zini siquv ostida his qilmasligi uchun tezlikni kamaytiring. Shu bilan birga, keskin tormozlash yoki masofa yetishmasligi sabab yuz berishi mumkin bo‘lgan to‘qnashuvlardan saqlanasiz.
answerBTarget: Chunki bu qarama-qarshi harakatga xalal beradi
answerCTarget: Chunki quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan belgi bor

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for when a truck in front of you wishes to turn right into a narrow street?
explanationSource: Keep a large distance from the lorry and under no circumstances overtake on the right-hand side, even if you would have the opportunity to do so as a two-wheeler driver. The road train must first reduce its speed considerably to turn. For reasons of space, the truck driver may then have to steer the towing vehicle far to the left so that the trailer or semi-trailer does not roll over the kerb when turning.
answerASource: - reduce speed drastically
answerBSource: - swing out to the left before turning
answerCSource: - move well over to the right
questionTextTarget: Oldingizdagi yuk mashinasi tor ko‘chaga o‘ngga burilmoqchi bo‘lsa, nimaga tayyor bo‘lishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasidan katta masofa saqlang va hech qanday holatda o‘ng tomondan quvib o‘tmang, hatto ikki g‘ildirakli transportda bunga imkon bo‘lsa ham. Avtopoezd burilish qilish uchun avval tezligini ancha kamaytirishi kerak. Joy yetishmagani sabab yuk mashinasi haydovchisi tirkama yoki yarim tirkama bordyurga chiqib ketmasligi uchun tirkovchi qismini ancha chapga olib chiqishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: What do you have to particularly remember at crossroads and junctions in a built-up area?
explanationSource: By driving on a wide and well-developed road, you don't automatically have the right of way. The rule "right before left" does not apply always and everywhere. Instead, signs or traffic lights regulate who has priority. Therefore, pay attention to the signposted right-of-way regulation or the traffic lights at each individual intersection and junction.
answerASource: Intersecting roads may have priority even though they are narrow and less well developed
answerBSource: A better developed road always has priority
answerCSource: The rule "right before left" applies without exception at all crossroads and junctions
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidagi chorraha va kesishmalarda ayniqsa nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Keng va yaxshi qurilgan yo‘lda haydash sizga avtomatik ravishda ustunlik bermaydi. “O‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi ham har doim va hamma joyda amal qilmaydi. Aksincha, kimning ustunligi belgilar yoki svetoforlar bilan belgilanadi. Shuning uchun har bir chorraha va kesishmada belgilangan ustunlik qoidalariga yoki svetoforga e’tibor bering.
answerATarget: Kesishuvchi yo‘llar tor va kamroq rivojlangan bo‘lsa ham ustunlikka ega bo‘lishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Yaxshiroq qurilgan yo‘l har doim ustunlikka ega
answerCTarget: “O‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi barcha chorraha va kesishmalarda istisnosiz amal qiladi

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for?
explanationSource: The wet leaves on the road significantly increase the braking distance of your vehicle. Adjust your speed accordingly. Also keep in mind that due to the non-existent priority traffic signs, the "Left yields to right" rule applies. You would have to give priority to a vehicle coming from the right, even if the driver is slow and hesitant to approach the intersection due to the weather.
answerASource: The appearance of a vehicle from the right
answerBSource: A longer braking distance on account of the wet, leaf-covered road
answerCSource: A vehicle coming from the right will give way despite having priority
questionTextTarget: Nimaga tayyor bo‘lishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘ldagi ho‘l barglar avtomobilingizning tormoz yo‘lini sezilarli uzaytiradi. Tezlikni shunga moslashtiring. Shuni ham unutmangki, ustunlikni ko‘rsatuvchi belgilar bo‘lmagani uchun “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi amal qiladi. Hatto ob-havo sabab o‘ngdan kelayotgan haydovchi kesishmaga sekin va ikkilangan holda yaqinlashsa ham, unga yo‘l berishingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: Ho‘l, barg bilan qoplangan yo‘l sabab tormoz yo‘li uzayishiga
answerCTarget: O‘ngdan kelayotgan transport ustunlikka ega bo‘lsa ham yo‘l berishiga

questionTextSource: Why must you be particularly careful here?
explanationSource: The wet leaves on the road result in a longer braking distance and a high risk of slipping. Therefore, reduce your speed and drive carefully before reaching the intersection. A vehicle could be coming from the right and you should give it the right of way. Since there are no signs at the intersection that give you the right of way, the rule here is "right before left".
answerASource: Because there is an increased danger of skidding when braking
answerBSource: Because a vehicle could come from the right
answerCSource: Because the car coming from the left has priority
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda ayniqsa ehtiyot bo‘lishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘ldagi ho‘l barglar tormoz yo‘lini uzaytiradi va sirpanish xavfini oshiradi. Shuning uchun kesishmaga yetib borguncha tezlikni kamaytiring va ehtiyotkorlik bilan haydang. O‘ngdan transport kelishi mumkin va siz unga yo‘l berishingiz kerak. Kesishmada sizga ustunlik beruvchi belgilar yo‘qligi sabab bu yerda “o‘ngdan kelganga yo‘l ber” qoidasi amal qiladi.
answerCTarget: Chunki chapdan kelayotgan avtomobil ustunlikka ega

questionTextSource: You want to pull out of a row of parked vehicles into the stream of moving traffic. Behind you is a parked truck and trailer. How do you assess this situation?
explanationSource: Because of the truck, you can hardly see the traffic behind you. It also obscures the view of the flowing traffic on your vehicle. You must therefore be particularly careful and slowly feel your way on the road. You may need to go back to avoid hazards.
answerASource: Because of your restricted view, you cannot see approaching vehicles until they are very close
answerBSource: Because of their restricted view, vehicles approaching from behind cannot see your vehicle until very late
answerCSource: It is safe to pull out here because you are protected by the truck and trailer behind you
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘xtab turgan avtomobillar qatoridan yo‘l oqimiga chiqmoqchisiz. Orqangizda esa to‘xtab turgan yuk mashinasi va tirkama bor. Bu vaziyatni qanday baholaysiz?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi sabab orqadan kelayotgan harakatni zo‘rg‘a ko‘rasiz. U sizning avtomobilingizni ham oqimdagi transportdan to‘sib qo‘yadi. Shu sabab siz ayniqsa ehtiyot bo‘lishingiz va yo‘lga asta-sekin chiqishingiz kerak. Xavfdan qochish uchun kerak bo‘lsa, yana ortga qaytishingiz ham mumkin.
answerATarget: Ko‘rinish cheklangani sabab yaqinlashayotgan transport vositalarini ular juda yaqinlashgandagina ko‘ra olasiz
answerBTarget: Ko‘rinish cheklangani sabab orqadan kelayotgan transport vositalari sizning avtomobilingizni juda kech ko‘radi
answerCTarget: Orqadagi yuk mashinasi va tirkama himoya qilgani uchun bu yerda chiqish xavfsiz

questionTextSource: You are approaching a school. Children are leaving the school grounds. What must you reckon with?
explanationSource: In the vicinity of schools, you must always be prepared for children crossing the road abruptly. Children are not yet able to assess themselves and other road users very well and often react impulsively and in a way that is not appropriate for traffic. As a car driver, you must adapt your driving style accordingly in order to exclude any danger to children. In addition to driving at a low speed, you must be particularly attentive and ready to brake at all times.
answerASource: - rush onto the road without thinking
answerBSource: - ride their bicycles onto the road without paying attention
answerCSource: - always conduct themselves properly on the road
questionTextTarget: Siz maktabga yaqinlashyapsiz. Bolalar maktab hududidan chiqib kelmoqda. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Maktablar yaqinida siz doimo bolalar to‘satdan yo‘lni kesib o‘tishiga tayyor bo‘lishingiz kerak. Bolalar o‘zlarini va boshqa harakat ishtirokchilarini hali yaxshi baholay olmaydi va ko‘pincha impulsiv hamda yo‘l harakatiga mos kelmaydigan tarzda harakat qiladi. Haydovchi sifatida bolalarga hech qanday xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun haydash uslubingizni shunga moslashtirishingiz kerak. Past tezlikda yurishdan tashqari, ayniqsa diqqatli bo‘lib, har doim tormozlashga tayyor turishingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: - e’tibor bermasdan velosipedlarini yo‘lga olib chiqishiga
answerCTarget: - har doim yo‘lda to‘g‘ri harakat qilishiga

questionTextSource: What do hazard warning lights draw your attention to?
explanationSource: The use of the hazard warning lights is subject to strict rules. You may only switch on the hazard warning lights to secure your broken-down vehicle or to indicate a traffic jam or a school bus. A school bus with its hazard warning lights on may only be overtaken at walking speed.
answerASource: To vehicles which have broken down
answerBSource: To school buses with children boarding or alighting
answerCSource: To a traffic congestion
questionTextTarget: Avariya chiroqlari nimaga e’tiboringizni qaratadi?
explanationTarget: Avariya chiroqlaridan foydalanish qat’iy qoidalarga bo‘ysunadi. Siz ularni faqat buzilib qolgan transport vositangizni belgilash yoki tirbandlikni yoxud maktab avtobusini ko‘rsatish uchun yoqishingiz mumkin. Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan maktab avtobusidan faqat piyoda tezligida o‘tish mumkin.
answerBTarget: Bolalar chiqayotgan yoki tushayotgan maktab avtobuslariga

questionTextSource: A big combine harvester in front of you wishes to turn right. What is correct?
explanationSource: The combine harvester needs to gain amplitude to turn because the turn is very narrow. To do this, it will greatly reduce his speed. Keep your distance and also drive slowly and be prepared to brake. Especially for cyclists: Never overtake on the right!
answerASource: - will swing far out to the left when turning
answerBSource: - will slow down drastically before turning
answerCSource: - cannot swing out due to rear-wheel steering
questionTextTarget: Oldingizdagi katta kombayn o‘ngga burilmoqchi. Qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Kombayn burilish juda tor bo‘lgani uchun kengroq yoy olib burilishi kerak. Buning uchun u tezligini ancha kamaytiradi. Masofa saqlang, o‘zingiz ham sekin yuring va tormozga tayyor bo‘ling. Ayniqsa velosipedchilar uchun: hech qachon o‘ng tomondan quvib o‘tmang!
answerCTarget: - orqa g‘ildirakli boshqaruv sabab keng chiqolmaydi

questionTextSource: You are driving behind a farm vehicle with a trailer carrying a high load. What must you reckon with?
explanationSource: If you are riding behind an agricultural vehicle with a heavily loaded trailer, you must expect the following: Part of the load could fall onto the roadway when turning or braking. The indicator could be dirty or covered by the load. The braking distance of the vehicle is increased by the weight of the load.
answerASource: Particularly when braking or turning, parts of the load can fall off
answerBSource: The indicators may be concealed, making it difficult to see when the combination of vehicles wishes to turn off
answerCSource: The braking distance of the combination of vehicles is particularly short because of its load
questionTextTarget: Siz baland yuk ortilgan tirkamali qishloq xo‘jaligi transport vositasi ortidan ketyapsiz. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar siz og‘ir yuk ortilgan tirkamali qishloq xo‘jaligi transporti ortidan yursangiz, quyidagilarni kutishingiz kerak: burilganda yoki tormozlaganda yukning bir qismi yo‘lga tushib ketishi mumkin. Burilish ko‘rsatkichlari ifloslangan yoki yuk bilan yopilgan bo‘lishi mumkin. Yuk og‘irligi sabab transportning tormoz yo‘li uzayadi.
answerATarget: Ayniqsa tormozlaganda yoki burilganda yukning bir qismi tushib ketishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Burilish ko‘rsatkichlari yopilib qolishi mumkin, natijada transport vositalari to‘plami qachon burilmoqchi ekanini ko‘rish qiyinlashadi
answerCTarget: Yuk sabab transport vositalari to‘plamining tormoz yo‘li ayniqsa qisqa bo‘ladi

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: You must expect to have to stop before the intersection when turning. For one thing, the view of the bus coming from the right is obstructed. In addition, other vehicles could pass it. In addition, pedestrians may also want to cross the road. You must always show special consideration for them.
answerASource: Pedestrians crossing the road
answerBSource: A vehicle driving past the bus
answerCSource: That I will have to stop at the junction
questionTextTarget: Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilayotganda kesishma oldida to‘xtashga to‘g‘ri kelishini kutishingiz kerak. Birinchidan, o‘ngdan kelayotgan avtobus ko‘rinmayapti. Bundan tashqari, uning yonidan boshqa transport vositalari ham o‘tib ketishi mumkin. Shuningdek, piyodalar ham yo‘lni kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lishi mumkin. Ularga har doim alohida e’tibor ko‘rsatishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What should you do in this situation?
explanationSource: The driver of the vehicle coming out of the exit cannot see the cross traffic. He would have to be directed into the traffic. He could drive further ahead and prevent you from continuing your journey. You should reduce your speed, be ready to brake, even stop if necessary.
answerASource: Reduce speed
answerBSource: Be ready to brake
answerCSource: Continue driving as before
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chiqish joyidan chiqayotgan haydovchi ko‘ndalang harakatni ko‘ra olmaydi. Uni harakat oqimiga yo‘naltirish kerak bo‘lardi. U oldinga chiqib, sizning yo‘lingizni to‘sib qo‘yishi mumkin. Siz tezlikni kamaytirishingiz, tormozga tayyor turishingiz, kerak bo‘lsa to‘xtashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Why do you have to wait before turning right?
explanationSource: If you want to turn right, you will need to let all the vehicles drive past that meet you. This also applies to bicycles that go into the same direction as you. Likewise, you must give priority to pedestrians who want to cross the road in front of you.
answerASource: - the pedestrian
answerBSource: - the cyclist
answerCSource: - the van
questionTextTarget: Nega o‘ngga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar o‘ngga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, qarshingizdan kelayotgan barcha transport vositalarini o‘tkazishingiz kerak. Bu siz bilan bir yo‘nalishda ketayotgan velosipedlarga ham taalluqli. Shuningdek, oldingizdan yo‘lni kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lgan piyodalarga ham yo‘l berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: Because the oncoming vehicle is being slowed down by a bicycle, you should expect the vehicle to overtake the bicycle. If this happens, the vehicle could cross into your lane. In the event that the driver does not brake, you should keep as far to the right on the road as possible. Also, be prepared to brake so that you can react to any situation as quickly as possible.
answerASource: - pull out and cross the centre of the road
answerBSource: - brake and not cross the centre of the road
answerCSource: - overtake without crossing the centre of the road
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qarama-qarshi tomondan kelayotgan transport vositasi velosiped sabab sekinlashayotgani uchun u velosipedchini quvib o‘tishini kutishingiz kerak. Shunday bo‘lsa, u sizning yo‘lagingizga chiqib ketishi mumkin. Agar haydovchi tormoz bermasa, yo‘lning iloji boricha o‘ng chetida yuring. Shuningdek, har qanday vaziyatga tez javob bera olish uchun tormozga tayyor bo‘ling.
answerATarget: - chetga chiqib, yo‘l markazidan o‘tib ketishiga

questionTextSource: You want to turn right at the junction. What is the right thing to do?
explanationSource:
There is a protective lane for cyclists between your lane and the turning lane. It is part of the carriageway and is separated by a broken line.
You may only use the protective lane to change lanes, swerve or turn. If you use it, you are not allowed to obstruct cyclists or come to a standstill in the protective lane. You should therefore change lanes as early as possible.
answerASource: I change lanes as early as possible
answerBSource: I may not impede cyclists in the cycle lane
answerCSource: I may only cross over the cycle lane at walking speed
questionTextTarget: Siz chorrahada o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Sizning yo‘lagingiz bilan burilish yo‘lagi orasida velosipedchilar uchun himoya yo‘lagi bor. U qatnov qismining bir qismi bo‘lib, uzilgan chiziq bilan ajratilgan. Himoya yo‘lagidan faqat yo‘lak almashtirish, chetlab o‘tish yoki burilish uchun foydalanishingiz mumkin. Undan foydalansangiz, velosipedchilarga xalaqit bera olmaysiz va himoya yo‘lagida to‘xtab qolishingiz ham mumkin emas. Shuning uchun yo‘lakni imkon qadar erta almashtirishingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: Velosiped yo‘lagidagi velosipedchilarga xalaqit bera olmayman
answerCTarget: Velosiped yo‘lagidan faqat piyoda tezligida kesib o‘tishim mumkin

questionTextSource: When could a dangerous situation arise here?
explanationSource: You can see that the cycle path ends immediately in front of you. The cyclist would have to stop and wait until she can safely merge into the flowing traffic. However, rather than stopping, expect her to swing into the lane in front of you.
answerASource: If I continue driving at the same speed
answerBSource: If [the cyclist] changes to my lane
answerCSource: If [the cyclist] stops at the end of the cycle path
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda qachon xavfli vaziyat yuzaga kelishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Velosiped yo‘lagi darhol sizning oldingizda tugayotganini ko‘ryapsiz. Velosipedchi oqimdagi harakatga xavfsiz qo‘shila olguncha to‘xtab kutishi kerak bo‘lardi. Biroq to‘xtash o‘rniga, uning to‘g‘ridan-to‘g‘ri oldingizga yo‘lakka chiqishini kuting.
answerATarget: Agar men xuddi shu tezlikda davom etsam

questionTextSource: What must you watch out for when turning right here?
explanationSource: Before you can turn right, you have to let the cyclist on the bike path to your right pass by. Cyclists have priority when they drive in your direction. Cyclists in oncoming traffic have no priority. Nevertheless, watch out for them to avoid danger.
answerASource: For the cyclist
answerBSource: For oncoming traffic
answerCSource: For the motorbike
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda o‘ngga burilganda nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: O‘ngga burilishdan oldin o‘ng tomoningizdagi veloyo‘lakdagi velosipedchini o‘tkazishingiz kerak. Velosipedchilar sizning yo‘nalishingizda harakatlanayotgan bo‘lsa, ustunlikka ega. Qarama-qarshi yo‘nalishdagi velosipedchilarda ustunlik yo‘q. Shunga qaramay, xavfning oldini olish uchun ularga ham e’tibor bering.

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: Since there is a car in the blind spot to the left of you and on the other side a truck wants to turn right into the construction site, you should stop. Do not drive on the middle lane. Wait for the truck to clear the road. Then you can continue.
answerASource: I stop
answerBSource: I drive carefully past to the right of the truck
answerCSource: I move into the middle lane now
questionTextTarget: Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chap tomoningizdagi ko‘r nuqtada avtomobil bor, boshqa tomonda esa yuk mashinasi qurilish maydoniga o‘ngga burilmoqchi. Shuning uchun siz to‘xtashingiz kerak. O‘rta yo‘lakdan yurmang. Yuk mashinasi yo‘lni bo‘shatguncha kuting. Shundan keyin davom etishingiz mumkin.
answerBTarget: Yuk mashinasining o‘ngidan ehtiyotkorlik bilan o‘taman

questionTextSource: What should you note about electric bicycles (e-bikes) participating in road traffic?
explanationSource: E-bikes are just as quiet as classic bikes and are therefore often perceived late. However, they often achieve significantly higher speeds than bicycles without an engine. This is underestimated by many road users, so dangerous situations can occur surprisingly. In general, expect cyclists to gain speed and perform speedy maneuvers.
answerASource: - be deceptively fast
answerBSource: - look very similar to conventional bicycles
answerCSource: - be easily recognised due to their engine noise
questionTextTarget: Yo‘l harakatida qatnashayotgan elektr velosipedlar (e-bike) haqida nimani bilish kerak?
explanationTarget: Elektr velosipedlar oddiy velosipedlar kabi jim harakatlanadi va shu sabab ko‘pincha kech seziladi. Biroq ular odatda motorsiz velosipedlarga qaraganda ancha yuqori tezlikka erishadi. Buni ko‘plab harakat ishtirokchilari kam baholaydi, natijada xavfli vaziyatlar kutilmaganda yuzaga kelishi mumkin. Umuman olganda, velosipedchilarning tezlashishi va tezkor manevrlar qilishini kuting.
answerATarget: - aldab qo‘yadigan darajada tez bo‘lishi mumkin

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You should be ready to brake and watch the cyclist carefully, because the cyclist can suddenly ride onto the road.
answerASource: I get ready to brake
answerBSource: I watch [the cyclist] closely
answerCSource: I manoeuvre over to the left side of the road
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday bo‘ladi?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchi to‘satdan yo‘lga chiqib ketishi mumkinligi sabab tormozga tayyor turing va uni diqqat bilan kuzating.

questionTextSource: You wish to overtake a farm tractor with a mounted implement extending beyond its back. What must you remember?
explanationSource: You should always refrain from an overtaking maneuver when the tractor wants to turn. The reason for this is that the tractor has a large turning circle and is likely to veer, blocking the road. Also keep in mind that the turn signals of such a vehicle are not always visible and that they could be covered by dirt due to the agricultural use.
answerASource: The mounted implement can swing out when the tractor turns off
answerBSource: The rear lights may be heavily soiled or concealed, making direction signals difficult or impossible to see when they are on
answerCSource: Farm tractors can always be overtaken safely due to their low speed
questionTextTarget: Siz orqasiga chiqib turgan jihoz o‘rnatilgan qishloq xo‘jaligi traktorini quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Traktor burilmoqchi bo‘lsa, har doim quvib o‘tishdan voz kechishingiz kerak. Sababi shundaki, traktorning burilish radiusi katta va u yo‘lni to‘sib qo‘yadigan darajada chetga chiqishi mumkin. Shuningdek, bunday transport vositalarining burilish chiroqlari doim ham ko‘rinmasligi va qishloq xo‘jaligi ishlaridan ifloslanib qolgan bo‘lishi mumkinligini ham unutmang.
answerATarget: Traktor burilganda orqa jihoz chetga chiqib ketishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Orqa chiroqlar juda ifloslangan yoki yopilgan bo‘lishi mumkin, shu sabab yo‘nalish ko‘rsatkichlarini ko‘rish qiyin yoki imkonsiz bo‘ladi
answerCTarget: Qishloq xo‘jaligi traktorlari past tezlikda yurgani uchun ularni har doim xavfsiz quvib o‘tish mumkin

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate on the other side of this crest in the road?
explanationSource: Due to the height of the crest, it is not possible to see what is behind it. Expect to encounter obstacles there, such as broken-down or slow-moving motor vehicles. Also be prepared for oncoming trucks sharing your lane. To be able to react to such dangers in time, you should reduce your speed accordingly.
answerASource: A vehicle may be stationary there
answerBSource: There may be an oncoming vehicle on my side of the road
answerCSource: There may be a slow moving vehicle on my side of the road
questionTextTarget: Yo‘lning bu tepasi ortida nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Tepa balandligi sabab uning ortida nima borligini ko‘rishning iloji yo‘q. U yerda buzilib qolgan yoki sekin harakatlanayotgan avtomobillar kabi to‘siqlar bo‘lishini kuting. Shuningdek, sizning yo‘lagingizdan foydalanayotgan qarshidan kelayotgan yuk mashinalariga ham tayyor bo‘ling. Bunday xavflarga vaqtida javob bera olish uchun tezlikni mos ravishda kamaytirishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: U yerda transport vositasi to‘xtab turgan bo‘lishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Qarama-qarshi transport mening yo‘lagimda bo‘lishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Mening yo‘lagimda sekin harakatlanayotgan transport vositasi bo‘lishi mumkin

questionTextSource: What do you do?
explanationSource: When a truck overtakes a cyclist crossing the continuous line, you must never try to push the truck back onto its lane. Brake instead and go far enough to the right.
answerASource: Brake and move to the right
answerBSource: Proceed without taking action so that the truck is forced to pull back over to the right
questionTextTarget: Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi uzluksiz chiziqni bosib velosipedchini quvib o‘tayotgan bo‘lsa, siz hech qachon uni o‘z yo‘lagiga qaytarishga urinmasligingiz kerak. Buning o‘rniga tormoz bering va yetarlicha o‘ngga o‘ting.
answerBTarget: Hech nima qilmay davom etaman, shunda yuk mashinasi majburan yana o‘ngga qaytadi

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for in this situation?
explanationSource: In the area of ​​a construction site you have to drive carefully and reduce the speed. For one, the road can be dirty. But it there can also be workers, construction equipment or construction vehicles on the road and obstruct the traffic.
answerASource: An uneven and dirty roadway
answerBSource: Workers and construction equipment on the roadway
answerCSource: Police regulating traffic
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimaga tayyor bo‘lishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qurilish maydoni hududida ehtiyotkorlik bilan haydashingiz va tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak. Birinchidan, yo‘l iflos bo‘lishi mumkin. Bundan tashqari, yo‘lda ishchilar, qurilish uskunalari yoki qurilish transportlari bo‘lishi va harakatga xalaqit qilishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for in this situation?
explanationSource: Due to the weather and the slush you have to expect a slippery road surface and a longer braking distance. The bus has priority over you and shows you by indicating that he wants to start driving. Carefully reduce the speed to allow it to veer. Under no circumstances may you overtake him.
answerASource: - braking distance will be longer
answerBSource: - bus moves off immediately
answerCSource: - bus driver allows you to pass
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimaga tayyor bo‘lishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ob-havo va qor-shira sabab yo‘l sirpanchiq bo‘lishi va tormoz yo‘li uzayishini kutishingiz kerak. Avtobus sizga nisbatan ustunlikka ega va burilish ko‘rsatkichini yoqib, yurishni boshlamoqchi ekanini ko‘rsatmoqda. U chiqib olishi uchun ehtiyotkorlik bilan tezlikni kamaytiring. Hech qanday holatda uni quvib o‘tmang.

questionTextSource: You are waiting at a traffic light and want to turn right. What should you remember?
explanationSource: If other road users are next to you, you must give priority to them if you wish to drive straight ahead. So, also pay attention to people on bike and pedestrian paths. It is not enough to just keep track of the road through the rear-view mirror. In addition, you have to control your surroundings with a look back over your shoulder to be able to perceive those road users who are hidden in the blind spot of the mirror.
answerASource: - make sure there is no road user beside you on the right intending to go straight on
answerBSource: - simply glance in the mirror to check that there is no cyclist behind you
answerCSource: - concentrate fully on the turning manoeuvre because any cyclist has to wait anyway
questionTextTarget: Siz svetoforda kutyapsiz va o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar yoningizda boshqa harakat ishtirokchilari bo‘lsa, siz to‘g‘riga yurmoqchi bo‘lsangiz ham ularga yo‘l berishingiz kerak. Demak, veloyo‘lak va piyodalar yo‘lagidagi odamlarni ham kuzating. Yo‘lni faqat orqa ko‘zgudan kuzatish yetarli emas. Bundan tashqari, ko‘zguning ko‘r nuqtasida yashirinib qolgan yo‘l ishtirokchilarini sezish uchun orqaga yelka oshirib qarab atrofni nazorat qilishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: - o‘ng tomoningizda to‘g‘riga ketmoqchi bo‘lgan harakat ishtirokchisi yo‘qligiga ishonch hosil qiling
answerBTarget: - orqangizda velosipedchi yo‘qligini tekshirish uchun ko‘zguga qisqacha qarashning o‘zi kifoya
answerCTarget: - butun diqqatni burilish manevriga qarating, chunki velosipedchi baribir kutishi kerak

questionTextSource: You are driving on a new road through a woodland area. What must you reckon with?
explanationSource: When roads are newly constructed in forested areas, they often pass through the natural habitat of various wild animals. Therefore, it is possible that their habitual paths cross your roadway. For this reason, you must expect wildlife crossings and obstacles due to frequent wildlife accidents on forest roads.
answerASource: Wild animals unexpectedly crossing the roadway
answerBSource: An obstruction caused by an accident involving a wild animal
answerCSource: There is no reason to expect accidents involving wild animals on new roads
questionTextTarget: Siz o‘rmon hududi orqali o‘tuvchi yangi yo‘lda harakatlanyapsiz. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘llar o‘rmon hududlarida yangi qurilganda, ular ko‘pincha turli yovvoyi hayvonlarning tabiiy yashash hududidan o‘tadi. Shu sabab ularning odatiy yo‘llari sizning qatnov qismingizni kesib o‘tishi mumkin. Shuning uchun yovvoyi hayvonlarning yo‘lni kesib o‘tishini va o‘rmon yo‘llarida tez-tez uchraydigan yovvoyi hayvonlar bilan bog‘liq avariyalar sabab to‘siqlarni kutishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: Yovvoyi hayvonlarning kutilmaganda yo‘lni kesib o‘tishini
answerBTarget: Yovvoyi hayvon bilan bog‘liq avariya sabab yuzaga kelgan to‘siqni
answerCTarget: Yangi yo‘llarda yovvoyi hayvonlar bilan bog‘liq avariyalarni kutish uchun sabab yo‘q

questionTextSource: Several deer run across the road in front of your vehicle. What must you reckon with?
explanationSource: If several deer flee across the road in front of your vehicle, you have to expect the following: several animals could follow. The entire herd could also turn in the opposite direction and switch to the other side of the roadway again.
answerASource: There could be stragglers following the herd
answerBSource: The herd may come back and cross the road again
answerCSource: There is no reason to expect any more animals
questionTextTarget: Bir nechta kiyiklar avtomobilingiz oldidan yo‘lni kesib o‘tdi. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar bir nechta kiyiklar avtomobilingiz oldidan yo‘lni kesib o‘tsa, quyidagilarni kutishingiz kerak: podadan ortda qolgan boshqa hayvonlar ham ortidan kelishi mumkin. Butun poda ham ortga qaytib, yana yo‘lning boshqa tomoniga o‘tishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Podadan ortda qolgan hayvonlar ham kelishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Poda qaytib kelib, yo‘lni yana kesib o‘tishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Yana hayvonlar chiqishini kutish uchun hech qanday sabab yo‘q

questionTextSource: At which time of the day do you have to reckon with increased wild animals crossing?
explanationSource: Expect more deer crossings during the morning and evening twilight. This transitional period between day and night is characterized by diffuse light conditions.
answerASource: At dusk
answerBSource: At dawn
answerCSource: At noon
questionTextTarget: Qaysi vaqtda yovvoyi hayvonlar ko‘proq yo‘lni kesib o‘tishini kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Ertalabki va kechki g‘ira-shirada kiyiklar ko‘proq yo‘lni kesib o‘tishini kuting. Bu kun bilan tun oralig‘idagi diffuz yorug‘lik davridir.

questionTextSource: Which dangers must you reckon with above all in rural areas?
explanationSource: Where farming is practised, tractors and other agricultural vehicles travel at slow speeds and/or over width. Often the roadway is also dirty with soil or manure.
answerASource: Soiled roadways
answerBSource: Slow farm vehicles
answerCSource: Excessively wide farm vehicles
questionTextTarget: Qishloq hududlarida, eng avvalo, qanday xavflarni kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Dehqonchilik qilinadigan joylarda traktorlar va boshqa qishloq xo‘jaligi transportlari sekin yuradi va/yoki juda keng bo‘ladi. Ko‘pincha yo‘l ham tuproq yoki go‘ng bilan ifloslangan bo‘ladi.
answerCTarget: Haddan tashqari keng qishloq xo‘jaligi transportlarini

questionTextSource: A farm tractor is approaching you at dawn with its headlights switched on. What must you reckon with?
explanationSource: In agricultural vehicles, the lights are sometimes broken. They can also be covered up by dirt or tarpaulins. The width of the vehicles is not easy to assess. In addition, these vehicles can have up to two trailers.
answerASource: The tractor can carry large work equipment which is difficult to see
answerBSource: Despite the headlights being switched on, it is not always possible to discern the width of the tractor
answerCSource: 2 wide trailers can be attached to the tractor
questionTextTarget: Tongda faralari yoqilgan qishloq xo‘jaligi traktori sizga qarab kelmoqda. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qishloq xo‘jaligi transportlarida chiroqlar ba’zan buzilgan bo‘ladi. Ular ifloslik yoki brezent bilan ham yopilib qolishi mumkin. Bunday transportlarning kengligini baholash oson emas. Bundan tashqari, ularga ikkitagacha tirkama ulanib kelishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Traktor ko‘rish qiyin bo‘lgan katta ish jihozlarini olib yurishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Faralari yoqilgan bo‘lsa ham, traktorning kengligini har doim ham aniqlab bo‘lmaydi
answerCTarget: Traktorga 2 ta keng tirkama ulangan bo‘lishi mumkin

questionTextSource: What must you observe when you enter a tunnel?
explanationSource: Before entering a tunnel, you must note the following: there is a light obligation in a tunnel. Therefore, turn on the dipped beam to see and be seen better. If you have sunglasses, take them off. You must not switch on the fog lamp in the tunnel.
answerASource: Drive with dipped headlights also during the day even in well lit tunnels
answerBSource: Take off your sunglasses
answerCSource: Switch fog headlamps on
questionTextTarget: Tunnelga kirishda nimaga rioya qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Tunnelga kirishdan oldin quyidagilarni yodda tuting: tunnellarda yorug‘lik yoqish majburiy. Shuning uchun yaxshi ko‘rish va ko‘rinish uchun qisqa faralarni yoqing. Agar ko‘zoynagingiz quyoshdan saqlovchi bo‘lsa, uni yeching. Tunnel ichida tuman faralarini yoqish mumkin emas.
answerATarget: Yaxshi yoritilgan tunnellarda ham kunduzi qisqa faralar bilan yuraman

questionTextSource: What must you do in case of a traffic jam in a tunnel?
explanationSource: If there is a traffic jam in a tunnel, stop and turn off the engine. By keeping a safe distance of about five metres, you reduce the risk of poisoning, preserve a manoeuvring option and allow rescue vehicles to pass. Switch on the hazard warning lights to warn following drivers. Only leave your vehicle after an appropriate loudspeaker announcement.
answerASource: Switch hazard warning lights on
answerBSource: When traffic comes to a standstill, keep a safety distance of approximately 5 m to the car ahead
answerCSource: Secure and leave vehicle immediately
questionTextTarget: Tunnelda tirbandlik bo‘lsa nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar tunnelda tirbandlik bo‘lsa, to‘xtang va dvigatelni o‘chiring. Taxminan besh metr xavfsiz masofa saqlash orqali zaharlanish xavfini kamaytirasiz, manevr qilish imkoniyatini saqlab qolasiz va qutqaruv transportlarining o‘tishiga yo‘l qoldirasiz. Orqadan kelayotganlarni ogohlantirish uchun avariya chiroqlarini yoqing. Transport vositangizni faqat tegishli ovozli e’lon qilingandan keyin tark eting.
answerBTarget: Harakat to‘xtaganda oldingi avtomobilgacha taxminan 5 m xavfsiz masofa saqlayman
answerCTarget: Transport vositasini darhol xavfsizlantirib tark etaman

questionTextSource: What can impair your attention when driving through a tunnel?
explanationSource: When passing through a tunnel, the following factors may interfere with your attention: the lack of visual stimulus and the monotony of speed, as well as the lights of oncoming traffic. In this case, an orientation on the right side of the road is helpful; avoid looking directly into the light of oncoming traffic.
answerASource: Reflections of light from oncoming traffic
answerBSource: Length of the tunnel
answerCSource: Good road markings
questionTextTarget: Tunneldan o‘tishda diqqatingizga nima xalaqit berishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Tunneldan o‘tayotganda quyidagi omillar diqqatingizga xalaqit berishi mumkin: ko‘rish taassurotlarining kamligi va tezlikning bir xilligi, shuningdek qarshidan kelayotgan transportning chiroqlari. Bunday holatda yo‘lning o‘ng tomoni bo‘ylab orientatsiya qilish foydali; qarshidan kelayotgan transportning chiroqlariga to‘g‘ridan-to‘g‘ri qaramang.

questionTextSource: What must you observe particularly when driving through a tunnel?
explanationSource: Driving noises in a tunnel are considerably louder due to the reverberation of the tunnel walls. You can also hear the noise of the ventilation system. This has an irritating effect on you as a driver. Your eyes first have to get used to the different visibility conditions. The increased light reflections from oncoming traffic make things more difficult. The emergency signage is also distracting.
answerASource: You may be distracted by very loud noise
answerBSource: Signs for safety installations such as emergency exits and emergency telephones
answerCSource: You may be distracted by light effects
questionTextTarget: Tunneldan o‘tishda ayniqsa nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Tunnel devorlarining aks sadosi tufayli tunneldagi harakat shovqini ancha baland eshitiladi. Ventilyatsiya tizimi shovqinini ham eshitasiz. Bu haydovchi sifatida sizni bezovta qilishi mumkin. Ko‘zlaringiz avval turli ko‘rinish sharoitlariga moslashishi kerak bo‘ladi. Qarshidan kelayotgan transportning kuchli yorug‘lik akslari vaziyatni qiyinlashtiradi. Favqulodda holat belgilarining o‘zi ham chalg‘itadi.
answerATarget: Juda baland shovqin sizni chalg‘itishi mumkin
answerBTarget: Favqulodda chiqishlar va avariya telefonlari kabi xavfsizlik vositalari ko‘rsatilgan belgilar

questionTextSource: Which basic code of conduct must you observe in a tunnel?
explanationSource: The following rules of conduct are mandatory in all tunnels: on the entire route no U-turns are allowed. Regardless of the lighting situation, the dim light must always be switched on. The instructions of the tunnel staff must always be followed. Personal protection antecedes the protection of vehicles and their security.
answerASource: Drive with dipped headlights also by day even in a well lit tunnel
answerBSource: Do not make a U-turn
answerCSource: Follow instructions and information from the tunnel personnel
questionTextTarget: Tunnelda qaysi asosiy xulq-atvor qoidalariga rioya qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Quyidagi xulq-atvor qoidalari barcha tunnellarda majburiy: butun tunnel bo‘ylab qayrilib burilish taqiqlanadi. Yoritilishidan qat’i nazar, qisqa chiroqlar doimo yoqilgan bo‘lishi kerak. Tunnel xodimlarining ko‘rsatmalari har doim bajarilishi kerak. Shaxsiy xavfsizlik transport vositasi va uning xavfsizligini himoya qilishdan ustun turadi.
answerATarget: Yaxshi yoritilgan tunnellarda ham kunduzi qisqa faralar bilan yuraman
answerCTarget: Tunnel xodimlarining ko‘rsatma va axborotlariga amal qilaman

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop here?
explanationSource: You want to turn left. A van approaching from the opposite lane also wants to turn into this road. The vehicle has priority. Likewise, you must pay attention to pedestrians who want to cross this street.
answerASource: - the van
answerBSource: - the pedestrian
answerCSource: - the cyclist
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qarshi yo‘lakdan yaqinlashayotgan furgon ham shu yo‘lga burilmoqchi. U transport vositasi ustunlikka ega. Xuddi shuningdek, bu ko‘chani kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lgan piyodalarga ham e’tibor berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: The white reversing lights of the vehicle that turned in front of you indicate that it would probably turn back to its original lane in reverse. Since you cannot tell if you have been noticed by the driver, you should reduce your driving speed and be ready to brake. Refrain from an evasive maneuver due to oncoming traffic.
answerASource: Slow down and be ready to brake
answerBSource: Continue driving as before
answerCSource: Swerve to the left
questionTextTarget: Hozir nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Oldingizga burilib kirgan transport vositasining oq orqaga yurish chiroqlari u ehtimol orqaga yurib asl yo‘lagiga qaytmoqchi ekanini bildiradi. Haydovchi sizni payqagan-payqamagani aniq emasligi sabab siz tezlikni kamaytirishingiz va tormozga tayyor turishingiz kerak. Qarshidan kelayotgan transport sabab chetlab o‘tish manevridan voz keching.

questionTextSource: You want to turn right. What should you do?
explanationSource: Before you can turn, you have to check in the rear-view mirror if the road is clear. It is also advisable to look over your shoulder: this is the only way to be aware of dangerous situations located in the blind spot of the mirror. Keep in mind that other road users - cyclists or pedestrians - whose way you cross, always have priority.
answerASource: Allow the cyclist to pass through the junction
answerBSource: Check way is clear by glancing over your shoulder
answerCSource: Take the turn ahead of the cyclist
questionTextTarget: Siz o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Burilishdan oldin orqa ko‘zguda yo‘l bo‘shligini tekshirishingiz kerak. Shuningdek, yelka oshirib qarash ham tavsiya etiladi: faqat shu yo‘l bilan ko‘zgudagi ko‘r nuqtada joylashgan xavfli vaziyatlarni ko‘rishingiz mumkin. Unutmang, yo‘lingizni kesib o‘tadigan boshqa yo‘l harakati ishtirokchilari – velosipedchilar yoki piyodalar – har doim ustunlikka ega.
answerBTarget: Yelka oshirib qarab yo‘l bo‘shligini tekshiraman

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: The other road users can react to this situation with all the different scenarios described. All are equally dangerous. Therefore, extreme caution and slow driving is required.
answerASource: The motorbike could swerve to the left
answerBSource: The person beside the van could encroach further onto the road
answerCSource: Pedestrians could cross the road in front of the van
questionTextTarget: Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Boshqa harakat ishtirokchilari bu vaziyatga tasvirlangan barcha turli ssenariylar bo‘yicha javob berishi mumkin. Ularning barchasi birdek xavfli. Shuning uchun juda ehtiyotkorlik va sekin haydash talab etiladi.
answerBTarget: Furgon yonidagi odam yanada ko‘proq yo‘lga kirib kelishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Piyodalar furgonning oldidan yo‘lni kesib o‘tishi mumkin

questionTextSource: Ahead of the motorbike a car is seeking to reverse on the road. What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: Car drivers often underestimate the speed of approaching motorcyclists; the same happens to the driver here while he's pulling out of a parking space. The action alternatives of the motorcyclist - braking or dodging - are both dangerous for him on a snow-slicked road surface. He could fall. Also consider the longer braking distance in this weather.
answerASource: That the car continues to reverse
answerBSource: That the motorbike will suddenly brake
answerCSource: That my braking distance will be longer than normal
questionTextTarget: Mototsikl oldida avtomobil orqaga yurib yo‘lga chiqmoqchi. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Avtomobil haydovchilari ko‘pincha yaqinlashayotgan mototsiklning tezligini kam baholaydi; bu yerda ham haydovchi to‘xtash joyidan chiqayotganda shunday qilmoqda. Mototsiklchi uchun har ikki harakat varianti – tormozlash yoki chetga burish – qor-shirali yo‘l yuzasida xavfli. U yiqilib ketishi mumkin. Bunday ob-havoda tormoz yo‘li ham uzunroq bo‘lishini hisobga oling.
answerCTarget: Mening tormoz yo‘lim odatdagidan uzunroq bo‘lishini

questionTextSource: You are driving quickly along a main road and some distance ahead you see a deer close to the carriageway. What should you do?
explanationSource: If the deer is not yet on the road, you should under no circumstances perform emergency braking. This would unnecessarily endanger other road users and force them to unpredictable reactions. Nevertheless, you should reduce the speed of your vehicle and keep it ready to brake: the animal could run without warning on the road.
answerASource: - reduce speed
answerBSource: - be ready to brake
answerCSource: - perform an emergency braking manoeuvre
questionTextTarget: Siz asosiy yo‘lda tez yurib ketyapsiz va biroz oldinda qatnov qismiga yaqin kiyikni ko‘rdingiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar kiyik hali yo‘lga chiqmagan bo‘lsa, hech qanday holatda keskin tormoz bermang. Bu boshqa harakat ishtirokchilariga asossiz xavf tug‘diradi va ularni oldindan aytib bo‘lmaydigan reaksiyaga majbur qiladi. Shunga qaramay, tezlikni kamaytirishingiz va tormozga tayyor turishingiz kerak: hayvon ogohlantirmasdan yo‘lga yugurib chiqishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: Why are you particularly at risk, if you are riding a two-wheeler?
explanationSource: Especially in restrictive visibility conditions such as fog and darkness, the small silhouette of the two-wheeled driver leads to the fact that he is recognized late or not at all. In addition, the small design makes it difficult to estimate its speed. In the event of a collision, two-wheelers generally offer less protection than most other motor vehicles.
answerASource: Because I can be easily overlooked
answerBSource: Because my speed can be underestimated
answerCSource: Because I am at greater risk of injury in the event of an accident
questionTextTarget: Agar siz ikki g‘ildirakli transportda bo‘lsangiz, nega ayniqsa xavf ostida bo‘lasiz?
explanationTarget: Ayniqsa tuman va qorong‘ilik kabi ko‘rish cheklangan sharoitlarda ikki g‘ildirakli transport haydovchisining kichik silueti uni kech yoki umuman sezilmasligiga olib keladi. Bundan tashqari, kichik ko‘rinishi sabab uning tezligini baholash qiyin. To‘qnashuv sodir bo‘lganda, ikki g‘ildirakli transport odatda boshqa motorli transport vositalarining ko‘pchiligiga qaraganda kamroq himoya beradi.
answerATarget: Chunki meni osongina payqamay qolishlari mumkin
answerBTarget: Chunki mening tezligim kam baholanishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Chunki avariya yuz bersa, jarohat olish xavfim yuqoriroq

questionTextSource: Driving on a very busy road with several lanes, you have manoeuvred yourself into the lane for taking a left turn. Now instead of turning left, you want to turn right. What should you do?
explanationSource: A lane change or turning right could endanger other road users due to the heavy traffic. You must therefore turn left and take a small detour. Watch out for lane markings that prohibit you from changing lanes.
answerASource: I turn left
answerBSource: I reverse with my hazard lights turned on
answerCSource: Driving a wide curve, I turn right
questionTextTarget: Siz juda gavjum, ko‘p yo‘lakli yo‘lda chapga burilish yo‘lagiga chiqib oldingiz. Endi esa chapga emas, o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘lakni almashtirish yoki o‘ngga burilish zich harakat sabab boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin. Shu sabab siz chapga burilishingiz va kichik aylanma yo‘l qilishingiz kerak. Yo‘lak almashtirishni taqiqlovchi chiziqlarga e’tibor bering.

questionTextSource: There is a tunnel on my route. What should I know?
explanationSource: If there is a tunnel on your route, you should know the appropriate traffic signs and behavioral measures in the event of a breakdown or an accident: in this case, alert the tunnel monitoring center and follow the next escape route. First, turn off the engine of your vehicle and leave the ignition key in the lock for rescue operations.
answerASource: I should know how I park and leave my vehicle if I am unable to continue driving
answerBSource: I should know how escape routes are indicated in tunnels
answerCSource: I can be sure that help will arrive promptly in the event of emergencies
questionTextTarget: Mening yo‘nalishimda tunnel bor. Men nimani bilishim kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar yo‘lingizda tunnel bo‘lsa, tegishli yo‘l belgilarini va buzilish yoki avariya yuz bergandagi xulq-atvor choralarini bilishingiz kerak: bunday holatda tunnel nazorat markazini ogohlantiring va eng yaqin qochish yo‘li bo‘ylab harakat qiling. Avvalo, transport vositangiz dvigatelini o‘chiring va qutqaruv ishlari uchun kalitni kontakt qulfida qoldiring.
answerATarget: Agar davom eta olmasam, transport vositasini qanday to‘xtatish va tark etishni bilishim kerak
answerBTarget: Tunnellarda qochish yo‘llari qanday ko‘rsatilishini bilishim kerak
answerCTarget: Favqulodda vaziyatda yordam tez yetib kelishiga ishonchim komil bo‘lishi mumkin

questionTextSource: While driving through a tunnel, you notice a fire in your vehicle. You are not able to exit the tunnel. What should you do?
explanationSource: If a fire in your vehicle prevents you from leaving a tunnel: Park the vehicle unlocked with the engine switched off and the ignition key remaining in the lock. Alarm the tunnel monitoring center. Take the next escape route on foot.
answerASource: - turn on the hazard warning system
answerBSource: - switch off the engine
answerCSource: - leave the key in the ignition
questionTextTarget: Tunnelda ketayotib avtomobilingizda yong‘in chiqqanini sezsangiz va tunneldan chiqib keta olmasangiz, nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar avtomobildagi yong‘in tunneldan chiqib ketishga yo‘l qo‘ymasa: transport vositasini qulflamasdan to‘xtating, dvigatelni o‘chiring va kalitni kontaktda qoldiring. Tunnel nazorat markazini ogohlantiring. Piyoda eng yaqin qochish yo‘li bo‘ylab chiqing.

questionTextSource: While driving in a tunnel you were forced to bring your vehicle to a halt due to a fire. What should you do now?
explanationSource: If you have to park your vehicle in a tunnel due to a fire, you should: stop the engine, leave the key in the ignition switch and quickly trigger the fire alarm at the emergency call station. Leave the tunnel as fast as possible on the next escape route.
answerASource: I trigger the fire alarm at the emergency call point
answerBSource: I extinguish the fire as best I can
answerCSource: I lock my vehicle
questionTextTarget: Tunnelda ketayotib yong‘in sabab transport vositangizni to‘xtatishga majbur bo‘ldingiz. Endi nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar tunnelda yong‘in sabab transport vositangizni to‘xtatishga majbur bo‘lsangiz, quyidagilarni qilishingiz kerak: dvigatelni o‘chiring, kalitni kontakt qulfida qoldiring va avariya chaqiruv punktida yong‘in signalini tezda faollashtiring. Eng yaqin qochish yo‘li orqali tunnelni imkon qadar tez tark eting.
answerATarget: Favqulodda chaqiruv punktida yong‘in signalini ishga tushiraman

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate?
explanationSource: You see that the truck wants to turn in front of you. The vehicle obscures the view of the traffic, so you need to adjust your speed. The truck probably swings out to take the curve. He might stop abruptly to let pedestrians or other road users pass.
answerASource: - will swing outwards even more
answerBSource: - will come to a sudden stop
answerCSource: - will remain stationary in the right-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz yuk mashinasi oldingizda burilmoqchi ekanini ko‘ryapsiz. Transport vositasi harakatni to‘sib ko‘rsatmayapti, shuning uchun tezligingizni moslashtirishingiz kerak. Yuk mashinasi burilish olish uchun keng chiqishi mumkin. U piyodalar yoki boshqa harakat ishtirokchilarini o‘tkazish uchun to‘satdan to‘xtab qolishi ham mumkin.

questionTextSource: What must you watch out for now?
explanationSource: You want to turn right, so does the car driver in front of you. The pedestrian traffic light of the cross street into which you want to turn shows green, and the pedestrian has priority. It is possible that your car in front will pull up and stop soon after to let the pedestrian pass. On the right you can also see a cyclist. The cyclist could drive past your vehicle on the right and circle your turning vehicle on the left. Avoid this danger.
answerASource: For the vehicle on the road ahead
answerBSource: For the bicycle
answerCSource: For the motorbike
questionTextTarget: Hozir nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz ham, oldingizdagi avtomobil haydovchisi ham o‘ngga burilmoqchisiz. Siz burilmoqchi bo‘lgan ko‘ndalang ko‘chaning piyodalar svetofori yashil, demak piyoda ustunlikka ega. Oldingizdagi avtomobil yaqin orada to‘xtab, piyodani o‘tkazib yuborishi mumkin. O‘ng tomonda velosipedchini ham ko‘rib turibsiz. Velosipedchi avtomobilingizning o‘ng tomonidan o‘tib, sizning burilayotgan transport vositangizni chap tomondan aylanib o‘tishi mumkin. Bu xavfning oldini oling.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop here?
explanationSource: The cyclist must be given priority because she is on the right-of-way. Wait until the cyclist has passed the vehicles and driven through.
answerASource: Because of the cyclist
answerBSource: Because of the green car
answerCSource: Because of the pedestrian
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchiga ustunlik yo‘lida ketayotgani uchun yo‘l berilishi kerak. Velosipedchi transport vositalaridan o‘tib, kesishmadan o‘tib ketguncha kuting.

questionTextSource: Why are you required to stop here?
explanationSource: The traffic on the main road is jammed. The red car drives around the parked vehicles to turn left. You have to wait and let this car pass as it has priority.
answerASource: Because of the red car
answerBSource: Because of the cyclist
answerCSource: Because of the pedestrian
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda to‘xtashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Asosiy yo‘ldagi harakat tirband. Qizil avtomobil chapga burilish uchun to‘xtab turgan transport vositalarini chetlab o‘tmoqda. U ustunlikka ega bo‘lgani uchun siz kutib, uni o‘tkazishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: When an animal walks on the road from the right, an evasive maneuver is a natural reflex. However, this can have far more fatal consequences than keeping the track. An evasive maneuver to the right may lead into the roadside ditch, one to the left may lead to a collision with oncoming traffic. It's safest to continue straight ahead.
answerASource: I keep a tight hold of the steering wheel and keep it pointed straight ahead
answerBSource: I swerve to the left
answerCSource: I swerve to the right
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday bo‘ladi?
explanationTarget: Hayvon o‘ng tomondan yo‘lga chiqib ketganda, chetlab o‘tish manevri tabiiy refleks bo‘lishi mumkin. Ammo bu yo‘nalishni saqlab qolishdan ko‘ra ancha og‘ir oqibatlarga olib kelishi mumkin. O‘ngga chetga burish yo‘l chetidagi ariqka olib kirishi, chapga burish esa qarshidan kelayotgan transport bilan to‘qnashuvga olib kelishi mumkin. Eng xavfsizi — to‘g‘ri yo‘nalishni saqlab qolish.
answerATarget: Rulni mahkam ushlab, uni to‘g‘ri yo‘nalishda ushlab turaman

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: You can see that the animal is about to run on the road. Horns may help stop it. In addition, you should initiate emergency braking to reduce a potential collision. Do not dodge! This rather leads to accidents with personal injury than a collision with an animal.
answerASource: I perform an emergency braking manoeuvre
answerBSource: I sound my horn
answerCSource: I swerve to the left
questionTextTarget: Hozir nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Hayvonning yo‘lga yugurib chiqmoqchi ekanini ko‘ryapsiz. Signal berish uni to‘xtatishga yordam berishi mumkin. Bundan tashqari, ehtimoliy to‘qnashuvni kamaytirish uchun keskin tormozlashni boshlashingiz kerak. Chetga burilmang! Bu ko‘proq hayvon bilan to‘qnashuvdan ko‘ra odamlar jarohatlanadigan avariyalarga olib keladi.

questionTextSource: What must you look out for when the traffic lights turn green?
explanationSource: While you wait at the red light, a motorcyclist pushes past the waiting vehicles. When the traffic light switches to green you should pay attention to the motorcyclist and let him pass before he initiates a risky overtaking maneuver. But you should also let the pedestrian cross the road, which is still on the road despite it being green.
answerASource: For [the pedestrian]
answerBSource: For the motorbike
answerCSource: For oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Svetofor yashilga o‘tganda nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qizil chiroqda kutayotganingizda mototsiklchi kutib turgan transport vositalari orasidan oldinga o‘tib oldi. Svetofor yashilga o‘tganda siz mototsiklchiga e’tibor berishingiz va u xavfli quvib o‘tish manevrini boshlashidan oldin uni o‘tkazib yuborishingiz kerak. Shuningdek, hali ham yo‘lda turgan piyodaga ham yo‘l berishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for in this situation?
