explanationTarget: Yo‘l belgisi sabab bu yerda kutishingiz kerak. Sizning yo‘nalishingizdagi qizil strelka qarshidan kelayotgan transportga ustunlik berilishini bildiradi. Hech qanday holatda piyodalar yo‘lagiga chiqishingiz mumkin emas. U faqat piyodalar uchun mo‘ljallangan.
answerATarget: Tezlikni kamaytiraman va zarur bo‘lsa to‘xtayman
answerCTarget: Tezlashib davom etaman, chunki qarshidan kelayotgan transport kutishi kerak

questionTextSource: During traffic congestion, what parts of the road infrastructure must be kept clear?
explanationSource: The pedestrian crossing (“zebra crossing”) is intended to enable pedestrians to cross the road safely and without danger. For this reason, you must leave pedestrian crossings free under all circumstances. If you drive into a junction (or crossing) in slow-moving traffic, there is a risk that you will block oncoming cross traffic or that oncoming traffic turning left will have to wait unnecessarily long. The junction or crossing would thus become even more clogged and traffic would be obstructed more than necessary.
answerASource: Traffic confluences
answerBSource: Pedestrian crossings
answerCSource: Bus stops
questionTextTarget: Tirbandlik paytida yo‘l infratuzilmasining qaysi qismlari bo‘sh qoldirilishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joyi (“zebra”) piyodalarga yo‘lni xavfsiz va xavfsiz tarzda kesib o‘tishga imkon berish uchun mo‘ljallangan. Shu sabab piyodalar o‘tish joylarini har qanday holatda bo‘sh qoldirishingiz kerak. Agar sekin harakatlanayotgan transportda kesishmaga kirsangiz, qarama-qarshi kesishuvchi transportni to‘sib qo‘yish yoki chapga burilayotgan qarama-qarshi transportni keraksiz uzoq kutishga majbur qilish xavfi bor. Kesishma yanada tiqilib qoladi va harakatga xalaqit beriladi.

questionTextSource: You want to continue driving straight ahead. What is correct in this situation?
explanationSource:
A group of pedestrians, cyclists or motor vehicles, which is traveling together in road traffic, is called a closed association. Closed associations must be clearly marked and must not be interrupted.
So you have to wait until all vehicles drove past you and as long as you have to wait at the stop line.
answerASource: I allow the convoy to pass through
answerBSource: I wait at the stop line
answerCSource: I cross over the junction
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchisiz. Bu vaziyatda qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l harakatida birgalikda harakatlanayotgan piyodalar, velosipedchilar yoki motorli transport vositalari guruhi yopiq kolonna deb ataladi. Yopiq kolonnalar aniq belgilanadi va ularni bo‘lib yuborish mumkin emas. Shuning uchun barcha transport vositalari yoningizdan o‘tib ketguncha kutishingiz va kutishingiz kerak bo‘lsa, to‘xtash chizig‘ida qolishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You want to continue driving straight ahead. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: The car ahead blocks you in your lane. Nevertheless, you should not overtake on the right before the intersection, as this would be too dangerous. Keep your distance, reduce the speed and stop if necessary. Continue straight ahead when the free lane is free and the traffic light turns green. You can only change on the right lane if there is a sufficiently large traffic gap.
answerASource: I remain waiting in my lane
answerBSource: I move to the right if there is sufficient space in the traffic
answerCSource: I overtake the vehicle alongside me and switch lanes
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurishda davom etmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Oldingizdagi avtomobil sizning yo‘lagizni to‘sib turibdi. Shunga qaramay, chorrahadan oldin o‘ng tomondan quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak, chunki bu juda xavfli bo‘ladi. Masofa saqlang, tezlikni kamaytiring va zarur bo‘lsa to‘xtang. Bo‘sh yo‘lak bo‘sh bo‘lganda va svetofor yashilga o‘tganda to‘g‘ri yurishda davom eting. O‘ng yo‘lakka faqat harakatda yetarlicha katta bo‘shliq bo‘lsa o‘tishingiz mumkin.
answerBTarget: Harakatda yetarli joy bo‘lsa, o‘ngga o‘taman
answerCTarget: Yonimdagi transport vositasini quvib o‘tib, yo‘lakni almashtiraman

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: If tram rails lead through the left turn lane and you want to turn left when the tram approaches, it is important to wait with sufficient distance in front of the tram rails. When the tram passed, you can turn left.
answerASource: Stop before the left turn and keep the central divide clear
answerBSource: Turn smoothly, so as not to impede following traffic
answerCSource: Come to a stop in the central divide, where the other car is stopped
questionTextTarget: Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Agar tramvay relslari chapga burilish yo‘lagi orqali o‘tsa va tramvay yaqinlashayotganda chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, tramvay relslari oldida yetarli masofada kutish muhim. Tramvay o‘tib ketgach, chapga burilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Chap burilishdan oldin to‘xtab, markaziy ajratmani bo‘sh qoldiraman
answerBTarget: Orqadagi transportga xalaqit bermaslik uchun silliq burilaman
answerCTarget: Boshqa avtomobil to‘xtagan markaziy ajratmada to‘xtayman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: If a cyclist who moves to the left drives in front of you, you must not overtake him if the road is narrowed at this point and there is oncoming traffic. A safety distance of 1.5 to 2 meters could not be maintained here.
answerASource: Slow down
answerBSource: Accelerate
answerCSource: Swerve to the left
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Agar chapga siljiyotgan velosipedchi oldingizda harakatlansa, bu joyda yo‘l toraygan va qarama-qarshi transport bo‘lsa, uni quvib o‘tmasligingiz kerak. Bu yerda 1,5–2 metr xavfsiz masofani saqlab bo‘lmaydi.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: On your side the road narrows. The cyclist will therefore move to the left. Because of oncoming traffic, you cannot overtake him with the required safety distance of 1.5 to 2 meters. That's why you have to wait and stay behind him.
answerASource: Slow down
answerBSource: Accelerate
answerCSource: Swerve to the left
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Siz tomonda yo‘l torayadi. Shu sabab velosipedchi chapga siljiydi. Qarama-qarshi transport sabab uni talab qilingan 1,5–2 metr xavfsiz masofa bilan quvib o‘ta olmaysiz. Shuning uchun kutishingiz va uning ortida qolishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: In which situations are you not allowed to enter a junction even though the traffic light is green?
explanationSource: In the following cases, you must not enter a crossroad, even though the traffic light shows "green": with slow-moving traffic, as crossings, for example, must remain free for emergency vehicles. Secondly, when police officers regulate traffic and give instructions that deviate from the meanings of the colors of the traffic lights. In general, a green traffic light tells you that you are allowed to drive and overrules other traffic signs that regulate the right of way. Policeman orders have the highest rank and override traffic lights.
answerASource: If I would have to stop in the middle of the junction because the traffic is backing up
answerBSource: If asked to stop by a police officer
answerCSource: If the sign "Stop. Give way" is displayed at the junction
questionTextTarget: Svetofor yashil bo‘lsa ham, qaysi vaziyatlarda chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Quyidagi hollarda svetofor “yashil” bo‘lsa ham chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas: sekin harakatlanayotgan tirbandlikda, chunki chorrahalar, masalan, favqulodda xizmat transportlari uchun bo‘sh qolishi kerak. Ikkinchidan, politsiya xodimi harakatni tartibga solib, svetofor ranglari ma’nosidan farq qiluvchi ko‘rsatma berganda. Umuman olganda, yashil svetofor sizga yurishga ruxsat beradi va ustunlikni tartibga soluvchi boshqa yo‘l belgilaridan ustun turadi. Politsiya ko‘rsatmalari esa eng yuqori ustunlikka ega.
answerATarget: Agar mening yo‘nalishimdagi tirbandlik sabab chorrahaning o‘rtasida to‘xtab qolishim kerak bo‘lsa
answerCTarget: Agar chorrahada “To‘xtang. Yo‘l bering” belgisi bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: In what instances may you not enter a junction, even though you have right of way?
explanationSource: If the traffic slows down, you may enter a crossroad with a green traffic light or right of way only if you can leave it quickly without blocking it. This is particularly important in order to keep the intersection free for police, fire and ambulance emergency vehicles. If you hear a siren, you have to drive carefully to the crossroad and may only continue if you hinder no emergency vehicle. When turning left, you must slowly enter the intersection and wait there until you can drive freely without oncoming traffic.
answerASource: - would have to wait in the junction because the traffic in my direction is backing up
answerBSource: - would impede a vehicle with flashing blue lights and siren
answerCSource: - want to turn left and would have to wait in the junction because of oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Ustunlikka ega bo‘lsangiz ham, qaysi hollarda chorrahaga kirishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Agar harakat sekinlashsa, yashil svetofor yoki ustunlik bo‘lsa ham chorrahaga faqat uni tezda tark eta olsangiz va to‘sib qo‘ymasangiz kirishingiz mumkin. Bu chorrahani politsiya, yong‘in xizmati va tez yordam transportlari uchun bo‘sh saqlashda ayniqsa muhim. Agar sirena eshitsangiz, chorrahaga ehtiyotkorlik bilan yaqinlashishingiz va hech qanday favqulodda transportga xalaqit bermasangizgina davom etishingiz mumkin. Chapga burilganda chorrahaga sekin kirib, qarama-qarshi transport bo‘lmaganda erkin harakatlana olguncha u yerda kutishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: - mening yo‘nalishimdagi tirbandlik sabab chorrahada kutishimga to‘g‘ri kelsa
answerBTarget: - ko‘k chiroq va sirenali transport vositasiga xalaqit bersam
answerCTarget: - chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsam va qarama-qarshi transport sabab chorrahada kutishimga to‘g‘ri kelsa

questionTextSource: Why must you slow down here and be ready to brake?
explanationSource: You don't have to slow down because of pedestrians. He is far enough away from the constriction. The red vehicle is standing and is therefore no reason to slow down as well. However, reduce the speed because of the cyclist. He could change lanes without a hand signal and bring about a dangerous situation.
answerASource: - cyclist
answerBSource: - [green] vehicle
answerCSource: - pedestrian
questionTextTarget: Nega bu yerda tezlikni kamaytirib, tormozga tayyor turishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar sabab sekinlashishingiz shart emas. U toraygan joydan yetarlicha uzoqda. Qizil transport vositasi to‘xtab turibdi, shuning uchun u ham sekinlashish uchun sabab emas. Biroq velosipedchi sabab tezlikni kamaytiring. U qo‘l ishorasisiz yo‘lakni almashtirib, xavfli vaziyat yaratishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: Why do you have to continue to brake?
explanationSource: A garbage truck comes towards the blue truck and forces him to stop. Since the garbage truck could swing out and get on your lane as it turns, you'll need to wait for the vehicle to be properly merged. The cyclist is no reason for braking.
answerASource: - refuse truck
answerBSource: - blue truck
answerCSource: - cyclist
questionTextTarget: Nega tormozlashda davom etishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Axlat tashuvchi mashina ko‘k yuk mashinasi tomon kelmoqda va uni to‘xtashga majbur qilmoqda. Axlat tashuvchi mashina burilayotganda keng chiqib, sizning yo‘lagizga kirishi mumkinligi sabab, transport vositasi to‘g‘ri qo‘shilib olguncha kutishingiz kerak. Velosipedchi tormozlash uchun sabab emas.

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. What should you do?
explanationSource: In case of halting traffic, you are not allowed to block any intersection or T-junction despite the green traffic light or right of way. You may need to wait at the stop line and cannot close up until the front vehicles are far enough away.
answerASource: - at the stop line
answerBSource: - between the yellow and the blue vehicle
answerCSource: - directly behind the yellow vehicle
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Harakat to‘xtab qolganida, svetofor yashil bo‘lsa ham yoki siz ustunlikka ega bo‘lsangiz ham, hech bir chorraha yoki T-kesishmani to‘sib qo‘yishingiz mumkin emas. To‘xtash chizig‘ida kutishingiz kerak bo‘lishi mumkin va oldingi transport vositalari yetarlicha uzoqlashmaguncha ularga yaqinlashib ololmaysiz.
answerBTarget: - sariq va ko‘k transport vositalari orasida

questionTextSource: When may you begin driving?
explanationSource: The truck was signaled by flasher that he waives his right of way. Therefore, is after the truck driver has clearly waived with hands on his priority right and if you do not endangering the cross traffic or not substantially behinderst.
answerASource: Once the truck driver has made a hand signal clearly yielding his right of way
answerBSource: If I do not endanger or significantly impede the cross traffic
answerCSource: Immediately because the truck driver is urging me to do so
questionTextTarget: Qachon harakatni boshlashingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi haydovchisi chiroq ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechayotganini bildirgan. Shuning uchun yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan aniq voz kechgandan keyin va siz kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasangiz yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasangiz, harakatni boshlashingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan aniq voz kechgandan keyin
answerBTarget: Agar men kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasam yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasam
answerCTarget: Darhol, chunki yuk mashinasi haydovchisi meni shunga undamoqda

questionTextSource: When may you take the turn?
explanationSource: As you want to turn left, oncoming traffic has priority. Only when the lorry driver gives up his right of way with a clear hand signal can you pass with due regard for oncoming traffic. Flashing your lights is not sufficient here.
answerASource: Only once the truck driver has used a hand signal to clearly indicate he is foregoing his right of way
answerBSource: Only if I do not endanger or significantly impede the cross traffic
answerCSource: Immediately, because the truck driver is prompting me to do that
questionTextTarget: Qachon burilishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lganingiz uchun qarama-qarshi transport ustunlikka ega. Faqat yuk mashinasi haydovchisi aniq qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechsa, qarama-qarshi transportga e’tibor bergan holda o‘tishingiz mumkin. Bu yerda chiroq bilan ishora qilishning o‘zi yetarli emas.
answerATarget: Faqat yuk mashinasi haydovchisi qo‘l ishorasi bilan ustunlik huquqidan voz kechayotganini aniq ko‘rsatgandan keyin
answerBTarget: Faqat men kesishuvchi transportga xavf tug‘dirmasam yoki sezilarli xalaqit bermasam
answerCTarget: Darhol, chunki yuk mashinasi haydovchisi meni shunga undamoqda

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. What should you do if the [red] truck immediately drives off on “green”?
explanationSource: You should wait here and let the truck move off, giving warning signs if necessary as it appears to be turning right. (Turn signal right)
answerASource: I wait and allow the truck to move off
answerBSource: I give a warning signal if necessary
answerCSource: I also drive off immediately
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Agar [qizil] yuk mashinasi “yashil” chiroqda darhol yurib ketsa, nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bu yerda kutishingiz va yuk mashinasining harakatlanishiga imkon berishingiz kerak; zarur bo‘lsa ogohlantiruvchi signal bering, chunki u o‘ngga burilayotgandek ko‘rinadi (o‘ngga burilish signali).
answerATarget: Kutaman va yuk mashinasining harakatlanishiga imkon beraman

questionTextSource: You want to drive straight ahead. Where should you position yourself in this situation?
explanationSource: The truck swerves to turn right, wait until the turn is completed. Then you can drive straight ahead.
answerASource: On the right behind the truck
answerBSource: On the left behind the truck
answerCSource: Directly behind the motorbike
questionTextTarget: Siz to‘g‘ri yurmoqchisiz. Bu vaziyatda qayerda joylashishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinasi o‘ngga burilish uchun keng chiqmoqda, burilish yakunlanguncha kuting. Shundan keyin to‘g‘ri yurishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource:
A "stop" is when a vehicle stops for less than 3 minutes for reasons unrelated to traffic without the driver getting out of the vehicle. If the driver stops for a longer time and/or leaves his vehicle, this is called parking. However, drivers are not allowed to stop everywhere so as not to endanger other road users.
Exceptions are:
- Non-stop zones
- Transitions through levels as there is a risk of collision with the train
- Narrow and incomprehensible places with a clearance width of less than 3 m
blind turns
- Hilltops
- Pedestrian crossings and the area in front of them (distance not less than 5 m), but not the area behind them.
answerASource: On level crossings
answerBSource: On narrow sections of the road and at blind spots
answerCSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: “To‘xtash” — haydovchi transport vositasidan tushmasdan, transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan 3 daqiqadan kam vaqtga to‘xtashidir. Agar haydovchi uzoqroq to‘xtasa va/yoki transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka deyiladi. Biroq haydovchilarga boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun hamma joyda to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi. Bunga quyidagilar kiradi: to‘xtash taqiqlangan hududlar, temiryo‘l kesishmalari, chunki poyezd bilan to‘qnashuv xavfi bor, o‘tish kengligi 3 metrdan kam bo‘lgan tor va noaniq joylar, ko‘rinmaydigan burilishlar, tepalik cho‘qqilari, piyodalar o‘tish joylari va ularning oldidagi hudud (kamida 5 m masofa), lekin ularning ortidagi hudud emas.
answerBTarget: Yo‘lning tor qismlarida va ko‘rinishi cheklangan joylarda

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: As soon as you stop or leave your vehicle for more than three minutes without any traffic-related reason, this is called parking. This is not allowed everywhere. You may not park in areas with stopping restrictions or parking prohibition - nor where you impede or obstruct other road users. Parking restrictions also apply to priority roads outside built-up areas. Use side strips or parking bays.
answerASource: At the edge of the roadway if this would prevent others from using designated parking areas
answerBSource: On priority roads outside built-up areas
answerCSource: On priority roads within built-up areas
questionTextTarget: Qayerda parkovka qilish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan uch daqiqadan ko‘proq to‘xtasangiz yoki transport vositasini tark etsangiz, bu parkovka hisoblanadi. Bunga hamma joyda ruxsat berilmaydi. To‘xtash cheklangan yoki parkovka taqiqlangan joylarda, shuningdek boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit beradigan yoki to‘sqinlik qiladigan joylarda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas. Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi ustun yo‘llarda ham parkovka cheklovlari amal qiladi. Yon chiziqlar yoki parkovka cho‘ntaklaridan foydalaning.
answerATarget: Agar bu belgilangan parkovka joylaridan boshqalar foydalanishiga to‘sqinlik qilsa, qatnov qismi chetida
answerBTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi ustun yo‘llarda

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource:
You may only stop where you do not endanger other road users. This is the case, for example, if there is a hard shoulder separated from the carriageway by a continuous white line. 
You are not allowed to stop
- on marked lanes that are used for getting into the right of way
- on the carriageway if there is a hard shoulder
- at bus stops or other public transport stops
- at taxi ranks, so as not to block the access of taxis
- on motorways or motorways, not even on the hard shoulder.
Watch out for two-wheelers!
answerASource: On the roadway, if there is a sufficiently wide hard shoulder on the right
answerBSource: Between lane dividers when direction arrows are marked on the roadway
answerCSource: At taxi ranks
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Faqat boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaydigan joyda to‘xtashingiz mumkin. Masalan, qatnov qismidan uzluksiz oq chiziq bilan ajratilgan yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa. Quyidagi joylarda to‘xtash mumkin emas: ustun yo‘lga kirish uchun mo‘ljallangan belgilangan yo‘laklarda, o‘ngda yetarli yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa qatnov qismida, avtobus bekatlari yoki boshqa jamoat transporti bekatlarida, taksilar kirishini to‘smaslik uchun taksi turargohlarida, avtomagistrallarda va shunga o‘xshash yo‘llarda, hatto yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida ham. Ikki g‘ildirakli transportlarga e’tibor bering!
answerATarget: O‘ngda yetarlicha keng yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa, qatnov qismida
answerBTarget: Qatnov qismida yo‘nalish strelkalari belgilangan bo‘lsa, yo‘lak ajratgichlari orasida

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource: On and up to five meters in front of a pedestrian crossing, you must not stop because you obstruct the clear view of other drivers on pedestrians. These could then be recognized too late. After a pedestrian crossing you may stop. Waiting is allowed on manhole covers, but not parking.
answerASource: On pedestrian crossings and up to 5 m before pedestrian crossings
answerBSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings
answerCSource: Over manholes and other road covers
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joyida va undan 5 metrgacha oldinda to‘xtamasligingiz kerak, chunki boshqa haydovchilarning piyodalarni aniq ko‘rishini to‘sib qo‘yasiz. Natijada piyodalar juda kech sezilishi mumkin. Piyodalar o‘tish joyidan keyin to‘xtashingiz mumkin. Lyuk qopqoqlari ustida kutishga ruxsat beriladi, lekin parkovka qilishga emas.
answerATarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joylarida va ulardan 5 m oldingacha
answerCTarget: Lyuklar va boshqa yo‘l qopqoqlari ustida

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to park at the edge of the road, where you block marked parking areas and thus hinder other road users. Parking is also prohibited in front of lowered curbs, which are for example located in front of driveways. Lowered curbs should allow wheelchair users to cross the road. Safe and therefore allowed is parking after a pedestrian crossing.
answerASource: At the edge of the roadway if this would prevent others from using designated parking areas
answerBSource: Before sunken kerbstones
answerCSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings
questionTextTarget: Qayerda parkovka qilish taqiqlanadi?
explanationTarget: Belgilangan parkovka joylarini to‘sib, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit beradigan yo‘l chetida parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas. Shuningdek, masalan kirish yo‘llari oldida joylashgan pasaytirilgan bordyurlar oldida ham parkovka taqiqlanadi. Pasaytirilgan bordyurlar nogironlar aravachasidagi odamlarga yo‘lni kesib o‘tishga imkon berishi kerak. Piyodalar o‘tish joyidan keyin parkovka qilish xavfsiz va shuning uchun ruxsat etilgan.
answerATarget: Agar bu belgilangan parkovka joylaridan boshqalar foydalanishiga to‘sqinlik qilsa, qatnov qismi chetida

questionTextSource: Where are you allowed to park on the left in the direction of travel?
explanationSource: You may only park in the direction of travel on the right-hand side of the road. This also applies if all parking spaces are on the left side. You may have to turn if allowed. The only exception are one-way streets, as they are only used in one direction of travel. If there are rails on the right, you must also park on the left, but always observe parking restrictions.
answerASource: In one-way streets
answerBSource: Where there are rails on the right-hand side
answerCSource: Where parking is prohibited on the right
questionTextTarget: Harakat yo‘nalishi bo‘yicha chap tomonda qayerda parkovka qilishga ruxsat beriladi?
explanationTarget: Odatda faqat harakat yo‘nalishi bo‘yicha yo‘lning o‘ng tomonida parkovka qilishingiz mumkin. Bu barcha parkovka joylari chap tomonda bo‘lsa ham amal qiladi. Ruxsat berilgan bo‘lsa, qayrilib olishingiz kerak bo‘lishi mumkin. Faqat bir tomonlama ko‘chalar istisno, chunki ulardan faqat bitta harakat yo‘nalishida foydalaniladi. Agar o‘ng tomonda relslar bo‘lsa, chap tomonda ham parkovka qilishingiz kerak, ammo parkovka cheklovlariga doimo rioya qiling.

questionTextSource: Who is parked?
explanationSource: If the car driver deliberately stops the vehicle and stays there for more than three minutes or leaves the stopped vehicle, this is considered parking. The stopping and waiting in front of a gate is not considered parking, no matter how long it takes, because the break is due to traffic.
answerASource: Anyone who stops for more than 3 minutes
answerBSource: Anyone who leaves his vehicle
answerCSource: Anyone who waits longer than 3 minutes at a closed level crossing barrier
questionTextTarget: Kim parkovka qilgan hisoblanadi?
explanationTarget: Agar avtomobil haydovchisi transport vositasini ataylab to‘xtatib, u yerda uch daqiqadan ko‘proq qolsa yoki to‘xtagan transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka hisoblanadi. Darvoza oldida to‘xtash va kutish qancha davom etishidan qat’i nazar parkovka hisoblanmaydi, chunki to‘xtash transport harakati sababli bo‘ladi.
answerBTarget: Transport vositasini tark etgan har kim
answerCTarget: Yopiq temiryo‘l shlagbaumi oldida 3 daqiqadan ko‘proq kutgan har kim

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are allowed to stop in the second row alongside other vehicles?
explanationSource: Even with the hazard lights on, waiting in the second row is only permitted for taxis. They count to public transport. Exceptions are tolerated if you let an old or handicapped person get off or on.
answerASource: Taxis, to let passengers board or alight, traffic conditions permitting
answerBSource: Any vehicle, provided the hazard warning lights are switched on
questionTextTarget: Qaysi transport vositalariga boshqa transport vositalari yonida ikkinchi qatorda to‘xtashga ruxsat beriladi?
explanationTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa ham, ikkinchi qatorda kutishga faqat taksilarga ruxsat beriladi. Ular jamoat transporti hisoblanadi. Keksa yoki nogiron odamni tushirish yoki chiqarish uchun istisnolar toqat qilinishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Taksilar, yo‘lovchilarni chiqarish yoki tushirish uchun, agar harakat sharoiti imkon bersa
answerBTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa, har qanday transport vositasi

questionTextSource: Who has stopped wrongly?
explanationSource: On the hard shoulder, the right vehicle is allowed to wait. However, it's not permitted to wait on the left of the solid line. The left vehicle stops illegally.
answerASource: [The dark-coloured car]
answerBSource: [The light-coloured car]
answerCSource: [Both cars]
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri to‘xtagan?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida o‘ngdagi transport vositasiga kutishga ruxsat beriladi. Biroq uzluksiz chiziqning chap tomonida kutishga ruxsat yo‘q. Chapdagi transport vositasi noqonuniy to‘xtagan.

questionTextSource: Who is wrongly parked?
explanationSource: The zigzagged line shortens or extends a waiting or parking ban. As far as there is no border marking on the road, a parking ban of up to 15 meters in front of and behind the stop sign applies. In this situation, the white car parked incorrectly because it stands on the marking. The dark car, however, parks properly.
answerASource: The [white] car
answerBSource: The [dark-coloured] car
answerCSource: Neither of the two cars
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan?
explanationTarget: Zigzag chiziq kutish yoki parkovka taqiqini qisqartiradi yoki uzaytiradi. Yo‘lda chegara belgisi bo‘lmasa, bekat belgisi oldidan va ortidan 15 metrgacha parkovka taqiqi amal qiladi. Bu vaziyatda oq avtomobil noto‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan, chunki u belgi ustida turibdi. To‘q rangli avtomobil esa to‘g‘ri parkovka qilgan.

questionTextSource: When are you not allowed to park on the right-hand lane?
explanationSource: In general, parking is prohibited if it could endanger other road users. Visual disability is also included. Therefore, parking is not allowed in all three situations.
answerASource: If there is an adequately paved hard shoulder to the right of the road lane
answerBSource: If there are rails integrated into the road lane
answerCSource: If there is a suitable parking lane to the right of the road lane
questionTextTarget: Qachon o‘ng yo‘lakda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Umuman olganda, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin bo‘lsa, parkovka taqiqlanadi. Ko‘rinishni to‘sish ham bunga kiradi. Shuning uchun uchala vaziyatda ham parkovka qilish mumkin emas.
answerATarget: Yo‘l yo‘lagining o‘ng tomonida yetarlicha mustahkamlangan yo‘l cheti yo‘lagi bo‘lsa
answerCTarget: Yo‘l yo‘lagining o‘ng tomonida mos parkovka yo‘lagi bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of here?
explanationSource: Never wait on the left of the lane mark. You would impede or even endanger subsequent vehicles. The side strip is designed for parking, waiting and slow vehicles. As a slow-moving driver you must use such a sufficiently wide and well-fastened strip for driving. In this case, you may drive over the thick solid line with your vehicle representing an official lane boundary.
answerASource: It is not permitted to stop to the left of the boundary line of the carriageway
answerBSource: Slower moving vehicles must use the hard shoulder if possible
answerCSource: Driving across the boundary line of the carriageway is prohibited at all times
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘lak belgisi chap tomonida hech qachon kutmang. Siz keyingi transport vositalariga xalaqit berishingiz yoki hatto xavf tug‘dirishingiz mumkin. Yon chiziq parkovka, kutish va sekin transport vositalari uchun mo‘ljallangan. Sekin harakatlanuvchi haydovchi sifatida yetarlicha keng va mustahkam bunday chiziqdan haydash uchun foydalanishingiz kerak. Bu holda rasmiy yo‘lak chegarasini bildiruvchi qalin uzluksiz chiziqdan transport vositangiz bilan o‘tishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Qatnov qismining chegara chizig‘ining chap tomonida to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi
answerBTarget: Sekin harakatlanuvchi transport vositalari imkon bo‘lsa yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan foydalanishi kerak
answerCTarget: Qatnov qismi chegara chizig‘idan o‘tish har doim taqiqlangan

questionTextSource: What vehicles may be parked alongside other parked cars on the roadway?
explanationSource: In exceptional cases, taxis are only allowed to stop in the second row when passengers are getting on or off.
answerASource: All taxis when passengers get in or out
answerBSource: All vehicles when they are being loaded or unloaded
answerCSource: All vehicles when they have their hazard lights switched on
questionTextTarget: Qaysi transport vositalari yo‘lda parkovka qilingan boshqa avtomobillar yonida to‘xtashi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Istisno hollarda, taksilarga faqat yo‘lovchilar chiqayotgan yoki tushayotgan paytda ikkinchi qatorda to‘xtashga ruxsat beriladi.
answerATarget: Yo‘lovchilar chiqayotganda yoki tushayotganda barcha taksilar
answerBTarget: Yuk ortilayotgan yoki tushirilayotgan barcha transport vositalari
answerCTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan barcha transport vositalari

questionTextSource: Where is it forbidden to stop?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to wait on acceleration or on deceleration lanes. Also, on lanes with directional arrows you must not stop. This does not apply to driveways in front of properties.
answerASource: On merging and demerging lanes
answerBSource: On lanes marked with direction arrows
answerCSource: In front of entrances to and exits from property lots
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Tezlanish yoki sekinlashish yo‘laklarida kutishingiz mumkin emas. Shuningdek, yo‘nalish strelkalari belgilangan yo‘laklarda ham to‘xtamasligingiz kerak. Bu mulklar oldidagi kirish yo‘llariga taalluqli emas.
answerCTarget: Mulk hududlariga kirish va chiqish joylari oldida

questionTextSource: Who has stopped incorrectly?
explanationSource: On pedestrian crossings and five meters before, waiting is prohibited. The aim of this provision is to ensure a clear view of approaching vehicles and pedestrians who are willing to cross in this traffic situation.
answerASource: The [red car]
answerBSource: The [yellow car]
answerCSource: Both cars
questionTextTarget: Kim noto‘g‘ri to‘xtagan?
explanationTarget: Piyodalar o‘tish joylarida va ulardan besh metr oldin kutish taqiqlanadi. Bu qoidaning maqsadi yaqinlashayotgan transport vositalari va o‘tmoqchi bo‘lgan piyodalarni aniq ko‘rishni ta’minlashdir.

questionTextSource: Where may you park here?
explanationSource: In one-way streets you're only allowed to drive in one direction. If you have a side strip on the left and right, you may park both left and right.
answerASource: - on the left and right-hand hard shoulders
answerBSource: - on the left-hand hard shoulder only
answerCSource: - on the right-hand hard shoulder only
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda qayerda parkovka qilishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Bir tomonlama ko‘chalarda faqat bitta yo‘nalishda harakatlanishga ruxsat beriladi. Agar chap va o‘ng tomonda yon chiziq bo‘lsa, chapda ham, o‘ngda ham parkovka qilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: - chap va o‘ng tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘laklarida
answerBTarget: - faqat chap tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida
answerCTarget: - faqat o‘ng tomondagi yo‘l cheti yo‘lagida

questionTextSource: Where is it forbidden to stop?
explanationSource: Stopping occurs when a vehicle is stopped for less than 3 minutes for non-traffic reasons while the driver does not leave the vehicle. If the driver stops longer and/or leaves his vehicle, this is referred to as parking. You are not allowed to stop everywhere, for example so as not to endanger other road users. Fire brigade access roads and no-stopping zones, among other things, are excluded; a distinction is made here between parking and stopping. This does not apply to all one-way streets.
answerASource: In front of fire service access roads
answerBSource: On any one-way road
answerCSource: In non-parking zones
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi transport vositasini tark etmasdan, transportga bog‘liq bo‘lmagan sabab bilan 3 daqiqadan kam to‘xtasa, bu to‘xtash hisoblanadi. Agar haydovchi uzoqroq to‘xtasa va/yoki transport vositasini tark etsa, bu parkovka deb ataladi. Hamma joyda to‘xtashga ruxsat berilmaydi, masalan boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun. Yong‘in xizmati kirish yo‘llari va to‘xtash taqiqlangan hududlar bundan mustasno; bu yerda parkovka va to‘xtash farqlanadi. Bu barcha bir tomonlama ko‘chalarga taalluqli emas.

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when you see a hazard warning light flash?
explanationSource: A driver must exercise special caution when perceiving a hazard light. Drivers of a car can use this for a variety of reasons. One example are regular buses that want to allow children to get in and out. Also, when a vehicle is towed, both the towed car and the towing vehicle will turn on their hazard light. A breakdown is also indicated by pressing the warning light and coming to a stop as soon as possible on the edge of the road.
answerASource: - a school bus with children alighting
answerBSource: - a car being towed
answerCSource: - a truck broken down
questionTextTarget: Avariya chirog‘i yonib-o‘chayotganini ko‘rsangiz, nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi avariya chirog‘ini ko‘rganda alohida ehtiyotkorlik ko‘rsatishi kerak. Avtomobil haydovchilari uni turli sabablarga ko‘ra yoqishi mumkin. Masalan, bolalarni tushirish yoki chiqarish uchun muntazam avtobuslar. Transport vositasi tortib ketilayotganda tortilayotgan avtomobil ham, tortayotgan transport vositasi ham avariya chiroqlarini yoqadi. Buzilish ham avariya chirog‘ini yoqish va imkon qadar tez yo‘l chetida to‘xtash orqali ko‘rsatiladi.

questionTextSource: What should you do if you see a vehicle with its hazard lights on?
explanationSource: If the hazard warning lights are switched on, there can be many reasons and causes. To be on the safe side, you should reduce your speed and drive past the vehicle with extra attention.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I ready myself for hazards occurring suddenly
answerCSource: I continue driving just as before
questionTextTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan transport vositasini ko‘rsangiz, nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan bo‘lsa, buning ko‘plab sabablari bo‘lishi mumkin. Xavfsizlik uchun tezlikni kamaytirib, transport vositasi yonidan alohida e’tibor bilan o‘ting.
answerBTarget: To‘satdan yuzaga kelishi mumkin bo‘lgan xavflarga tayyorlanaman

questionTextSource: For which purposes is a horn allowed to be used outside built-up areas?
explanationSource: You may announce your intention to overtake by horns or by flash light. You may also use a horn or flash light to warn other road uses and alert them of hazards. The operation of the horn as a call sign, without a specific danger situation, is not allowed.
answerASource: As a warning signal
answerBSource: As an overtaking signal
answerCSource: As a calling signal
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida signal qaysi maqsadlarda ishlatilishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish niyatingizni signal yoki chiroq chaqnashi bilan bildirishingiz mumkin. Shuningdek, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilarini ogohlantirish va xavflardan xabardor qilish uchun signal yoki chiroq chaqnashidan foydalanishingiz mumkin. Aniq xavfli vaziyat bo‘lmasa, signalni chaqirish belgisi sifatida ishlatishga ruxsat berilmaydi.

questionTextSource: For which purposes is a horn allowed to be used in built-up areas?
explanationSource: In built-up areas, the horn may only be used by the driver as a warning signal in the event of a dangerous situation. As it would be an unnecessary noise nuisance, the use of the horn for greeting purposes or to indicate that you want to overtake, is inadmissible in built-up areas. Only outside of built-up areas, you may use the horn or flash light to announce an overtaking process.
answerASource: As a warning signal
answerBSource: As an overtaking signal
answerCSource: As a calling signal
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududlarida signal qaysi maqsadlarda ishlatilishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Aholi yashash hududlarida haydovchi signalni faqat xavfli vaziyatda ogohlantiruvchi signal sifatida ishlatishi mumkin. Salomlashish uchun yoki quvib o‘tmoqchi ekanini bildirish uchun signal ishlatish bu hududlarda keraksiz shovqin bo‘lgani sabab ruxsat etilmaydi. Faqat aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida quvib o‘tishni signal yoki chiroq chaqnashi bilan bildirish mumkin.

questionTextSource: What do you do in case of a breakdown in a tunnel?
explanationSource: In the tunnel, the alternatives for subsequent traffic are low. Therefore, if possible, stop in one of the marked emergency bays in the event of a breakdown. Switch on the hazard light on your car and switch off the engine due to the high risk of poisoning by exhaust gases in the tunnel.
answerASource: Switch on the hazard warning lights
answerBSource: If possible, park the vehicle in an emergency bay
answerCSource: Let the engine run by way of precaution
questionTextTarget: Tunnelda buzilish yuz bersa nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Tunnelda ortdan kelayotgan transport uchun muqobil harakat imkoniyatlari kam. Shuning uchun buzilish yuz bersa, imkon bo‘lsa, belgilangan favqulodda to‘xtash joylaridan birida to‘xtang. Avtomobilingizning avariya chiroqlarini yoqing va tunnelda chiqindi gazlaridan zaharlanish xavfi yuqori bo‘lgani sabab dvigatelni o‘chiring.
answerBTarget: Imkon bo‘lsa, transport vositasini favqulodda to‘xtash joyiga qo‘yaman
answerCTarget: Ehtiyot chorasi sifatida dvigatelni ishlatib qo‘yaman

questionTextSource: You are driving in a tunnel and approach the end of a traffic jam. What do you do?
explanationSource: If you recognize a traffic jam in front of you, it is important to immediately press the hazard light to warn the drivers behind you of the coming situation. This way, they can react better to the situation. During traffic jams, the car driver should stop the engine but not leave the car. Turning in the tunnel is not allowed.
answerASource: Switch on the hazard warning lights
answerBSource: Make a U-turn and leave the tunnel
answerCSource: At the end of the traffic jam, switch off the engine and leave the vehicle
questionTextTarget: Siz tunnelda ketyapsiz va tirbandlik oxiriga yaqinlashyapsiz. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Oldinda tirbandlikni sezsangiz, ortingizdagi haydovchilarni yaqinlashayotgan vaziyatdan ogohlantirish uchun darhol avariya chiroqlarini yoqish muhim. Shunda ular vaziyatga yaxshiroq javob bera oladi. Tirbandlik paytida haydovchi dvigatelni o‘chirishi, lekin avtomobilni tark etmasligi kerak. Tunnelda qayrilib burilish taqiqlanadi.
answerCTarget: Tirbandlik oxirida dvigatelni o‘chirib, transport vositasini tark etaman

questionTextSource: What must you remember concerning the lighting equipment of your vehicle?
explanationSource: The car driver must properly install the lighting equipment on the vehicle. In addition to full functionality, the lighting must also be clean, so that other drivers see you in bad weather or in a tunnel.
answerASource: It must be correctly installed
answerBSource: It must be clean
answerCSource: It must be in operating condition
questionTextTarget: Transport vositangizning yoritish uskunalari haqida nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Avtomobil haydovchisi transport vositasidagi yoritish uskunalarini to‘g‘ri o‘rnatilgan holatda saqlashi kerak. To‘liq ishlashidan tashqari, yoritish uskunalari toza ham bo‘lishi kerak, shunda boshqa haydovchilar yomon ob-havoda yoki tunnelda sizni ko‘ra oladi.

questionTextSource: What can cause even dipped headlights to dazzle oncoming traffic?
explanationSource: Head lamps set too high, wrongly installed bulbs, or loading a car the wrong way can dazzle oncoming traffic as well as the car driver driving in front. Back-heavy loads change the radius of the light cone of the low beam; he goes upwards and leads to dangerous glares. You should know the headlamp beam height control on your car.
answerASource: Headlights set too high
answerBSource: Incorrectly inserted bulbs
answerCSource: An overloaded vehicle
questionTextTarget: Qisqa faralar ham qarshidan kelayotgan transportni ko‘r qilishi nimadan bo‘lishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Juda baland sozlangan faralar, noto‘g‘ri o‘rnatilgan lampalar yoki avtomobilni noto‘g‘ri yuklash qarshidan kelayotgan transportni ham, oldinda ketayotgan haydovchini ham ko‘r qilishi mumkin. Orqa qismi og‘ir yuklangan avtomobil qisqa faraning yorug‘lik konusi yo‘nalishini o‘zgartiradi; u yuqoriga ko‘tarilib, xavfli ko‘r qiluvchi yorug‘likka olib keladi. Avtomobilingizdagi fara balandligini sozlash moslamasini bilishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: How is it possible to prevent oncoming traffic being dazzled by the dipped headlights?
explanationSource: In order not to dazzle oncoming traffic, you should pay attention to how your low beam is adjusted and that it is adjusted after repairs to the headlights or after new installations. If you have to change the bulbs, only use approved bulbs.
answerASource: The headlight range can be adjusted depending on the load carried by the vehicle
answerBSource: Only permissible light bulbs may be used in each of the headlights
answerCSource: The headlight setting should be checked after every repair
questionTextTarget: Qisqa faralar bilan qarshidan kelayotgan transportni ko‘r qilishning oldini qanday olish mumkin?
explanationTarget: Qarshidan kelayotgan transportni ko‘r qilmaslik uchun qisqa faralaringiz qanday sozlanganiga va faralar ta’mirlangandan yoki yangidan o‘rnatilgandan keyin qayta sozlanganiga e’tibor bering. Lampalarni almashtirishingiz kerak bo‘lsa, faqat ruxsat etilgan lampalardan foydalaning.
answerATarget: Transport vositasi yukiga qarab fara yorug‘lik masofasini sozlash mumkin
answerBTarget: Har bir farada faqat ruxsat etilgan lampalardan foydalanish mumkin
answerCTarget: Har bir ta’mirdan keyin fara sozlamasi tekshirilishi kerak

questionTextSource: A railway employee is waving a white-red-white flag at a level crossing. What does this mean?
explanationSource: If a railroad employee waves a white-red-white flag in front of a railroad crossing, you must wait at a safe distance before the crossing. Waving the flag means that the railroad crossing is blocked until the train has passed and the railroad employee stops the blockage.
answerASource: You must wait in front of the level crossing
answerBSource: You may cross the rails carefully
answerCSource: The flag is only meant for the train driver
questionTextTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasida temiryo‘l xodimi oq-qizil-oq bayroqni silkityapti. Bu nimani bildiradi?
explanationTarget: Agar temiryo‘l xodimi temiryo‘l kesishmasi oldida oq-qizil-oq bayroqni silkitsa, kesishmadan oldin xavfsiz masofada kutishingiz kerak. Bayroqni silkitish poyezd o‘tib ketguncha va temiryo‘l xodimi to‘sishni tugatguncha temiryo‘l kesishmasi yopiq ekanini bildiradi.
answerBTarget: Relslarni ehtiyotkorlik bilan kesib o‘tishingiz mumkin
answerCTarget: Bayroq faqat poyezd haydovchisi uchun mo‘ljallangan

questionTextSource: When must you wait at a level crossing?
explanationSource: All road users are required to stop and wait immediately before a level crossing in the following situations: when the signal system's red flashing light comes on, when the barriers start to lower, when the barriers are closed, when a staff member waves a white-red-white flag , You must wait until the blockage is released again.
answerASource: When a flashing red light comes on
answerBSource: When the barriers are being lowered
answerCSource: When a railway employee is waving a white-red-white flag
questionTextTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasida qachon kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Barcha yo‘l harakati ishtirokchilari quyidagi vaziyatlarda temiryo‘l kesishmasidan oldin darhol to‘xtab kutishi shart: signal tizimining qizil miltillovchi chirog‘i yonganda, shlagbaumlar tushishni boshlaganda, shlagbaumlar yopiq bo‘lganda, xodim oq-qizil-oq bayroqni silkiganda. To‘sish yana olib tashlanguncha kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You are approaching a level crossing with half barriers and flashing lights. The red light is flashing but the barrier is still open. What do you do?
explanationSource: When the red flashing light of a barrier system lights up before rail traffic, all road users must stop in front of the diagonal cross. Driving on is strictly prohibited even if the half barrier is still open. The barriers have fast closing times and can jam vehicles. Rail vehicles are approaching at high speeds and have a very long braking distance of several hundred meters. Therefore, they cannot react situationally to obstacles.
answerASource: Wait in front of the St. Andrew's Cross
answerBSource: Proceed as long as the half barrier is open
answerCSource: If no rail vehicle is in sight, cross the level crossing
questionTextTarget: Siz yarim shlagbaumli va miltillovchi chiroqli temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashyapsiz. Qizil chiroq miltillayapti, lekin shlagbaum hali ochiq. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Temiryo‘l harakati oldidan shlagbaum tizimining qizil miltillovchi chirog‘i yonsa, barcha yo‘l ishtirokchilari Avliyo Andrey xochi oldida to‘xtashi kerak. Yarim shlagbaum hali ochiq bo‘lsa ham, davom etish qat’iyan taqiqlanadi. Shlagbaumlar tez yopiladi va transport vositalarini qisib qo‘yishi mumkin. Relsli transport yuqori tezlikda yaqinlashadi va bir necha yuz metrlik juda uzun tormoz yo‘liga ega. Shu sabab ular to‘siqlarga vaziyatga mos ravishda javob bera olmaydi.
answerBTarget: Yarim shlagbaum ochiq ekan, davom etaman
answerCTarget: Relsli transport ko‘rinmasa, temiryo‘l kesishmasidan o‘taman

questionTextSource: A railway employee is waving a red lamp at a level crossing. What does this mean?
explanationSource: All car drivers and other road users must wait at a safe distance before the crossing if a member of the railway staff secures a low-traffic level crossing with a red light. Waving the red light means that the tracks are inaccessible and trains have priority.
answerASource: You must wait in front of the level crossing
answerBSource: You may cross the rails carefully
answerCSource: The red lamp is of no significance for you
questionTextTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasida temiryo‘l xodimi qizil chiroqni silkityapti. Bu nimani bildiradi?
explanationTarget: Agar temiryo‘l xodimi kam harakatli temiryo‘l kesishmasini qizil chiroq bilan himoyalasa, barcha avtomobil haydovchilari va boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilari kesishmadan oldin xavfsiz masofada kutishi kerak. Qizil chiroqni silkitish relslardan o‘tib bo‘lmasligini va poyezdlar ustunlikka ega ekanini bildiradi.
answerBTarget: Relslarni ehtiyotkorlik bilan kesib o‘tishingiz mumkin
answerCTarget: Qizil chiroq siz uchun ahamiyatga ega emas

questionTextSource: You are approaching a level crossing where the barriers are open. What do you do?
explanationSource: Nevertheless, as you approach a railroad crossing with the barriers open, you must reduce speed and be aware of the track. The car driver should make sure that the transition is possible without danger and that no rail vehicle is approaching. When you see that a traffic jam could occur, it is necessary to keep a distance so that no car will end up standing on the rails. Rail transport always has priority.
answerASource: Approach at moderate speed, look along the railway line
answerBSource: Wait in front of the level crossing if you would have to wait on it because of traffic congestion
answerCSource: Proceed at the same speed as road traffic always has precedence in such cases
questionTextTarget: Siz shlagbaumlari ochiq temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashyapsiz. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Shunga qaramay, shlagbaumlari ochiq bo‘lgan temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashayotganda tezlikni kamaytirishingiz va rels yo‘nalishiga e’tibor berishingiz kerak. Haydovchi o‘tish xavfsiz ekaniga va hech qanday relsli transport yaqinlashmayotganiga ishonch hosil qilishi kerak. Agar tirbandlik yuzaga kelishi mumkinligini ko‘rsangiz, hech bir avtomobil relslar ustida to‘xtab qolmasligi uchun masofa saqlashingiz kerak. Relsli transport har doim ustunlikka ega.
answerATarget: O‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashaman, temiryo‘l yo‘nalishi bo‘ylab qarayman
answerBTarget: Agar tirbandlik sabab kesishmada kutishimga to‘g‘ri kelsa, temiryo‘l kesishmasi oldida kutaman
answerCTarget: Yo‘l transporti bunday holatda har doim ustun bo‘lgani uchun xuddi shu tezlikda davom etaman

questionTextSource: At the entrance to an industrial estate, there is a St. Andrew's Cross with the supplementary sign "Industrial estate, rail vehicles have precedence". What must you remember?
explanationSource: Even if there are no barriers and no diagonal crosses at the level crossings in the entire industrial area, motor vehicle drivers must give priority to all rail vehicles. Always expect a train to come, observe the railway lines accurately and approach the tracks only with moderate speed.
answerASource: Approach level crossings at moderate speed only
answerBSource: Rail vehicles have precedence at all level crossings
answerCSource: Motor vehicles have precedence at level crossings without St. Andrew's Cross
questionTextTarget: Sanoat hududi kiraverishida Avliyo Andrey xochi va “Sanoat hududi, relsli transport vositalari ustunlikka ega” qo‘shimcha belgisi bor. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Butun sanoat hududidagi temiryo‘l kesishmalarida shlagbaumlar va diagonal xochlar bo‘lmasa ham, motorli transport haydovchilari barcha relsli transport vositalariga ustunlik berishi kerak. Har doim poyezd kelishi mumkinligini kuting, rels yo‘llarini diqqat bilan kuzating va relslarga faqat o‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashing.
answerATarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmalariga faqat o‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashing
answerBTarget: Barcha temiryo‘l kesishmalarida relsli transport vositalari ustunlikka ega
answerCTarget: Avliyo Andrey xochi bo‘lmagan temiryo‘l kesishmalarida motorli transport vositalari ustunlikka ega

questionTextSource: These level crossing barriers have not moved for some time now. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource:
Apparently there is a fault with the railway barrier. It is therefore not safe to use the railway crossing. Do not cross the railway crossing under any circumstances; if a train is approaching, there is an acute danger to your life and there is also a risk that you will not be able to get through under the barriers and get stuck.
Inform the police about the fault at the railway crossing so that the problem can be fixed.
answerASource: I do not cross the level crossing
answerBSource: I report the fault to the police
answerCSource: I cross the level crossing taking extra care
questionTextTarget: Bu temiryo‘l kesishmasi shlagbaumlari anchadan beri harakatlanmayapti. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Ko‘rinishidan temiryo‘l shlagbaumida nosozlik bor. Shuning uchun temiryo‘l kesishmasidan foydalanish xavfsiz emas. Hech qanday holatda kesishmadan o‘tmang; agar poyezd yaqinlashsa, hayotingiz uchun keskin xavf tug‘iladi va shlagbaum ostidan o‘ta olmay, tiqilib qolish xavfi ham bor. Nosozlik tuzatilishi uchun temiryo‘l kesishmasidagi nosozlik haqida politsiyaga xabar bering.
answerCTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasidan juda ehtiyotkorlik bilan o‘taman

questionTextSource: You are driving along a forest track and approach a level crossing without St. Andrew's Cross. What do you do?
explanationSource: Even without the diagonal cross, a car driver must always give priority to a rail vehicle on field and forest paths. Approach the railroad crossing at moderate speed and listen for a train approaching. Observe the track and stop, if necessary, to let the rail vehicle pass.
answerASource: Proceed at moderate speed and check to see if a rail vehicle is approaching
answerBSource: Listen out in case a rail vehicle signals its approach by whistling or ringing a bell
answerCSource: Proceed without taking any special precautions since rail vehicles are required to wait here
questionTextTarget: Siz o‘rmon yo‘lida ketyapsiz va Avliyo Andrey xochi bo‘lmagan temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashyapsiz. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Diagonal xoch bo‘lmasa ham, dala va o‘rmon yo‘llarida haydovchi relsli transport vositasiga har doim ustunlik berishi kerak. Temiryo‘l kesishmasiga o‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashing va poyezd yaqinlashayotganini eshitishga harakat qiling. Relslarni kuzating va zarur bo‘lsa relsli transportni o‘tkazish uchun to‘xtang.
answerATarget: O‘rtacha tezlikda davom etaman va relsli transport yaqinlashayotganini tekshiraman
answerBTarget: Relsli transport hushtak yoki qo‘ng‘iroq bilan yaqinlashayotganini bildirishi mumkinligini tinglayman
answerCTarget: Maxsus ehtiyot chorasiz davom etaman, chunki relsli transport bu yerda kutishi kerak

questionTextSource: What do you do at a level crossing when the barriers are closed?
explanationSource: At a level crossing with closed gates, all car drivers have to wait in front of the diagonal cross. If there is no traffic sign, a safe distance to the rails must be observed. Be careful not to block intersections or T-junctions when waiting. In the dark, lighting must be switched on, parking light is sufficient.
answerASource: Wait before the St. Andrew's Cross without obstructing junctions
answerBSource: If possible, switch on the side lights when it is dark
answerCSource: Use the road area and wait directly in front of the barrier
questionTextTarget: Shlagbaumlar yopiq bo‘lgan temiryo‘l kesishmasida nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Shlagbaumlari yopiq temiryo‘l kesishmasida barcha haydovchilar diagonal xoch oldida kutishi kerak. Yo‘l belgisi bo‘lmasa, relslargacha xavfsiz masofa saqlanishi kerak. Kutayotganda chorraha yoki T-kesishmalarni to‘sib qo‘ymaslikka e’tibor bering. Qorong‘ida yoritish yoqilishi kerak, parkovka chirog‘i yetarli.
answerATarget: Kesishmalarga xalaqit bermasdan Avliyo Andrey xochi oldida kutaman
answerBTarget: Qorong‘ida imkon bo‘lsa yon chiroqlarni yoqaman
answerCTarget: Yo‘l hududidan foydalanib, bevosita shlagbaum oldida kutaman

questionTextSource: When are you allowed to cross a level crossing with flashing lights but no barriers after a train has passed?
explanationSource: You may only cross the level crossing after the red flashing light turns off, as another train could follow. Do not be fooled by oncoming traffic to drive on if the car driver waiting on the opposite site already starts driving too early after the train has passed through.
answerASource: When the flashing red light goes out
answerBSource: Immediately after the train has passed, even if the flashing red light is still on
answerCSource: As soon as the oncoming traffic sets off, even if the flashing red light is still on
questionTextTarget: Poyezd o‘tib ketgandan keyin miltillovchi chiroqlari bor, lekin shlagbaumi yo‘q temiryo‘l kesishmasidan qachon o‘tishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasidan faqat qizil miltillovchi chiroq o‘chganidan keyin o‘tishingiz mumkin, chunki ortidan yana bir poyezd kelishi mumkin. Qarama-qarshi tomonda kutayotgan haydovchi poyezd o‘tishi bilan juda erta yurishni boshlasa ham, bunga aldanib davom etmang.
answerBTarget: Poyezd o‘tishi bilan darhol, qizil miltillovchi chiroq hali yoniq bo‘lsa ham
answerCTarget: Qarama-qarshi transport harakatlana boshlashi bilan, qizil miltillovchi chiroq hali yoniq bo‘lsa ham

questionTextSource: You are approaching a level crossing with barriers. Where must you wait if you would have to stop on the rails in case of traffic congestion?
explanationSource: If you are unable to pass the railroad crossing quickly due to slow traffic, then stop in front of the diagonal cross. Waiting directly in front of the rails or at the level of the barriers is prohibited, because at these positions you may be hit by the dynamic pressure of the train, by the train itself or trapped by the barriers.
answerASource: In front of the St. Andrew's Cross
answerBSource: Just before the rails
answerCSource: Level with the barrier
questionTextTarget: Siz shlagbaumli temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashyapsiz. Tirbandlik sabab relslar ustida to‘xtashingizga to‘g‘ri kelsa, qayerda kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Sekin harakat tufayli temiryo‘l kesishmasidan tez o‘tib keta olmasangiz, diagonal xoch oldida to‘xtang. Bevosita relslar oldida yoki shlagbaum darajasida kutish taqiqlanadi, chunki bu joylarda poyezdning bosim to‘lqini, poyezdning o‘zi yoki shlagbaumlar sizga xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: What do you do here?
explanationSource: All car drivers and other road users must wait at a safe distance if railway staff guards a low-traffic level crossing with a white-red-white flag. Under no circumstances should you cross the tracks here, as the transition is blocked by the swinging of the flag and trains have priority.
answerASource: Approach at moderate speed and wait
answerBSource: Proceed as the roadway is only blocked for oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Temiryo‘l xodimi kam harakatli temiryo‘l kesishmasini oq-qizil-oq bayroq bilan qo‘riqlayotgan bo‘lsa, barcha haydovchilar va boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilari xavfsiz masofada kutishi kerak. Hech qanday holatda relslardan o‘tmang, chunki bayroq silkitilishi bilan o‘tish yopilgan va poyezdlar ustunlikka ega.
answerBTarget: Yo‘l faqat qarama-qarshi transport uchun yopilganligi sabab davom etaman

questionTextSource: The barrier of a level crossing inside a built-up area is closed. Where must you wait?
explanationSource: Trucks and vehicles with trailers (vehicles over 7.5 t permissible total mass) are only required to wait outside of built-up areas behind the level crossing (80 m) ahead marker. Inside a built-up area they have to wait in front of the diagonal crosses.
answerASource: In front of the St. Andrew's Cross
answerBSource: Before the last beacon
answerCSource: Before the first beacon
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududi ichidagi temiryo‘l kesishmasi shlagbaumi yopiq. Qayerda kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yuk mashinalari va tirkamali transport vositalari (ruxsat etilgan umumiy massasi 7,5 t dan ortiq) faqat aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida temiryo‘l kesishmasi oldidagi 80 m ogohlantirish belgisi orqasida kutishi kerak. Aholi yashash hududi ichida ular diagonal xochlar oldida kutishi kerak.

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign mean?
explanationSource: The diagonal crosses indicate that rail transport has priority here. As a car driver you need to wait when a rail vehicle is approaching. A lightning bolt in the diagonal cross is an indication of an electrical overhead line, which is important for special transports with a big height.
answerASource: Indication of existing overhead electrical wires
answerBSource: Rail traffic always has priority
answerCSource: You must always wait when a rail vehicle is approaching
questionTextTarget: Bu yo‘l belgisi nimani bildiradi?
explanationTarget: Diagonal xochlar bu yerda relsli transport ustunlikka ega ekanini bildiradi. Haydovchi sifatida relsli transport yaqinlashayotgan bo‘lsa kutishingiz kerak. Diagonal xochdagi chaqmoq belgisi elektr havo liniyasi borligini bildiradi, bu baland maxsus transportlar uchun muhim.
answerATarget: Mavjud elektr havo simlari haqida ko‘rsatma
answerBTarget: Relsli transport har doim ustunlikka ega
answerCTarget: Relsli transport yaqinlashganda har doim kutishingiz kerak

questionTextSource: A light signal with no level crossing sign is positioned before a level crossing, and in front of a junction with a road to the right. The red light is illuminated. Where must you wait?
explanationSource: In the event of a red light, the car driver must stop in any case. In order not to block the traffic from the road going to the right, the red light is located several meters from the actual railroad crossing. Wait at the stop line or at the light sign if no line is available.
answerASource: At the stop line
answerBSource: In front of the light signal, if there is no stop line
answerCSource: I can drive up to the level crossing after watching out for other traffic
questionTextTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasidan va o‘ng tomonga yo‘l qo‘shilishidan oldin, temiryo‘l kesishmasi belgisisiz svetofor joylashgan. Qizil chiroq yonib turibdi. Qayerda kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qizil chiroq bo‘lsa, haydovchi har qanday holatda to‘xtashi kerak. O‘ng tomonga ketadigan yo‘ldan transportni to‘sib qo‘ymaslik uchun qizil chiroq temiryo‘l kesishmasidan bir necha metr oldinda joylashtirilgan. To‘xtash chizig‘ida kuting, chiziq bo‘lmasa svetofor oldida kuting.
answerCTarget: Boshqa transportga e’tibor berib, temiryo‘l kesishmasigacha borishim mumkin

questionTextSource: What must you do in this situation?
explanationSource: The railway employee secures the level crossing and therefore you should stop before the St. Andrew's cross.
answerASource: - stop in front of the level crossing
answerBSource: - drive on past to the left of the flagman
answerCSource: - drive on past to the right of the flagman
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Temiryo‘l xodimi temiryo‘l kesishmasini xavfsizlantirmoqda, shuning uchun siz Avliyo Andrey xochi oldida to‘xtashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: Who is required to stop at a level crossing with a red flashing light in the form of an arrow point to the right?
explanationSource: A diagonal cross with a red flashing light in the form of an arrow to the right can be found behind an intersection or T-junction after a railroad crossing. The right arrow indicates that the car drivers who turn right must wait.
answerASource: Traffic turning right
answerBSource: Traffic moving straight ahead
answerCSource: Traffic turning left
questionTextTarget: O‘ngga yo‘naltirilgan qizil miltillovchi chiroqli temiryo‘l kesishmasida kim to‘xtashi kerak?
explanationTarget: O‘ngga yo‘naltirilgan qizil miltillovchi chiroqli diagonal xoch temiryo‘l kesishmasidan keyingi chorraha yoki T-kesishma ortida joylashishi mumkin. O‘ng strelka o‘ngga burilayotgan haydovchilar kutishi kerakligini bildiradi.

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign combination shows you a level crossing. The car driver may approach the level crossing only at 10 km/h and cross it at that speed.
answerASource: - which I may approach at a maximum speed of 10 km/h
answerBSource: - which I may cross at a maximum speed of 10 km/h
answerCSource: - over which rail traffic may pass at a maximum speed of 10 km/h
questionTextTarget: Bu yo‘l belgilari kombinatsiyasi nimani bildiradi?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l belgilari kombinatsiyasi temiryo‘l kesishmasini ko‘rsatadi. Haydovchi temiryo‘l kesishmasiga faqat 10 km/soat tezlikda yaqinlashishi va shu tezlikda kesib o‘tishi mumkin.
answerATarget: - unga maksimal 10 km/soat tezlikda yaqinlashishim mumkin
answerBTarget: - uni maksimal 10 km/soat tezlikda kesib o‘tishim mumkin
answerCTarget: - undan relsli transport maksimal 10 km/soat tezlikda o‘tishi mumkin

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: If there is a risk that you might come to a stop on the rails due to a traffic jam situation or stagnant traffic, stop in front of the diagonal cross and wait until you can safely and quickly cross the level crossing. Fatal accidents occur when a car standing on the rails cannot avoid the approaching train.
answerASource: I wait in front of the level crossing
answerBSource: I carefully cross the tracks
answerCSource: I wait at the tracks
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Tirbandlik yoki sekin harakat sabab relslar ustida to‘xtab qolish xavfi bo‘lsa, diagonal xoch oldida to‘xtang va temiryo‘l kesishmasidan xavfsiz hamda tez o‘tishingiz mumkin bo‘lguncha kuting. Relslar ustida turgan avtomobil yaqinlashayotgan poyezddan qochib qutula olmasa, halokatli avariyalar yuz beradi.

questionTextSource: You are approaching a rail crossing. The barrier begins to lower. What should you do?
explanationSource: When a driver approaches a gate that lowers, he/she must wait in front of the level crossing. This also applies if no rail vehicle is yet to be seen. Switching on the hazard light is not required.
answerASource: I wait in front of the level crossing
answerBSource: I continue driving if there is no train in sight
answerCSource: I switch on my hazard lights
questionTextTarget: Siz temiryo‘l kesishmasiga yaqinlashyapsiz. Shlagbaum tushishni boshladi. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi tushayotgan shlagbaumga yaqinlashsa, temiryo‘l kesishmasi oldida kutishi kerak. Bu hali relsli transport ko‘rinmayotgan bo‘lsa ham amal qiladi. Avariya chiroqlarini yoqish talab qilinmaydi.

questionTextSource: Where do you have to anticipate encountering rail crossings with no technical safeguards?
explanationSource: In industrial and port areas, on field and forest paths, as well as on roads with little traffic, you should expect a railway crossing without a barrier or traffic light (technically unprotected).
answerASource: On less-frequently used roads
answerBSource: In industrial zones and port areas
answerCSource: On dirt tracks and forest tracks
questionTextTarget: Texnik himoya vositalari bo‘lmagan temiryo‘l kesishmalarini qayerda uchratishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Sanoat va port hududlarida, dala va o‘rmon yo‘llarida hamda kam harakatli yo‘llarda shlagbaum yoki svetoforsiz, ya’ni texnik himoyasiz temiryo‘l kesishmasini kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You want to cross a level crossing with the barriers open. What is correct?
explanationSource: Even when the barriers are open, you must always approach level crossings with caution and at a moderate speed. This gives you enough time to react to possible bumps and dangerous situations. Moderate speed in this context does not mean walking speed, because you can also drive faster than walking speed. In this way you can prevent possible dangerous situations with approaching trains or lowering barriers.
answerASource: I have to approach the level crossing at a moderate speed
answerBSource: I expect there to be bumps in the road when crossing the level crossing
answerCSource: I am only allowed to cross the tracks at walking speed
questionTextTarget: Siz shlagbaumlari ochiq temiryo‘l kesishmasidan o‘tmoqchisiz. Qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Shlagbaumlar ochiq bo‘lsa ham, temiryo‘l kesishmalariga doimo ehtiyotkorlik bilan va o‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashish kerak. Bu ehtimoliy notekisliklar va xavfli vaziyatlarga javob berish uchun yetarli vaqt beradi. Bu yerda o‘rtacha tezlik piyoda tezligi degani emas, chunki piyoda tezligidan tezroq yurish ham mumkin. Shu tariqa yaqinlashayotgan poyezdlar yoki tushayotgan shlagbaumlar bilan xavfli vaziyatlarning oldini olasiz.
answerATarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasiga o‘rtacha tezlikda yaqinlashishim kerak
answerBTarget: Temiryo‘l kesishmasidan o‘tayotganda yo‘ldagi notekisliklarni kutaman
answerCTarget: Relslardan faqat piyoda tezligida o‘tishim mumkin

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: Level crossings may not be crossed when the flashing lights above the St. Andrew's Cross are on or the barriers are lowered.
answerASource: - stop before the cross
answerBSource: - cross the level crossing quickly
answerCSource: - drive through an open barrier
questionTextTarget: Hozir nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Avliyo Andrey xochi ustidagi miltillovchi chiroqlar yoniq bo‘lsa yoki shlagbaumlar tushirilgan bo‘lsa, temiryo‘l kesishmasidan o‘tish mumkin emas.

questionTextSource: At a tramstop, passengers are getting on and off a tram onto the roadway. What do you do if you want to drive past on the right?
explanationSource: Tram passengers who get off the tram at one stop and walk to the sidewalk or walk from the sidewalk to the tram have absolute priority over vehicle traffic. You as a car driver have to wait. Only when an obstruction or threat to passengers is ruled out, you may pass by the tram carefully at walking pace.
answerASource: Wait if passengers can be endangered or inconvenienced
answerBSource: Drive cautiously at walking speed past if this is possible without endangering or inconveniencing passengers
answerCSource: Give a clear warning signal and proceed
questionTextTarget: Tramvay bekatida yo‘lovchilar tramvaydan qatnov qismiga tushmoqda va chiqmoqda. O‘ng tomondan o‘tmoqchi bo‘lsangiz nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Bekatda tramvaydan tushib piyodalar yo‘lagiga ketayotgan yoki piyodalar yo‘lagidan tramvayga ketayotgan yo‘lovchilar transport harakatidan mutlaq ustunlikka ega. Haydovchi sifatida siz kutishingiz kerak. Faqat yo‘lovchilarga xalaqit yoki xavf bo‘lmasligi aniq bo‘lganda, tramvay yonidan piyoda tezligida ehtiyotkorlik bilan o‘tishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Yo‘lovchilar xavf ostiga qo‘yilishi yoki ularga xalaqit berilishi mumkin bo‘lsa kutish
answerBTarget: Yo‘lovchilarga xavf yoki xalaqit bo‘lmasa, ehtiyotkorlik bilan piyoda tezligida o‘tish
answerCTarget: Aniq ogohlantiruvchi signal berib, davom etish

questionTextSource: What do you do when a bus has stopped at a bus stop with its hazard warning lights switched on?
explanationSource: A bus that stops at a stop with the hazard light on may only be passed at walking pace. Walking speed also applies to oncoming traffic. Drive past the bus at a distance that preempts any danger or obstruction to passengers. Wait, if necessary. A sufficient distance improves the visibility to see pedestrians stepping forward.
answerASource: Drive past at walking speed only and, if necessary, stop
answerBSource: Drive past at a sufficient distance in order to exclude endangering pedestrians
answerCSource: Wait if passengers could be inconvenienced
questionTextTarget: Avtobus bekatda avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan holda to‘xtagan bo‘lsa, nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Bekatda avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan holda to‘xtagan avtobus yonidan faqat piyoda tezligida o‘tish mumkin. Piyoda tezligi qarama-qarshi transportga ham taalluqli. Yo‘lovchilarga xavf yoki xalaqit bo‘lmasligi uchun avtobus yonidan yetarli masofada o‘ting. Zarur bo‘lsa, kuting. Yetarli masofa oldinga chiqayotgan piyodalarni ko‘rishni yaxshilaydi.
answerATarget: Faqat piyoda tezligida o‘taman va zarur bo‘lsa to‘xtayman
answerBTarget: Piyodalarga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun yetarli masofada o‘taman
answerCTarget: Yo‘lovchilarga xalaqit berilishi mumkin bo‘lsa kutaman

questionTextSource: Which vehicles must you allow to set off from marked bus stops?
explanationSource: You have to give priority to public and school buses that want to drive away from marked stops, as long as they announce their intention of departure, for example, by indicating. Exceptions are taxis, as these are not bound to fixed stops and timetables.
answerASource: Regular buses
answerBSource: School buses
answerCSource: Taxis
questionTextTarget: Qaysi transport vositalariga belgilangan avtobus bekatlaridan harakatni boshlashga imkon berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Belgilangan bekatlardan harakatni boshlamoqchi bo‘lgan jamoat va maktab avtobuslariga ustunlik berishingiz kerak, agar ular harakatlanish niyatini, masalan, burilish ko‘rsatkichini yoqish orqali bildirsa. Taksilar bundan mustasno, chunki ular belgilangan bekatlar va jadvalga bog‘liq emas.

questionTextSource: A regular bus in the oncoming traffic has stopped at a bus stop on the same roadway with hazard warning lights switched on. What do you do?
explanationSource: Even if the bus is on the side of oncoming traffic, you are obliged to drive past the bus at walking pace as soon as it stops with the hazard light on. This is also the case when no pedestrians want to cross the street. Likewise, a sufficiently large safety distance must be maintained in order not to endanger persons entering or leaving. The car driver must guarantee all pedestrians safe crossing of the road and, if necessary, wait for them.
answerASource: Slow down to walking speed
answerBSource: Only proceed at walking speed if passengers want to cross the roadway
answerCSource: Proceed at the same speed since the bus has stopped on the other side of the roadway
questionTextTarget: Qarama-qarshi yo‘nalishdagi muntazam avtobus xuddi shu qatnov qismidagi bekatda avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan holda to‘xtagan. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Avtobus qarama-qarshi tomonda bo‘lsa ham, u avariya chiroqlari bilan to‘xtagan zahoti yonidan piyoda tezligida o‘tishingiz shart. Bu, hatto piyodalar yo‘lni kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lmasa ham, amal qiladi. Shuningdek, chiqayotgan yoki tushayotgan odamlarga xavf tug‘dirmaslik uchun yetarli xavfsiz masofa saqlash kerak. Haydovchi barcha piyodalarga yo‘lni xavfsiz kesib o‘tishni ta’minlashi va zarur bo‘lsa kutishi kerak.
answerBTarget: Faqat yo‘lovchilar qatnov qismini kesib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lsa piyoda tezligida yurish
answerCTarget: Avtobus yo‘lning boshqa tomonida to‘xtagani uchun xuddi shu tezlikda davom etish

questionTextSource: You are approaching a regular bus which has stopped in the opposite direction at a bus stop on your roadway and its hazard warning lights are switched on. What must you expect?
explanationSource: It is to be expected that passers-by carelessly and without paying any attention to traffic will cross the street because they still want to reach the bus. Likewise, it can be expected with people exiting the bus who walk on the road in front of or behind the bus.
answerASource: Passengers will cross the roadway in order to catch the bus
answerBSource: Passengers leaving the bus will suddenly cross the roadway
answerCSource: Passengers will cross the roadway only after the hazard warning lights of the bus are switched off
questionTextTarget: Siz qarama-qarshi yo‘nalishda, sizning qatnov qismingizdagi bekatda avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan holda to‘xtagan muntazam avtobusga yaqinlashyapsiz. Nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘lovchilar avtobusga ulgurish uchun yo‘l harakatiga e’tibor bermasdan yo‘lni kesib o‘tishini kutish kerak. Shuningdek, avtobusdan tushayotgan odamlar avtobusning oldidan yoki orqasidan yo‘lga chiqishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Yo‘lovchilar avtobusga ulgurish uchun qatnov qismini kesib o‘tadi
answerBTarget: Avtobusdan tushayotgan yo‘lovchilar to‘satdan qatnov qismini kesib o‘tadi
answerCTarget: Yo‘lovchilar qatnov qismini faqat avtobusning avariya chiroqlari o‘chirilgandan keyin kesib o‘tadi

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You have to slow down and stay behind the bus first. As long as the bus is driving with the hazard lights on, you must not overtake the it. You also can not change the lane because to the left of you is a vehicle that is just overtaking you.
answerASource: I remain behind the bus for the moment
answerBSource: I overtake the bus at a moderate speed
answerCSource: I switch lane without overtaking immediately
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Siz sekinlashib, avval avtobus ortida qolishingiz kerak. Avtobus avariya chiroqlari yoqilgan holda harakatlanayotgan ekan, uni quvib o‘tishingiz mumkin emas. Chap tomoningizda sizni quvib o‘tayotgan transport borligi sabab yo‘lakni ham almashtira olmaysiz.

questionTextSource: You would like to drive past a local transport bus, which has come to a stop at a bus stop. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: It is allowed to drive past a public bus that stops at a bus stop. When passing by, you will usually get into the traffic lane of oncoming traffic. Therefore, pay particular attention to oncoming traffic. It is still wrong to drive as close to the bus as possible though. Because this way you might endanger pedestrians. It is not necessary to drive at walking speed, but drive very carefully.
answerASource: - drive past maintaining sufficient space to the side
answerBSource: - drive past carefully
answerCSource: - watch out for oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Siz bekatda to‘xtagan mahalliy transport avtobusi yonidan o‘tmoqchisiz. Nimaga e’tibor berishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bekatda to‘xtagan jamoat avtobusi yonidan o‘tishga ruxsat beriladi. O‘tishda odatda qarama-qarshi transport yo‘lagiga chiqasiz. Shuning uchun qarama-qarshi transportga alohida e’tibor bering. Avtobusga imkon qadar yaqin yurish esa noto‘g‘ri, chunki bu piyodalarga xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin. Piyoda tezligida yurish shart emas, lekin juda ehtiyotkor haydang.

questionTextSource: You are approaching a local transport bus, which has come to a stop at a bus stop. What must you watch out for when driving past it?
