questionTextSource: You are transporting hazardous goods during winter. What should you do if road conditions deteriorate due to packed snow?
explanationSource: In icy weather, you must take special care to avoid endangering others. If you reach a point where you can no longer guarantee that others will not be endangered, you should find a good place to park.

answerCSource: - always transport hazardous goods to the destination with no intermediate stops

answerBTarget: - zarur bo‘lsa, keyingi mos to‘xtash va parkovka joyini qidirish

explanationSource: In order to continue to ensure the flow of traffic, please go possible on the shoulder of your agricultural tractor.

questionTextSource: What risks could arise if you drive in the right-hand lane alongside a truck?
explanationSource: A truck will diminish both your visibility and other people on the road seeing you (including the driver of the truck)
answerASource: - be unseen

answerCSource: - get burned by the hot exhaust fumes from the truck
questionTextTarget: Yuk mashinasi yonida o‘ng yo‘lakda harakatlansangiz, qanday xavflar yuzaga kelishi mumkin?

answerCTarget: - yuk mashinasining issiq chiqindi gazlaridan kuyish

questionTextSource: A large pool of water has formed on the roadway. What do you do?
explanationSource: You should drive slowly on large pools of water as there is a risk of aquaplaning.

answerBTarget: Tormoz bermayman, oyoqlarimni ko‘tarib suvdan o‘taman

questionTextSource: You are driving in fog on the autobahn and have 50 m visibility. What is the maximum speed you may drive?
explanationSource: If the visibility in fog is less than 50 meters, the driver must not drive faster than 50 km/h. This also applies when there's snowfall or heavy rain.
answerASource: 50 km/h
answerBSource: 70 km/h
answerCSource: 90 km/h
questionTextTarget: Siz avtomagistralda tumanda ketyapsiz va ko‘rinish 50 m. Eng yuqori qanday tezlikda yurishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Tumanda ko‘rinish 50 metrdan kam bo‘lsa, haydovchi 50 km/soatdan tez yurmasligi kerak. Bu qor yog‘ishi yoki kuchli yomg‘irda ham amal qiladi.

questionTextSource: What is the rule of thumb when calculating the braking distance for evasive braking on a dry, even and asphalted roadway?
explanationSource: The rule of thumb for determining the stopping distance is: thinking distance (speed divided by 10 times 3) plus braking distance (speed divided by 10 times speed divided by 10). At 50 km/h, this is 50 divided by 10 times 3 equals 15 m thinking distance plus 50 divided by 10 times 50 divided by 10 equals 25 m braking distance. The stopping distance is 40 m. In case of emergency braking, this value is halved. That would be 20 m in this example.
answerASource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x {Speed in km/h}/{10}) : 2
answerBSource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x 5) : 2
answerCSource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x 3) : 2
questionTextTarget: Quruq, tekis va asfaltlangan yo‘lda favqulodda tormozlashdagi tormoz yo‘lini hisoblash uchun oddiy qoida qanday?
explanationTarget: To‘xtash masofasi: fikrlash masofasi (tezlik/10 x 3) plus tormoz yo‘li (tezlik/10 x tezlik/10). 50 km/soatda bu 15 m fikrlash masofasi va 25 m tormoz yo‘li, jami 40 m. Favqulodda tormozlashda bu qiymat ikki baravar kamayadi, ya’ni misolda 20 m.

questionTextSource: You are travelling at 40 km/h. The braking distance for evasive braking is approximately 8 metres. How long is the braking distance at 50 km/h given otherwise the same conditions?
explanationSource: The normal braking distance of your car is determined by this rule of thumb: Speed ​​divided by 10 times speed divided by 10. To calculate the braking distance during emergency braking, the determined value is halved again (divided by 2). Here there means: 50/10 * 50/10 = 25, divided by 2 = 12.5 meters.
questionTextTarget: Siz 40 km/soat tezlikda ketyapsiz. Favqulodda tormozlashdagi tormoz yo‘li taxminan 8 metr. Boshqa sharoitlar bir xil bo‘lsa, 50 km/soatda tormoz yo‘li qancha bo‘ladi?
explanationTarget: Oddiy tormoz yo‘li qoida bo‘yicha hisoblanadi: tezlikni 10 ga bo‘lish va natijani o‘ziga ko‘paytirish. Favqulodda tormozlashda topilgan qiymat yana ikkiga bo‘linadi. Bu yerda: 50/10 * 50/10 = 25, 2 ga bo‘linsa = 12,5 metr.

questionTextSource: You are driving on a good quality road in good visibility conditions. Within what distance must you be able to stop?
explanationSource: With good visibility on a developed road, you must be able to stop within the assessable distance. At night, the range of the low beam is considered assessable.
answerASource: - the stretch of road visible to me
answerBSource: - half of the stretch of road visible to me
answerCSource: - twice the stretch of road visible to me
questionTextTarget: Yaxshi ko‘rinish sharoitida sifatli yo‘lda ketyapsiz. Qaysi masofada to‘xtay olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yaxshi ko‘rinish va yaxshi qurilgan yo‘lda siz baholay oladigan masofa ichida to‘xtay olishingiz kerak. Tunda qisqa faralar yoritadigan masofa baholay olinadigan masofa hisoblanadi.
answerCTarget: - menga ko‘rinadigan yo‘l qismining ikki baravari

questionTextSource: You are driving in darkness on a well-constructed main road, using your dipped headlights. Within what distance must you be able to stop?
explanationSource: You must be able to stop within the range of the low beam, as the parking light is so weak that it has no illumination visible to you. If you need the range of the high beam to stop, you risk a collision.
answerASource: - dipped headlights
answerBSource: - main beam lights
answerCSource: - side lights
questionTextTarget: Qorong‘ida yaxshi qurilgan asosiy yo‘lda qisqa faralar bilan ketyapsiz. Qaysi masofada to‘xtay olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qisqa faralar yoritadigan masofa ichida to‘xtay olishingiz kerak. Gabarit chiroqlari juda zaif va sizga yetarli yoritish bermaydi. Agar to‘xtash uchun uzoq faralar masofasiga muhtoj bo‘lsangiz, to‘qnashuv xavfi tug‘iladi.

questionTextSource: What must you remember particularly when driving with winter tyres (M + S)?
explanationSource: The car driver must comply with the maximum speed limit when driving with winter tires. The speed depends on the type of winter tires and may exceed 80 km/h.

answerBSource: You may not drive faster than 80 km/h
answerCSource: The maximum permissible speed for cars fitted with snow chains applies
questionTextTarget: Qishki shinalar (M + S) bilan haydashda ayniqsa nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Qishki shinalar bilan haydashda haydovchi maksimal ruxsat etilgan tezlikka rioya qilishi kerak. Tezlik qishki shina turiga bog‘liq va 80 km/soatdan yuqori bo‘lishi mumkin.

answerCTarget: Qor zanjiri o‘rnatilgan avtomobillar uchun maksimal tezlik amal qiladi

questionTextSource: You are driving with dipped headlights on a main road. Within what distance must you be able to stop?
explanationSource: You are only allowed to drive so fast that you can stop within the assessable track. In this situation, this corresponds to the range of your low beam.

answerBSource: - main beam lights
answerCSource: - side lights
questionTextTarget: Qisqa faralar bilan asosiy yo‘lda ketyapsiz. Qaysi masofada to‘xtay olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Siz faqat ko‘ra va baholay oladigan yo‘l qismi ichida to‘xtay oladigan tezlikda haydashingiz mumkin. Bu vaziyatda bu qisqa faralaringiz yoritadigan masofaga teng.

questionTextSource: What vehicles are subject to the recommended speed on motorways?
explanationSource: On motorways, all cars and vehicles up to a maximum gross vehicle weight of 3.5 t have to observe the freeway speed limit. Exceeding this speed limit is not recommended even in favorable traffic conditions.
answerASource: - cars
answerBSource: - motorbikes
answerCSource: - Trucks with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 3.5 t
questionTextTarget: Avtomagistrallardagi tavsiya etilgan tezlik qaysi transport vositalariga taalluqli?
explanationTarget: Avtomagistrallarda barcha yengil avtomobillar va maksimal umumiy og‘irligi 3,5 t gacha bo‘lgan transport vositalari tavsiya etilgan tezlikka rioya qilishi kerak. Qulay sharoitda ham bu tezlikdan oshish tavsiya etilmaydi.
answerCTarget: - umumiy og‘irligi 3,5 t dan ortiq yuk mashinalari

questionTextSource: On what roads does the recommended speed of 130 km/h apply?
explanationSource: On freeway and roads outside the city, whose lanes are structurally separated, an advisory speed limit of 130 km/h applies. However, you must keep enough distance to be able to stop any time.
answerASource: Motorways
answerBSource: On vehicle roadways not inside a built-up area, with separate carriageways for each direction
answerCSource: On vehicle roadways not inside a built-up area, with at least two marked lanes for each direction
questionTextTarget: 130 km/soat tavsiya etilgan tezlik qaysi yo‘llarda amal qiladi?
explanationTarget: Avtomagistrallarda va aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi yo‘llarda, agar yo‘nalishlar konstruktiv ravishda ajratilgan bo‘lsa, 130 km/soat tavsiya etilgan tezlik amal qiladi. Biroq har doim to‘xtay oladigan darajada yetarli masofa saqlashingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida, har bir yo‘nalish uchun alohida qatnov qismlari bo‘lgan yo‘llarda
answerCTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida, har bir yo‘nalishda kamida ikki belgilangan yo‘lak bo‘lgan yo‘llarda

questionTextSource: You are driving at 80 km/h behind a motor vehicle. What is the minimum safe distance you must observe?
explanationSource: When driving behind another vehicle, you need to maintain the so-called "two-second distance" to be able to stop in time. At a speed of 80 km/h, this corresponds to about 40 meters.
answerASource: A "distance of 2 seconds"
answerBSource: A "distance of 1 second"
answerCSource: A distance of 15 metres
questionTextTarget: Siz motorli transport vositasi ortidan 80 km/soat tezlikda ketyapsiz. Minimal xavfsiz masofa qancha bo‘lishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Boshqa transport vositasi ortidan yurganda vaqtida to‘xtash uchun “ikki soniya masofasi”ni saqlashingiz kerak. 80 km/soat tezlikda bu taxminan 40 metrga teng.

questionTextSource: You are driving in urban traffic on a dry roadway in a stream of traffic travelling at a speed of 50 km/h. What is the minimum safe distance you must keep from the vehicle in front?
explanationSource: In urban areas, as well as in city traffic, shorter distances are justified according to jurisdiction than out of town. The general "two-second distance" may be reduced to one second with attentive driving and favorable road conditions. At 50 km/h, this is about 15 meters. That's about three car lengths.
answerASource: 15 m or approximately 3 car lengths
answerBSource: 10 m or approximately 2 car lengths
answerCSource: 5 m or approximately 1 car length
questionTextTarget: Siz shahar harakatida, quruq yo‘lda 50 km/soat tezlikdagi transport oqimida ketyapsiz. Oldingizdagi transport vositasigacha minimal xavfsiz masofa qancha bo‘lishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Shahar ichida, ya’ni shahar harakatida, sud amaliyotiga ko‘ra, shahar tashqarisiga qaraganda qisqaroq masofa yetarli bo‘lishi mumkin. Umumiy “ikki soniya masofasi” diqqatli haydash va qulay yo‘l sharoitida bir soniyagacha qisqartirilishi mumkin. 50 km/soat tezlikda bu taxminan 15 metr, ya’ni uchta avtomobil uzunligiga teng.

questionTextSource: What is the minimum distance that should normally be maintained from the vehicle in front outside built-up areas?
explanationSource: Outside built-up areas, the distance to the vehicle in front must be at least half the mileage in meters, that means at 100 km/h the distance should be about 50 meters. Even the so-called 'two-second distance' was legally recognized.
answerASource: 1/2 the speedometer reading in metres
answerBSource: 1/5 the speedometer reading in metres
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida oldindagi transport vositasigacha odatda qanday minimal masofa saqlanishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida oldindagi transport vositasigacha masofa metrda tezlik ko‘rsatkichining kamida yarmi bo‘lishi kerak. Masalan, 100 km/soatda masofa taxminan 50 metr bo‘lishi kerak. “Ikki soniya masofasi” ham huquqiy jihatdan tan olingan.

questionTextSource: When driving outside of a built-up area, what minimum safety distance should you maintain between you and the vehicle ahead?
explanationSource:
Outside of closed towns, the StVO prescribes a safety distance of 2 seconds. This then corresponds again to the "half speedometer" rule of thumb.
Attention: These rules only apply to dry roads. In the case of rain, snow and black ice, the safety distance should be at least twice as large.
answerASource: One half of the speedometer reading in metres
answerBSource: One third of the speedometer reading in metres
answerCSource: One fifth of the speedometer reading in metres
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida haydaganda oldingizdagi transport vositasigacha qanday minimal xavfsiz masofa saqlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida StVO ikki soniyalik xavfsiz masofani belgilaydi. Bu yana “spidometrning yarmi” qoidasiga mos keladi. E’tibor bering: bu qoidalar faqat quruq yo‘l uchun amal qiladi. Yomg‘ir, qor yoki muzda xavfsiz masofa kamida ikki baravar katta bo‘lishi kerak.

questionTextSource: You are driving your car and caravan trailer (length of combination of vehicles exceeding 7 m) outside built-up areas on a road with only one marked lane in each direction. What must you remember?
explanationSource: Trains which are longer than 7 meters must keep a distance from the vehicle in front outside of built-up areas which has to be at least half the speed in meters. For cars with trailers, a maximum speed of 80 km/h applies outside the city. That would correspond to a distance of 40 m. This way, overtaking vehicles can go back into lane.

answerBSource: The distance from the car in front must not be greater than the length of your combination of vehicles in order to make best use of traffic space

questionTextTarget: Siz avtomobil va karavan tirkamasi bilan, transport birikmasi uzunligi 7 m dan ortiq holda, aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida har bir yo‘nalishda faqat bitta belgilangan yo‘lakli yo‘lda ketyapsiz. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Uzunligi 7 metrdan ortiq transport birikmalari aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida oldindagi transport vositasigacha shunday masofa saqlashi kerakki, quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira olsin. answerCSource: - my reaction time may become longer

questionTextSource: What is true here regarding the distance between you and the vehicle ahead?

explanationTarget: Masofa mos, chunki quvib o‘tayotgan sariq avtomobil sizning oldingizga qayta kira oladi. Sariq avtomobil oldingizga kirgandan keyin unga nisbatan yetarli xavfsiz masofani tiklashingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You are driving a vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating of 7 t at 60 km/h outside of a built-up area. When do you have to maintain such a great distance, that an overtaking vehicle can pull back into lane?

explanationTarget: Agar sizning yo‘nalishingizda faqat bitta yo‘lak mavjud bo‘lsa, quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira olishi uchun yetarlicha katta masofa saqlashingiz kerak.

answerCTarget: Agar mening harakat yo‘nalishimda bittadan ortiq yo‘lak bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: You are driving a truck (12 t gross vehicle weight rating) at 80 km/h on a motorway. What must you ensure?
explanationSource: With your truck (12 t permissible total weight) should be at a speed on the highway at 80 km / h, the minimum distance is 50 m. Please make sure also that the rate does not increase your vehicle.

answerBSource: - the speed of my vehicle does not increase
answerCSource: - the distance between me and the vehicle ahead equal half the speedometer reading in metres
questionTextTarget: Siz yuk mashinasida, umumiy og‘irligi 12 t, avtomagistralda 80 km/soat tezlikda ketyapsiz. Nimani ta’minlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: 12 t ruxsat etilgan umumiy og‘irlikdagi yuk mashinangiz bilan avtomagistralda 80 km/soat tezlikda minimal masofa 50 m bo‘lishi kerak. Shuningdek, transport vositangiz tezligi oshib ketmasligiga e’tibor bering.

answerCTarget: - men bilan oldindagi transport vositasi orasidagi masofa metrda spidometr ko‘rsatkichining yarmiga teng bo‘lishi

questionTextSource: Which vehicles have to maintain such a great distance between them and the vehicle ahead when not inside a built-up area, that an overtaking vehicle can pull back into lane?
explanationSource: Here we speak of the so-called Einscherabstand. This is true for trucks of over 3.5t maximum permissible weight and vehicle combinations which are more than 7 m. In a motor vehicle up to 3.5 tons, this is not necessary outside urban areas generally.
answerASource: Trucks with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 3.5 t
answerBSource: Combination vehicles longer than 7 m
answerCSource: Trucks with a gross vehicle weight rating up to 3.5 t
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida qaysi transport vositalari oldindagi transport vositasigacha quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira oladigan darajada katta masofa saqlashi kerak?
explanationTarget: Bu “qayta kirish masofasi” deb ataladi. Bu ruxsat etilgan maksimal og‘irligi 3,5 t dan ortiq yuk mashinalari va uzunligi 7 m dan ortiq transport birikmalari uchun amal qiladi. 3,5 t gacha bo‘lgan motorli transport vositalari uchun shahar tashqarisida bunday talab odatda yo‘q.
answerATarget: Umumiy og‘irligi 3,5 t dan ortiq yuk mashinalari
answerBTarget: Uzunligi 7 m dan ortiq transport birikmalari
answerCTarget: Umumiy og‘irligi 3,5 t gacha bo‘lgan yuk mashinalari

questionTextSource: You are driving in a combination vehicle outside of a built-up area on a road with only one lane in each direction. What distance must you usually maintain to the vehicle ahead?
explanationSource: Must be so great the distance that you should observe that an overtaking vehicle may Reeve.
answerASource: - must be large enough that an overtaking vehicle can pull back into lane

answerCSource: - must be large enough that at least two overtaking vehicles can pull back into lane
questionTextTarget: Siz transport birikmasida aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida, har bir yo‘nalishda faqat bitta yo‘lakli yo‘lda ketyapsiz. Oldingizdagi transport vositasigacha odatda qanday masofa saqlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Saqlanadigan masofa shunday katta bo‘lishi kerakki, quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira olsin.
answerATarget: - quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira oladigan darajada katta bo‘lishi kerak

answerCTarget: - kamida ikki quvib o‘tayotgan transport vositasi qayta yo‘lakka kira oladigan darajada katta bo‘lishi kerak

questionTextSource: You are driving at 100 km/h on a road not inside a built-up area. In front of you a truck is travelling at 70 km/h. What is the minimum distance you must be from a crest in the road when commencing an overtaking manoeuvre?
explanationSource: The rule of thumb for the overtaking path is calculated from the speeds, the overtaking time and the vehicle lengths. The formula is s = v1 / (v1−v2) * (l1+l2+sv+sh). V1/ (V1-V2)) times L. V1 =100 km/h, V2 = 70 km/h, L = 2 times 50 m safety distance plus the vehicle lengths. Truck 12.5 m plus car 4.5 m = 17 m. L = 117 m. 100 km/h divided by 30 km/h times 117 m = 389.9 m, that means 400 m overtaking path. In confusing areas this has to be doubled. You must be at least 800 meters from the crest of the road.
questionTextTarget: Siz aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi yo‘lda 100 km/soat tezlikda ketyapsiz. Oldingizda yuk mashinasi 70 km/soat tezlikda yurmoqda. Quvib o‘tishni boshlaganda yo‘l tepaligi cho‘qqisigacha minimal masofa qancha bo‘lishi kerak?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish yo‘li tezliklar, quvib o‘tish vaqti va transport vositalari uzunligidan hisoblanadi. Bu vaziyatda quvib o‘tish yo‘li taxminan 400 m. Ko‘rinishi noaniq joylarda bu masofa ikki baravar olinadi. Shuning uchun tepalik cho‘qqisigacha kamida 800 m bo‘lishi kerak.

questionTextSource: Fog is limiting visibility to just about 50 m. What applies in this situation for vehicles with a gross vehicle weight rating over 7.5 t?
explanationSource: With limited view your maximum speed must not be higher than 50 h km / on all roads. Outside built overtaking is forbidden for you. Adapt your driving style to the conditions (fog).
answerASource: Overtaking is prohibited on roads outside of built-up areas
answerBSource: The permissible maximum speed on all roads is 50 km/h
answerCSource: Overtaking is permitted on motorways
questionTextTarget: Tuman ko‘rinishni taxminan 50 m gacha cheklamoqda. Umumiy og‘irligi 7,5 t dan ortiq transport vositalari uchun bu vaziyatda nima amal qiladi?
explanationTarget: Ko‘rinish cheklanganda barcha yo‘llarda maksimal tezlik 50 km/soatdan oshmasligi kerak. Aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida sizga quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi. Haydash uslubingizni sharoitga, ya’ni tumanga moslashtiring.
answerATarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi yo‘llarda quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan
answerBTarget: Barcha yo‘llarda ruxsat etilgan maksimal tezlik 50 km/soat

questionTextSource: You are driving on a road not within a built-up area and want to overtake a car. Where is the very latest point at which the overtaking manoeuvre should be completed?
explanationSource: You must finish the overtaking process at the latest before the restriction on overtaking. The restriction on overtaking is indicated by either a traffic sign or a solid line. While you are not allowed to drive over the solid line, you may drive over a broken line in order to overtake.
answerASource: Before the start of a solid line (traffic lane boundary)
answerBSource: Before a no-overtaking sign for vehicles of all types
answerCSource: Before the start of a lane marking
questionTextTarget: Siz aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi yo‘lda ketyapsiz va avtomobilni quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Quvib o‘tish manevri eng kechi qayerda tugashi kerak?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish manevrini eng kechi quvib o‘tish cheklovi boshlanishidan oldin tugatishingiz kerak. Cheklov yo‘l belgisi yoki uzluksiz chiziq bilan ko‘rsatiladi. Uzluksiz chiziqdan o‘tish mumkin emas, uzilgan chiziqdan esa quvib o‘tish uchun o‘tish mumkin.
answerATarget: Uzluksiz chiziq, ya’ni yo‘lak chegarasi boshlanishidan oldin
answerBTarget: Barcha transport vositalari uchun quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan belgidan oldin

questionTextSource: For what reason do you have to switch to the right-hand lane now?
explanationSource: After you have overtaken the green van, you have to merge right again due to the obligation to drive on the right-hand side. But even the red car, which meets you on your lane, has to get back on the right lane again.
answerASource: Because of the rule on using the right-hand lane unless overtaking etc
answerBSource: Because of the red car
answerCSource: Because of the tractor
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir o‘ng yo‘lakka o‘tishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yashil furgonni quvib o‘tgandan keyin o‘ng tomonda yurish majburiyati sabab yana o‘ngga qaytishingiz kerak. Sizning yo‘lagizda qarshingizdan kelayotgan qizil avtomobil ham o‘zining o‘ng yo‘lagiga qaytishi kerak.
answerATarget: O‘ng tomonda yurish qoidasi sabab, quvib o‘tish va boshqa holatlar bundan mustasno

questionTextSource: What is the right thing to do in this situation?
explanationSource: You may drive on the lane of the oncoming traffic if you intend to overtake. To overtake, you must increase your speed above the allowed 130 km/h, which is not permitted in this situation. You must cancel the overtaking procedure and get into the right lane.

answerBSource: I accelerate and continue the overtaking manoeuvre
answerCSource: I continue the overtaking manoeuvre at an unchanged speed
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda to‘g‘ri harakat nima?
explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish niyatida qarama-qarshi harakat yo‘lagidan foydalanishingiz mumkin. Biroq quvib o‘tish uchun ruxsat etilgan 130 km/soatdan tezlashishingiz kerak bo‘ladi, bu vaziyatda bunga ruxsat yo‘q. Quvib o‘tishni to‘xtatib, o‘ng yo‘lakka qaytishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: Quvib o‘tish manevrini to‘xtatib, o‘ng yo‘lakka o‘taman
answerCTarget: Tezlikni o‘zgartirmasdan quvib o‘tishni davom ettiraman

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for now?
explanationSource: You may have to be prepared for the fact that you cannot overtake now because the other road users are already involved in overtaking maneuvers. A solid line represents a restriction on overtaking and is equivalent to the road sign "No overtaking".

answerBSource: - overtaking manoeuvre of the green truck takes longer to complete
answerCSource: - white truck accelerates

Ruxsat etilgan masofada manevrni tugatish uchun shoshilmang. Uzluksiz chiziq quvib o‘tish cheklovini bildiradi va “quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan” belgisi bilan teng.
answerBTarget: - yashil yuk mashinasining quvib o‘tish manevri uzoqroq davom etishiga

questionTextSource: To whom must you pay particularly close attention in this situation?
explanationSource: In this situation, as a car driver you have to pay attention to the cyclist who approaches you from the right. The cyclist could drive onto the road in front of the truck, causing a dangerous situation.

answerBSource: - red car
answerCSource: - white truck

questionTextSource: Why do you have to slow down here?
explanationSource: The overtaking motorcyclist represents a danger to you. He will meet you in your lane during his overtaking process. You should delay and avoid a collision. The behavior of the other drivers is traffic compliant.

explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tayotgan mototsiklchi siz uchun xavf tug‘diradi. Quvib o‘tish jarayonida u sizning yo‘lagizda sizga ro‘baro‘ keladi. Siz sekinlashib, to‘qnashuvdan qochishingiz kerak. Boshqa haydovchilarning xatti-harakati qoidalarga mos.

questionTextSource: You are driving a truck with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 3.5 t. What should you do now?
explanationSource: You should keep to the correct distance and not overtake and reduce the speed.

answerBSource: I reduce my speed

answerBTarget: Agar u quvib o‘tsa, [ko‘k avtomobil]ning darhol ortidan yuraman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?

answerCSource: - overtake the tram on the left

explanationTarget: Quvib o‘tish manevringiz uzluksiz chiziq boshlanishidan oldin yakunlanadigan qilib rejalashtiring. Uzluksiz chiziq va tegishli yo‘l belgisi bu yerda quvib o‘tishga ruxsat yo‘qligini bildiradi.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake a truck with a trailer. What must you be aware of when doing so?
explanationSource: If you want to overtake a truck with a trailer, you should take into account the overtaking distance required because of the trailer (it is longer) and that the vehicle outline of the truck might obscure vehicles ahead.

answerCSource: You may only pull out to overtake once you are directly behind the truck-trailer combination
questionTextTarget: Siz tirkamali yuk mashinasini quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Bunda nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?

answerATarget: Tirkama sabab zarur quvib o‘tish masofasi sezilarli ortadi
answerBTarget: Yuk mashinasi-tirkama birikmasining tashqi qismi oldindagi transport vositalarini yashirishi mumkin
answerCTarget: Faqat tirkamali yuk mashinasining darhol ortiga kelgandan keyin quvib o‘tish uchun chiqish mumkin

questionTextSource: Why are you not permitted to now overtake the bus driving ahead of you?
explanationSource: The bus has turned on the warning lights and is still driving to the bus stop. In this case, it is not allowed to overtake the bus. In addition, the motorbike behind you has started to overtake.

answerBSource: Because of the motorbike
answerCSource: Because of the lack of road markings
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir oldingizdagi avtobusni quvib o‘tishingizga ruxsat yo‘q?

questionTextSource: You want to overtake the vehicle driving up ahead of you. What is necessary to allow you to do so?
explanationSource:
Show other road users in good time that you will move onto the left or medium lane. Each overtaking must be displayed by flashing. After you have observed the rear and side traffic and could make sure that the left lane is free, the indicator must be pressed. Then you can change the lane and overtake the slower vehicle at a much increased speed.
Just make sure that you do not exceed the permissible maximum speed.
answerASource: - considerably increase my speed
answerBSource: - use my indicator to signal my intention to overtake
answerCSource: - observe the traffic to the rear and side of me
questionTextTarget: Oldingizda ketayotgan transport vositasini quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. Buning uchun nima zarur?
explanationTarget: Boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga chap yoki o‘rta yo‘lakka o‘tishingizni o‘z vaqtida ko‘rsating. Har bir quvib o‘tish signal bilan bildirilishi kerak. Orqa va yon harakatni kuzatib, chap yo‘lak bo‘shligiga ishonch hosil qilgach, signal bering va yo‘lakni almashtirib, sekinroq transport vositasini sezilarli yuqori tezlikda quvib o‘ting. Ruxsat etilgan maksimal tezlikdan oshmang.
answerBTarget: - quvib o‘tish niyatimni signal bilan bildirish
answerCTarget: - orqa va yon tomondagi harakatni kuzatish

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The tractor seems to want to turn onto the dirt track on the right. Stay behind the tractor and wait until it has turned off. Overtaking it is dangerous because the implement installed at the rear could swerve.
answerASource: I remain behind the tractor
answerBSource: I anticipate that the tractor may turn off the road
answerCSource: I comply with the tractor driver’s indicator-based prompt to overtake
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Traktor o‘ngdagi dala yo‘liga burilmoqchi ko‘rinadi. Traktor ortida qoling va u burilib ketguncha kuting. Uni quvib o‘tish xavfli, chunki orqa tomonda o‘rnatilgan jihoz chetga chiqishi mumkin.
answerBTarget: Traktor burilib ketishi mumkinligini hisobga olaman
answerCTarget: Traktor haydovchisining signal orqali quvib o‘tishga undashiga amal qilaman

questionTextSource: You want to overtake [the blue car]. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: Wait for the bike to leave the blind spot. Note that the car driver who is overtaking has to drive at a higher speed than the driver who is being overtaken. Accelerate if necessary and finish your overtaking process before the restriction on overtaking takes effect, such as a solid line.
answerASource: - overtake at a substantially higher speed
answerBSource: - overtake while maintaining the speed driven
answerCSource: - do not overtake, because a motorbike is in moving in the blind spot
questionTextTarget: Siz [ko‘k avtomobil]ni quvib o‘tmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Mototsikl ko‘r nuqtadan chiqib ketguncha kuting. Quvib o‘tayotgan haydovchi quvib o‘tilayotgan haydovchidan ancha yuqori tezlikda yurishi kerakligini unutmang. Zarur bo‘lsa tezlashib, uzluksiz chiziq kabi quvib o‘tish cheklovi kuchga kirishidan oldin manevrni yakunlang.
answerCTarget: - quvib o‘tmaslik, chunki ko‘r nuqtada mototsikl harakatlanmoqda

questionTextSource: What could make this situation become hazardous?
explanationSource: The car driver approaching from behind tries to overtake you before the turn, although he/she cannot see the curve. If traffic is approaching in this situation, it can lead to dangerous evasive maneuvers by the oncoming traffic and the driver who is overtaking. The tractor next to the road is no danger.
answerASource: Oncoming traffic
answerBSource: The [red car]
answerCSource: The tractor
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyat nimadan xavfli bo‘lib qolishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Orqadan yaqinlashayotgan haydovchi burilishdan oldin sizni quvib o‘tishga urinmoqda, garchi burilishni ko‘ra olmasa ham. Agar qarshidan transport kelsa, qarama-qarshi transport va quvib o‘tayotgan haydovchi xavfli chetlashish manevrlariga majbur bo‘lishi mumkin. Yo‘l yonidagi traktor xavf emas.

questionTextSource: You are driving on the motorway in a motor vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 7.5 t. The heavy snowfall has reduced visibility to less than 50 m. What must you do?
explanationSource: With your vehicle that exceeds a maximum permissible gross weight of 7.5 t, you are not allowed on the leftmost lane driving, because this is there for overtaking for faster cars. You can not with your vehicle faster than 50 km / h drive. You can continue to drive on the highway even with snowfall.
answerASource: I may not use the extreme left-hand lane
answerBSource: I may not drive faster than 50 km/h
answerCSource: I must leave the motorway at the next exit
questionTextTarget: Siz avtomagistralda umumiy og‘irligi 7,5 t dan ortiq motorli transport vositasida ketyapsiz. Kuchli qor yog‘ishi ko‘rinishni 50 m dan kamga tushirdi. Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: 7,5 t dan ortiq maksimal ruxsat etilgan og‘irlikdagi transport vositangiz bilan eng chap yo‘lakda yurishingiz mumkin emas, chunki u tezroq avtomobillarni quvib o‘tish uchun mo‘ljallangan. Shuningdek, 50 km/soatdan tez yurishingiz mumkin emas. Qor yog‘ayotgan bo‘lsa ham avtomagistralda davom etishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Eng chap yo‘lakdan foydalanishim mumkin emas

questionTextSource: You are driving outside built-up areas. Visibility is reduced to just about 50 m due to heavy rain. What applies in this situation?
explanationSource: Here, too (50 m due to heavy rain) applies in reduced visibility is the speed limit 50 km / h. Motor vehicles above 7.5 t street legal. Total mass is prohibited overtaking. The rear fog light does not have to be switched on.
answerASource: Overtaking is prohibited for motor vehicles with a permissible total mass exceeding 7.5 t
answerBSource: The maximum permissible speed is 50 km/h
answerCSource: The rear fog lamp must be switched on
questionTextTarget: Siz aholi yashash hududidan tashqarida ketyapsiz. Kuchli yomg‘ir sabab ko‘rinish taxminan 50 m gacha kamaygan. Bu vaziyatda nima amal qiladi?
explanationTarget: Bu yerda ham kuchli yomg‘ir sabab ko‘rinish 50 m bo‘lgani uchun maksimal tezlik 50 km/soat. Ruxsat etilgan umumiy og‘irligi 7,5 t dan ortiq motorli transport vositalariga quvib o‘tish taqiqlanadi. Orqa tuman chirog‘ini yoqish shart emas.
answerATarget: Ruxsat etilgan umumiy og‘irligi 7,5 t dan ortiq motorli transport vositalari uchun quvib o‘tish taqiqlangan

questionTextSource: You are driving an agricultural machine. A queue of cars has formed behind you. What is correct?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Reduce speed at an appropriate place or wait in order to let the cars following you overtake
answerBSource: Switch on the hazard warning lights to show that overtaking is not possible
answerCSource: Agricultural machines are not required to let faster vehicles overtake
questionTextTarget: Siz qishloq xo‘jaligi mashinasini boshqaryapsiz. Ortingizda avtomobillar navbati hosil bo‘ldi. Qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Tezroq transport vositalari sizni xavfsiz quvib o‘tishi uchun mos joyda tezlikni kamaytiring yoki kuting. Bu xavfli quvib o‘tish manevrlarining oldini oladi.
answerATarget: Mos joyda tezlikni kamaytiraman yoki kutaman, shunda ortimdagi avtomobillar quvib o‘ta oladi
answerBTarget: Quvib o‘tish mumkin emasligini ko‘rsatish uchun avariya chiroqlarini yoqaman
answerCTarget: Qishloq xo‘jaligi mashinalari tezroq transport vositalariga quvib o‘tishga imkon berishi shart emas

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should pull into the bus stop and let the car behind overtake you

answerBSource: I increase my speed
answerCSource: I maintain my speed and continue driving
questionTextTarget: Hozir nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bu vaziyatda o‘ngdagi avtobus bekatiga kirib, ortingizdagi avtomobilga sizni quvib o‘tishga imkon berishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: O‘ngdagi avtobus bekatiga kirib, u yerda to‘xtayman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The bottleneck is in your lane. Therefore, you must stop before the bottleneck and wait until the oncoming car has passed.
answerASource: I stop and wait ahead of the constricted point until the oncoming car has passed through
answerBSource: I pass through the constricted point by carefully driving between the oncoming car and the construction site barrier
answerCSource: I signal to the driver of the oncoming car that he should drive closer to the edge of the roadway
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Toraygan joy sizning yo‘lagizda. Shuning uchun tor joy oldida to‘xtab, qarshidan kelayotgan avtomobil o‘tib ketguncha kutishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: Toraygan joy oldida to‘xtab, qarshidan kelayotgan avtomobil o‘tib ketguncha kutaman
answerBTarget: Qarshidan kelayotgan avtomobil va qurilish to‘sig‘i orasidan ehtiyotkorlik bilan o‘taman
answerCTarget: Qarshidan kelayotgan avtomobil haydovchisiga yo‘l chetiga yaqinroq yurishini signal bilan bildiraman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action here?
explanationSource: The distance to the bicycle is not enough. Stop and wait until the biker has passed the bottleneck. This is the only way to be sure that you do not endanger the biker.
answerASource: I slow down and wait
answerBSource: I queue behind the vehicles already waiting on the right
answerCSource: I continue driving at a reduced speed
questionTextTarget: Bu yerda to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Velosipedchigacha masofa yetarli emas. To‘xtang va velosipedchi tor joydan o‘tib ketguncha kuting. Faqat shunda unga xavf tug‘dirmasligingizga ishonch hosil qilasiz.
answerBTarget: O‘ng tomonda allaqachon kutayotgan transport vositalari ortidan navbatga turaman

questionTextSource: You are approaching a "red" traffic light. Several cars are waiting in all lanes. What do you do?
explanationSource: You should always wait in line behind the last car in your lane and not try to pass over other cars by either side.
answerASource: Wait in your lane behind the last car
answerBSource: Pass the waiting cars on the right-hand side and proceed to the stop line
answerCSource: Pass the waiting cars on the left-hand side and proceed to the stop line
questionTextTarget: Siz qizil svetoforga yaqinlashyapsiz. Barcha yo‘laklarda bir nechta avtomobil kutmoqda. Nima qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Har doim o‘z yo‘lagizda oxirgi avtomobil ortida navbatda kutishingiz kerak. Avtomobillar yonidan o‘ng yoki chap tomondan o‘tishga urinmang.

answerBTarget: Kutayotgan avtomobillar yonidan o‘ng tomondan o‘tib, to‘xtash chizig‘igacha boraman
answerCTarget: Kutayotgan avtomobillar yonidan chap tomondan o‘tib, to‘xtash chizig‘igacha boraman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: In Germany, you must drive on the right. As there is no vehicle in front of you that is travelling slower than you, you must move into the right lane. As soon as slower vehicles appear, you may move back into the centre or left lane to overtake them.
answerASource: I move into the right-hand lane
answerBSource: I stay in the centre lane
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Germaniyada o‘ng tomonda yurish kerak. Oldingizda sizdan sekinroq ketayotgan transport vositasi yo‘q, shuning uchun o‘ng yo‘lakka o‘tishingiz kerak. Sekinroq transport vositalari paydo bo‘lsa, ularni quvib o‘tish uchun yana o‘rta yoki chap yo‘lakka o‘tishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: How could a dangerous situation arise?
explanationSource: The fast driving style of the yellow truck suggests that he will overtake the blue truck in front of him as well. Their speed is faster than that of the trucks. You cannot dodge to the left as a car on the left lane is directly behind you. If the yellow truck swings out, you have to brake hard.
answerASource: - yellow truck
answerBSource: - green truck
answerCSource: - the car up ahead
questionTextTarget: Xavfli vaziyat qanday yuzaga kelishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: Sariq yuk mashinasining tez haydash uslubi u oldidagi ko‘k yuk mashinasini ham quvib o‘tishini ko‘rsatadi. Uning tezligi yuk mashinalarnikidan yuqori. Chapga chetlab o‘ta olmaysiz, chunki chap yo‘lakda avtomobil bevosita ortingizda. Agar sariq yuk mashinasi chiqsa, keskin tormozlashingiz kerak bo‘ladi.

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: Since the driver of the car on the right of the acceleration lane increases its speed, you should enable him/her to merge into traffic. Refrain from your right of way and reduce your own speed. You cannot change on the left lane as a vehicle approaches there.
answerASource: I let the blue car merge in front of me
answerBSource: I accelerate
answerCSource: I immediately change from the left lane to the central lane
questionTextTarget: Hozir nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: O‘ngdagi tezlanish yo‘lagidagi avtomobil haydovchisi tezligini oshirayotganligi sabab unga harakat oqimiga qo‘shilish imkonini bering. Ustunligingizdan voz keching va o‘z tezligingizni kamaytiring. Chap yo‘lakka o‘ta olmaysiz, chunki u yerda transport vositasi yaqinlashmoqda.
answerATarget: Ko‘k avtomobilga oldimga qo‘shilish imkonini beraman
answerCTarget: Darhol chap yo‘lakdan o‘rta yo‘lakka o‘taman

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: Stay on the middle lane and delay. The leading car will also delay soon to let the car on the left lane merge into traffic, because this lane ends. You cannot go to the left, changing to the right lane would be too dangerous as well.
answerASource: I stay in the middle lane and slow down
answerBSource: I move to the right-hand lane and slow down
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane and overtake the red car in front of me
questionTextTarget: Nima qilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: O‘rta yo‘lakda qoling va sekinlashing. Oldindagi avtomobil ham tez orada chap yo‘lakdagi avtomobil harakat oqimiga qo‘shilishi uchun sekinlashadi, chunki u yo‘lak tugaydi. Chapga o‘ta olmaysiz, o‘ng yo‘lakka o‘tish ham juda xavfli bo‘ladi.
answerCTarget: Chap yo‘lakka o‘tib, oldimdagi qizil avtomobilni quvib o‘taman

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: You drive on the middle lane and in this situation you have to stay on the lane and reduce the speed. The driver on the left lane drives more slowly. You must not overtake him from the right. You cannot change to the right lane, there is a vehicle.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I continue driving without changing speed
answerCSource: I move into the right-hand lane
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Siz o‘rta yo‘lakda ketyapsiz va bu vaziyatda yo‘lakda qolib, tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak. Chap yo‘lakdagi haydovchi sekinroq yurmoqda. Uni o‘ng tomondan quvib o‘tishingiz mumkin emas. O‘ng yo‘lakka ham o‘ta olmaysiz, chunki u yerda transport vositasi bor.

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: Since the vehicle drives very fast from the right onto the freeway, you have to expect that it will change to the middle lane. This impression is reinforced by the fact that trucks maintain their speed and distance.
answerASource: - grey car will move into the middle lane
answerBSource: - yellow truck will pull out in front of me to overtake
answerCSource: - grey car will remain in the right-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nimani kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Transport vositasi o‘ng tomondan avtomagistralga juda tez kirayotgani sabab, u o‘rta yo‘lakka o‘tishini kutishingiz kerak. Yuk mashinalari tezlik va masofani saqlab turgani bu taassurotni kuchaytiradi.
answerBTarget: - sariq yuk mashinasi mening oldimda quvib o‘tish uchun chiqadi

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: A car drives in front of you and from the right a car drives onto the main carriageway of the freeway. It is clear that the car driving on the left lane has a slower pace than you. Since it's only allowed to overtake on the left, you must delay and reduce your speed.
answerASource: I reduce my speed and stay in the middle lane
answerBSource: I maintain my speed and stay in the middle lane
answerCSource: I maintain my speed and move into the right-hand lane
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Oldingizda avtomobil bor va o‘ng tomondan yana bir avtomobil avtomagistralning asosiy qatnov qismiga chiqmoqda. Chap yo‘lakdagi avtomobil sizdan sekinroq harakatlanayotgani aniq. Faqat chap tomondan quvib o‘tishga ruxsat bo‘lgani uchun tezlikni kamaytirishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: You are driving on a road that has several marked lanes going in one direction. When should the alternate merging procedure be employed?
explanationSource: The zipper merge is to be used when a lane cannot be used any further. The exception is on the motorway.
answerASource: When a lane comes to an end
answerBSource: If one lane is not continuously free to traffic due to an obstacle
answerCSource: When a merging lane ends on the motorway
questionTextTarget: Siz bir yo‘nalishda bir nechta belgilangan yo‘lakli yo‘lda ketyapsiz. Qachon navbat bilan qo‘shilish tartibi qo‘llaniladi?
explanationTarget: Zipper usulida qo‘shilish yo‘lakdan keyin foydalanib bo‘lmaydigan holatda qo‘llaniladi. Avtomagistraldagi kirish yo‘lagida esa istisno bor.
answerBTarget: To‘siq sabab bir yo‘lak doimiy ravishda harakat uchun bo‘sh bo‘lmaganda

questionTextSource: What is true about using the hard shoulder in this situation?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates that the hard shoulder is to be used like the right-hand lane.
answerASource: - must be used like a right-hand lane
answerBSource: - may only be used in a breakdown situation
answerCSource: - may only be used by trucks
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan foydalanish bo‘yicha qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l belgisi yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan o‘ng yo‘lak kabi foydalanish kerakligini bildiradi.

questionTextSource: What is true in this situation about using the hard shoulder?
explanationSource: The hard shoulder may not be used regularly. You can only use it in case of a breakdown.
answerASource: – may only be used in the case of a breakdown
answerBSource: – may only be used by trucks
answerCSource: – must be used in the same way as a right-hand lane
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan foydalanish bo‘yicha qaysi javob to‘g‘ri?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l cheti yo‘lagidan odatiy harakat uchun foydalanish mumkin emas. Undan faqat buzilish holatida foydalanishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: What is the right behaviour now?
explanationSource: If one of the lanes ends on a multi-lane road, vehicles must be allowed to move to the adjacent lane. Here it is important to drive with foresight in order to be able to react to lane changes in a timely manner and to adjust your own speed if necessary.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I let the [blue] car in front of me move over
answerCSource: I accelerate
questionTextTarget: Hozir to‘g‘ri xatti-harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Ko‘p yo‘lakli yo‘lda yo‘laklardan biri tugasa, transport vositalariga qo‘shni yo‘lakka o‘tishga imkon berilishi kerak. Bu yerda oldindan ko‘ra bilib haydash, yo‘lak o‘zgarishlariga vaqtida javob berish va zarur bo‘lsa o‘z tezligini moslashtirish muhim.

questionTextSource: What applies to this road that is outside of a built-up area?
explanationSource:
This traffic sign draws you aware that you will have to choose a direction shortly. At the same time, you will receive an idea of ​​the intersection, especially the turning traces.
Therefore, you can only use the left lanes outside if you want to turn left.
answerASource: - to perform a left turn
answerBSource: - to overtake
answerCSource: - if there is a traffic jam
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududidan tashqaridagi bu yo‘lda nima amal qiladi?
explanationTarget: Bu yo‘l belgisi tez orada yo‘nalishni tanlashingiz kerakligini bildiradi. Shu bilan birga chorraha, ayniqsa burilish yo‘nalishlari haqida tasavvur beradi. Shuning uchun tashqi chap yo‘laklardan faqat chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz foydalanishingiz mumkin.

questionTextSource: Where are U-turns prohibited?
explanationSource: You may not turn your vehicle on freeways or motorways. Reverse driving is strictly prohibited on both types of roads as well.
answerASource: On motorways
answerBSource: On clearways for motor vehicles only
answerCSource: On farm or forest tracks
questionTextTarget: Qayerda qayrilib burilish taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Avtomagistrallarda va motorli transport vositalari uchun mo‘ljallangan tezkor yo‘llarda transport vositasini qayrilib burish mumkin emas. Har ikkala turdagi yo‘lda orqaga yurish ham qat’iyan taqiqlangan.
answerBTarget: Faqat motorli transport vositalari uchun mo‘ljallangan tezkor yo‘llarda

questionTextSource: Why do you have to wait before the left turn?
explanationSource: As a left-turning driver, you generally have to give priority to oncoming traffic. Cyclists also have this right of way. So, in this situation you have to let the motorcyclist pass before turning. He will pass the left-turning driver from the right and continue straight ahead.
answerASource: - motorbike
answerBSource: - car
answerCSource: - bicycle
questionTextTarget: Nega chapga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilayotgan haydovchi sifatida siz odatda qarshidan kelayotgan transportga ustunlik berishingiz kerak. Velosipedchilar ham bu ustunlikka ega. Bu vaziyatda chapga burilishdan oldin mototsiklchini o‘tkazib yuborishingiz kerak. U chapga burilayotgan haydovchini o‘ng tomondan o‘tib, to‘g‘ri davom etadi.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: If you want to turn left and a car wants to overtake you from the left, you have to let it pass first. Only after the car has completed its overtaking maneuver, you may turn yourself.
answerASource: - once the [red] vehicle has overtaken me
answerBSource: - now in order to make the carriageway clear
answerCSource: - now so that following vehicles will not be confused by my slowing down
questionTextTarget: To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Agar chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz va avtomobil sizni chap tomondan quvib o‘tmoqchi bo‘lsa, avval uni o‘tkazib yuborishingiz kerak. Faqat avtomobil quvib o‘tish manevrini tugatgandan keyin burilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: - [qizil] transport vositasi meni quvib o‘tgandan keyin
answerCTarget: - hozir, ortdagi transport mening sekinlashishimdan chalg‘imasligi uchun

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: Since you are currently being overtaken, it is not possible to turn left. You must wait for the overtaking process to be finished before you can turn left.
answerASource: I stop at the farm track and wait
answerBSource: I accelerate and swiftly take the turn
answerCSource: I continue driving, because I cannot turn off the road here
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. To‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Siz hozir quvib o‘tilayotganingiz sabab chapga burilish mumkin emas. Chapga burilishdan oldin quvib o‘tish jarayoni tugashini kutishingiz kerak.
answerCTarget: Haydashda davom etaman, chunki bu yerda yo‘ldan burila olmayman

questionTextSource: In this situation, why do you have to wait before the left turn?
explanationSource: In this situation, you need to wait for the motorbike behind the car to pass before turning left
answerASource: - motorbike
answerBSource: - [red car]
answerCSource: - [bicycle]
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda nega chapga burilishdan oldin kutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bu vaziyatda chapga burilishdan oldin avtomobil ortidagi mototsikl o‘tib ketishini kutishingiz kerak.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should first wait for the motorcycle to overtake you before turning left.
answerASource: - after [the red car] has overtaken me
answerBSource: - now, so as not to impede the truck behind me
answerCSource: - now, so that my hesitation does not create any uncertainty in the minds of those behind me
questionTextTarget: Bu vaziyatda to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Bu vaziyatda chapga burilishdan oldin avval mototsikl sizni quvib o‘tishini kutishingiz kerak.
answerBTarget: - hozir, ortimdagi yuk mashinasiga xalaqit bermaslik uchun
answerCTarget: - hozir, ikkilanishim ortdagilarni noaniqlikka solmasligi uchun

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to turn off the road now?
explanationSource: The red car is overtaking and is travelling to your left. To avoid a collision, do not turn off now under any circumstances. Check whether your indicators are dirty and therefore cannot be seen.
answerASource: - [red car]
answerBSource: - bus
answerCSource: - oncoming traffic
questionTextTarget: Nega hozir yo‘ldan burilib chiqishingiz mumkin emas?
explanationTarget: Qizil avtomobil sizni quvib o‘tmoqda va chap tomoningizda harakatlanmoqda. To‘qnashuvdan qochish uchun hozir hech qanday holatda burilmang. Burilish ko‘rsatkichlaringiz iflos bo‘lib, ko‘rinmay qolmaganini ham tekshiring.

questionTextSource: You want to turn left at the next junction. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource:
If you drive past the waiting vehicles, you could obstruct oncoming vehicles and, in the worst case, block the entire road.
Therefore, start to flash left now to signal to other road users that you want to turn into the road. Also, stay behind the waiting vehicles and only turn off when you reach the road.
answerASource: I remain behind the stationary vehicles
answerBSource: I indicate left
answerCSource: I drive carefully to the left past the stationary vehicles
questionTextTarget: Keyingi kesishmada chapga burilmoqchisiz. Hozir to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Kutayotgan transport vositalari yonidan o‘tsangiz, qarama-qarshi transportga xalaqit berishingiz va eng yomon holatda butun yo‘lni to‘sib qo‘yishingiz mumkin. Shuning uchun hozir chapga signal bering, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga burilmoqchi ekaningizni bildiring. Kutayotgan transport vositalari ortida qoling va faqat yo‘lga yetganda buriling.
answerATarget: To‘xtab turgan transport vositalari ortida qolaman
answerCTarget: To‘xtab turgan transport vositalari yonidan chap tomondan ehtiyotkorlik bilan o‘taman

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you steer?
explanationSource: Keep to the centre line when turning left. This allows you to drive as far to the left as possible without driving in the opposite lane and the motorbike behind you can pass you on the right.
answerASource: The middle driving line
answerBSource: The left driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi chiziq bo‘ylab yurishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilganda o‘rta chiziqqa rioya qiling. Bu sizga qarama-qarshi yo‘lakka chiqmasdan imkon qadar chapda yurish imkonini beradi va ortingizdagi mototsikl sizdan o‘ng tomondan o‘tishi mumkin.

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you follow?
explanationSource: You should keep as far to the left as possible when turning left. However, you are riding in a one-way street where cycling is permitted in both directions. You are therefore not allowed to ride on the far left because cyclists may be coming towards you. Therefore, follow the centre line.
answerASource: The middle driving line
answerBSource: The left driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi chiziq bo‘ylab yurishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Chapga burilganda imkon qadar chapda yurishingiz kerak. Biroq siz bir tomonlama ko‘chadasiz va bu yerda velosipedlarga ikki yo‘nalishda harakatlanishga ruxsat berilgan. Shuning uchun juda chapda yurish mumkin emas, chunki qarshidan velosipedchilar kelishi mumkin. O‘rta chiziqqa rioya qiling.

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you follow?
explanationSource: If you want to turn left in a one-way street, you should keep as far to the left as possible, so follow the left lane.
answerASource: The left driving line
answerBSource: The middle driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line
questionTextTarget: Siz chapga burilmoqchisiz. Qaysi chiziq bo‘ylab yurishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Bir tomonlama ko‘chada chapga burilmoqchi bo‘lsangiz, imkon qadar chapga joylashishingiz kerak. Shuning uchun chap yo‘lak bo‘ylab yuring.

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action when reversing?
explanationSource: When reversing, look to the rear and additionally secure the traffic area by looking all around, drive attentively and slowly and, for safety reasons, make sure that there are no persons in the blind spot.
answerASource: - ensure the road area is safe by additionally looking all around me
answerBSource: - drive slowly and attentively
answerCSource: - make sure that no-one is located in my blind spot
questionTextTarget: Orqaga yurishda to‘g‘ri harakat qanday?
explanationTarget: Orqaga yurganda orqaga qarang va qo‘shimcha ravishda atrofga qarab harakat hududini xavfsizligini tekshiring. Diqqat bilan va sekin yuring hamda xavfsizlik uchun ko‘r nuqtada odamlar yo‘qligiga ishonch hosil qiling.
answerATarget: - qo‘shimcha ravishda atrofimga qarab yo‘l hududi xavfsizligini tekshirish
answerCTarget: - ko‘r nuqtamda hech kim yo‘qligiga ishonch hosil qilish

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 3.5 t. At what speed do you have to turn right within a built-up area?
explanationSource: Drive at walking speed to avoid danger and accidents.
answerASource: - walking speed
answerBSource: - at 20 km/h maximum
answerCSource: - at 30 km/h maximum
questionTextTarget: Siz umumiy og‘irligi 3,5 t dan ortiq motorli transport vositasini boshqaryapsiz. Aholi yashash hududida o‘ngga qanday tezlikda burilishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Xavf va avariyalarning oldini olish uchun piyoda tezligida buriling.

questionTextSource: Your vehicle is equipped with a rear view camera. You want to reverse into a forecourt from a very busy road. What must you be aware at this time?
answerASource: I should additionally arrange a look-out to help guide me in
answerBSource: I must also observe the traffic directly
answerCSource: I can rely solely on the rear view camera
questionTextTarget: Transport vositangiz orqa ko‘rish kamerasi bilan jihozlangan. Juda gavjum yo‘ldan hovliga orqaga yurib kirmoqchisiz. Bu vaqtda nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Orqa ko‘rish kamerasi yordam beradi, lekin unga to‘liq tayanish mumkin emas. Siz harakatni bevosita kuzatishingiz va zarur bo‘lsa, sizni yo‘naltiradigan odamdan foydalanishingiz kerak.
answerATarget: Qo‘shimcha ravishda meni yo‘naltiradigan kuzatuvchi tashkil qilishim kerak
answerCTarget: Faqat orqa ko‘rish kamerasiga tayanishim mumkin

questionTextSource: What can reduce the risk of serious accidents when turning right within a built-up area?
answerASource: By equipping vehicles with turn assist systems
answerBSource: By driving at walking speed when turning right
answerCSource: By taking rights turns quickly so as not to impede other road users
questionTextTarget: Aholi yashash hududida o‘ngga burilishda og‘ir avariyalar xavfini nima kamaytirishi mumkin?
explanationTarget: O‘ngga burilishda piyoda tezligida harakatlanish va transport vositalarini burilish yordamchi tizimlari bilan jihozlash jiddiy avariyalar xavfini kamaytiradi. Tez burilish xavfni oshiradi.

answerBTarget: O‘ngga burilganda piyoda tezligida yurish orqali

questionTextSource: When turning right, there is a risk of overlooking cyclists travelling straight ahead in the blind spot. How can you reduce this risk?
explanationSource:
When a bus or truck turns right, there is a risk of overlooking cyclists driving straight ahead in the blind spot. To minimize the risk, you should observe the following and side traffic at short intervals, make sure that your mirrors are adjusted correctly and ensure that no accessories are obstructing your view.
This way you can recognize cyclists in good time and avoid accidents.
answerASource: I monitor the traffic behind and to the side at short intervals
answerBSource: I make sure the mirrors are adjusted correctly
answerCSource: I make sure that accessories in the cab do not obstruct the view
questionTextTarget: O‘ngga burilishda ko‘r nuqtada to‘g‘ri ketayotgan velosipedchilarni ko‘rmay qolish xavfi bor. Bu xavfni qanday kamaytirish mumkin?

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource: In general, the road traffic act prohibits stopping at the traffic areas when stopping is dangerous for other road users or endangers public safety and order. This is especially true for holding on the lanes of the freeway or on a highway. The same goes for sharp and confusing curves.
answerASource: Outside parking areas on autobahns and clearways for motor vehicles only
answerBSource: Near or on sharp bends
answerCSource: At bus stops
questionTextTarget: Qayerda to‘xtash taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Umuman olganda, to‘xtash boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilari uchun xavfli bo‘lsa yoki jamoat xavfsizligi va tartibiga xavf tug‘dirsa, yo‘l harakati qoidalari to‘xtashni taqiqlaydi. Bu ayniqsa avtomagistral yoki motorli transport uchun mo‘ljallangan tezkor yo‘llar qatnov qismida to‘xtashga tegishli. Keskin va ko‘rinishi noaniq burilishlarda ham shunday.
answerATarget: Avtomagistrallar va faqat motorli transport uchun mo‘ljallangan tezkor yo‘llarda parkovka joylaridan tashqarida

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: A parking ban in accordance with the road traffic act applies in all places where other road users can be obstructed or the public order and safety is no longer guaranteed by parked cars. For example, parking is prohibited at taxi stands, narrow lanes or entrances and exits in front of property as well as on and up to 5 m before pedestrian crossings.
answerASource: On narrow roadways opposite driveway entrances and exits
answerBSource: At taxi ranks
answerCSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings
questionTextTarget: Qayerda parkovka qilish taqiqlangan?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l harakati qoidalariga ko‘ra, boshqa yo‘l ishtirokchilariga xalaqit beriladigan yoki jamoat tartibi va xavfsizligi parkovka qilingan avtomobillar sabab ta’minlanmaydigan joylarda parkovka taqiqlanadi. Masalan, taksi turargohlarida, tor yo‘llarda yoki mulk hududi kirish-chiqishlari ro‘parasida, shuningdek piyodalar o‘tish joylarida va ulardan 5 m oldin parkovka taqiqlanadi.
answerATarget: Kirish va chiqish yo‘llari qarshisidagi tor qatnov qismlarida

questionTextSource: Who has priority at this parking space?
explanationSource: The vehicle that reaches the parking space first always has priority. Priority will also be given in the event that the vehicle drives past the parking space just in order to park backwards or perform other movements that are useful for parking in this gap. Priority is also maintained when the vehicle is waiting at the parking space.
answerASource: The white vehicle, because it has arrived at the parking space first
answerBSource: No one, both drivers should agree
answerCSource: Me, because I am parking by driving in forwards
questionTextTarget: Bu parkovka joyida kim ustunlikka ega?
explanationTarget: Parkovka joyiga birinchi yetib kelgan transport vositasi har doim ustunlikka ega. Agar transport vositasi shu joyga orqaga parkovka qilish yoki parkovka uchun zarur manevrlar qilish maqsadida joydan o‘tib ketgan bo‘lsa ham ustunlik saqlanadi. Transport vositasi parkovka joyi oldida kutayotgan bo‘lsa ham ustunlik saqlanadi.
answerATarget: Oq transport vositasi, chunki u parkovka joyiga birinchi yetib kelgan
answerBTarget: Hech kim, ikkala haydovchi kelishishi kerak
answerCTarget: Men, chunki men oldinga yurib parkovka qilaman

questionTextSource: What do you have to consider when parking your motorbike?
explanationSource: The motorbike must not obstruct or endanger anyone. Make sure that the motorbike is parked safely and in a space-saving manner.
answerASource: - may not obstruct anyone
answerBSource: - may not fall over
answerCSource: - must be positioned on the sidewalk
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni parkovka qilganda nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsikl hech kimga xalaqit bermasligi yoki xavf tug‘dirmasligi kerak. Mototsikl xavfsiz va joyni tejaydigan tarzda parkovka qilinganiga ishonch hosil qiling.

questionTextSource: When are you allowed to park your two-wheeler on the footpath?
explanationSource: Basically, the lane is to be used for parking a vehicle. Motorbikes may be parked anywhere where car parking is permitted. In general, parking on sidewalks for motorcycles is not permitted unless a traffic sign permits it.
answerASource: When a traffic sign indicates this is permitted
answerBSource: If the footpath is at least 2.0 m wide
answerCSource: If there are no other options

explanationTarget: Asosan transport vositasini parkovka qilish uchun qatnov qismidan foydalanish kerak. Mototsikllar avtomobil parkovka qilishga ruxsat berilgan joylarda parkovka qilinishi mumkin. Umuman olganda, yo‘l belgisi ruxsat bermasa, mototsiklni piyodalar yo‘lagida parkovka qilish mumkin emas.
answerATarget: Yo‘l belgisi bunga ruxsat berilganini ko‘rsatsa
answerBTarget: Piyodalar yo‘lagi kamida 2,0 m keng bo‘lsa

questionTextSource: What should you take into account when parking a two-wheeler?
explanationSource:
When you park your motorbike or scooter, the same rules and prohibitions apply to you as for car drivers. This means that you are not allowed to simply park your motorbike or scooter on the pavement. You must park your bike on the road, just like car owners. Parking on the pavement is also only permitted for motorbikes or scooters in marked parking spaces or if appropriate traffic signs permit this.
You should also always pay attention to the surface on which you park your motorbike; if the surface is too soft or too uneven, your motorbike can quickly fall over and cause other hazards.
answerASource: I should not park the two-wheeler on soft underground
answerBSource: I may park the two-wheeler on footpaths only if this is appropriately signed
answerCSource: When parking the two-wheeler I am released from the duty to display a parking disc
questionTextTarget: Ikki g‘ildirakli transport vositasini parkovka qilganda nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsikl yoki skuter parkovka qilganda sizga avtomobil haydovchilari bilan bir xil qoidalar va taqiqlar amal qiladi. Mototsikl yoki skuterni shunchaki piyodalar yo‘lagiga qo‘yish mumkin emas. Uni avtomobil egalari kabi yo‘lda parkovka qilishingiz kerak. Piyodalar yo‘lagida parkovka faqat belgilangan joylarda yoki tegishli yo‘l belgisi ruxsat berganda mumkin. Shuningdek, parkovka qilayotgan sirtga e’tibor bering; u juda yumshoq yoki notekis bo‘lsa, mototsikl tezda yiqilib xavf tug‘dirishi mumkin.
answerATarget: Ikki g‘ildirakli transport vositasini yumshoq yerga parkovka qilmaslik kerak
answerBTarget: Piyodalar yo‘lagida faqat tegishli belgi bo‘lsa parkovka qilishim mumkin
answerCTarget: Ikki g‘ildirakli transport vositasini parkovka qilganda parkovka diskini ko‘rsatish majburiyatidan ozodman

explanationTarget: Maksimal ruxsat etilgan massasi 2 t dan ortiq tirkamani sanoat hududlarida va to‘liq turar joy hududlarida maxsus belgilangan parkovka joylarida parkovka qilish mumkin. explanationSource: Please note that you may not leave your vehicle trailers on regular parking spaces for more than two weeks if they are not attached to a towing vehicle. Incidentally, the same also applies to hard shoulders. Accordingly marked parking lots are excluded from this regulation.

explanationSource:

questionTextSource: Before which sign must you maintain a distance of at least 10 m, if it would otherwise be concealed by your vehicle?
explanationSource: In front of signs such as the diagonal crosses, light signals or the sign "Stop! Yield to cross traffic", you at least have to keep a distance of 10 meters. This is especially necessary if they are going to be covered by your vehicle. For example, you are allowed to come to a stop in front of a stop sign. Behind it, however, stopping is prohibited.
answerASource: Diagonal cross
answerBSource: Stop. Give way.
answerCSource: No stopping
questionTextTarget: Agar transport vositangiz belgini yopib qo‘yadigan bo‘lsa, qaysi belgidan kamida 10 m masofa saqlashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Diagonal xoch, svetofor yoki “To‘xtang! Yo‘l bering” kabi belgilar oldida, agar ularni transport vositangiz yopib qo‘ysa, kamida 10 metr masofa saqlashingiz kerak. Masalan, stop belgisi oldida to‘xtash mumkin, lekin uning orqasida to‘xtash taqiqlanadi.

questionTextSource: Where must you use a parking disc for parking?
explanationSource: Wherever traffic signs dictate, the car driver must use a parking disc. It is also used when a defective parking meter makes the withdrawal of a valid parking ticket impossible. The use of a parking disc for a limited stopping restriction does not entitle you to park here for more than three minutes.
answerASource: At a defective parking meter
answerBSource: Where stipulated by traffic signs
answerCSource: In a no-waiting area
questionTextTarget: Parkovka uchun qayerda parkovka diskidan foydalanishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Yo‘l belgisi talab qilgan joyda haydovchi parkovka diskidan foydalanishi kerak. Parkovka avtomati buzilgan bo‘lib, haqiqiy parkovka chiptasini olish imkonsiz bo‘lsa ham undan foydalaniladi. Cheklangan to‘xtash taqiqi hududida parkovka diskidan foydalanish sizga bu yerda 3 daqiqadan ortiq parkovka qilish huquqini bermaydi.

questionTextSource: The time on a parking meter has not yet run out. What do you do?
explanationSource: If a parking meter has not yet expired, you may use the rest of the parking time and leave your vehicle for free at the parking lot for the remaining time. You do not need a parking disc for this.

answerBSource: - not use the time left on the meter
answerCSource: - only use the time left on the meter when using a parking disc
questionTextTarget: Parkovka avtomatidagi vaqt hali tugamagan. Nima qilasiz?

answerATarget: - avtomatda qolgan vaqtdan qo‘shimcha pul solmasdan foydalanish
answerCTarget: - avtomatda qolgan vaqtdan faqat parkovka diskidan foydalanganda foydalanish

questionTextSource: At 10.40, you enter an area where parking is allowed for 2 hours when using a parking disc. What time must you set on the parking disc?
explanationSource: The pointer of the disc should be adjusted to the stroke of half an hour following the time of parking. If you arrive at 10.40 hours, the pointer should be at 11.00. Thereby the parking duration does not matter.
questionTextTarget: Soat 10:40 da parkovka diski bilan 2 soat parkovka qilishga ruxsat berilgan hududga kirdingiz. Parkovka diskida qaysi vaqtni ko‘rsatishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Disk ko‘rsatkichi parkovka boshlangan vaqtdan keyingi yarim soatlik belgi ustiga o‘rnatiladi. Agar 10:40 da kelgan bo‘lsangiz, ko‘rsatkich 11:00 da turishi kerak. Parkovka davomiyligi bu yerda ahamiyatli emas.

questionTextSource: You want to park in a car park where there is a parking-ticket machine. What must you remember?
explanationSource: The car driver must put the valid parking ticket from in the vehicle so that it is visible from the outside. Avoid exceeding the parking time printed on the receipt, also referred to as a parking ticket. A parking disc as a replacement for a parking ticket is only valid if the parking meter is defective.
answerASource: Place a valid parking ticket in your vehicle so it is easy to read from outside
answerBSource: Do not exceed the parking time indicated on the parking ticket
answerCSource: A parking ticket is never needed when using a parking disc
questionTextTarget: Siz parkovka chipta avtomati bor parkovka joyida parkovka qilmoqchisiz. Nimani yodda tutishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Haydovchi haqiqiy parkovka chiptasini transport vositasiga tashqaridan oson o‘qiladigan qilib qo‘yishi kerak. Parkovka chiptasida ko‘rsatilgan parkovka vaqtidan oshib ketmang. Parkovka diski chipta o‘rniga faqat parkovka avtomati buzilgan bo‘lsa amal qiladi.
answerATarget: Haqiqiy parkovka chiptasini transport vositasiga tashqaridan oson o‘qiladigan qilib qo‘yish
answerBTarget: Parkovka chiptasida ko‘rsatilgan parkovka vaqtidan oshmaslik
answerCTarget: Parkovka diskidan foydalanganda parkovka chiptasi hech qachon kerak emas

questionTextSource: When may you remain parked at a parking meter without activating it?
explanationSource: After field work, the lighting and signaling device must be cleaned if necessary. The parking time doesn't need to be monitored while entering or exiting and loading or unloading. When driving slowly up to the priority road, the road must be sufficiently visible.
answerASource: To allow people to get in and out of the vehicle
answerBSource: To load and unload the vehicle
answerCSource: To do some quick shopping
questionTextTarget: Qachon parkovka avtomatini ishga tushirmasdan parkovka joyida qolishingiz mumkin?
explanationTarget: Odamlar transport vositasiga chiqishi yoki tushishi uchun, shuningdek yuk ortish yoki tushirish uchun parkovka avtomatini ishga tushirmasdan qisqa vaqt turishga ruxsat beriladi. Tez xarid qilish bunga kirmaydi.
answerATarget: Odamlar transport vositasiga chiqishi yoki undan tushishi uchun
answerBTarget: Transport vositasiga yuk ortish yoki undan yuk tushirish uchun

questionTextSource: How must you safeguard your motorcycle against unauthorized use?
explanationSource: To avoid other people to use your motorcycle, remove the ignition key and lock the steering.
answerASource: By removing the ignition key
answerBSource: By locking the steering
answerCSource: By engaging top gear
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni ruxsatsiz foydalanishdan qanday himoyalashingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Boshqalar mototsiklingizdan foydalanmasligi uchun kontakt kalitini olib qo‘ying va rulni qulflang.

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle on its prop stand. What do you have to consider?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle and that you swing the side stand all the way up to the stop.
answerASource: The surface under the prop stand must be firm
answerBSource: You must extend the prop stand to the end position
answerCSource: You should only park your motorcycle on an upward slope
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni yon tayanchiga qo‘ymoqchisiz. Nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsiklingiz ag‘darilib ketmasligi uchun tayanch ostidagi sirt mustahkam va mototsikl og‘irligini ko‘tara oladigan bo‘lishi kerak. Yon tayanchni oxirgi holatigacha to‘liq oching.
answerATarget: Yon tayanch ostidagi sirt mustahkam bo‘lishi kerak
answerCTarget: Mototsiklingizni faqat tepaga qiyalikda parkovka qilish kerak

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle on its main stand. What do you have to consider?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle. You can park your bike that way for a short or long time.
answerASource: The surface under the main stand must be firm
answerBSource: You may only use the main stand to park your motorcycle for a short time
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni markaziy tayanchga qo‘ymoqchisiz. Nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsiklingiz ag‘darilib ketmasligi uchun tayanch ostidagi sirt mustahkam va mototsikl og‘irligini ko‘tara oladigan bo‘lishi kerak. Mototsiklni bu tarzda qisqa yoki uzoq muddatga parkovka qilishingiz mumkin.
answerATarget: Markaziy tayanch ostidagi sirt mustahkam bo‘lishi kerak
answerBTarget: Markaziy tayanchdan faqat qisqa muddatli parkovka uchun foydalanish mumkin

questionTextSource: Where should you not park a motorcycle (using main or side stand)?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle. Also make sure that the surface is not too steep downwards which would make the main or side stand unsafe and at risk of tipping over
answerASource: On soft ground
answerBSource: Where the parking surface is a steep downward slope
answerCSource: On firm ground
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklni qayerda parkovka qilmaslik kerak, markaziy yoki yon tayanchdan foydalanganda?
explanationTarget: Mototsiklingiz ag‘darilib ketmasligi uchun sirt mustahkam va og‘irlikni ko‘tara oladigan bo‘lishi kerak. Shuningdek, sirt pastga juda tik qiya bo‘lmasligiga e’tibor bering, aks holda markaziy yoki yon tayanch xavfsiz bo‘lmaydi va mototsikl ag‘darilishi mumkin.
answerBTarget: Parkovka yuzasi pastga juda tik qiya bo‘lgan joyda

questionTextSource: Which precautionary measures must you take when parking a motorcycle?
explanationSource: You must make sure that no other people can use your motorcycle by removing the ignition key and locking in the handlebar/steering. Also make sure that the groud is firm to avoid the motorcycle to tip over.
answerASource: Park on firm ground
answerBSource: Remove the ignition key
answerCSource: Lock the handlebar
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklni parkovka qilganda qanday ehtiyot choralarini ko‘rishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Kontakt kalitini olib qo‘yish va rulni qulflash orqali boshqa odamlar mototsiklingizdan foydalana olmasligiga ishonch hosil qiling. Shuningdek, mototsikl ag‘darilmasligi uchun yer mustahkamligiga e’tibor bering.

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle. What must you consider?
explanationSource: When parking, to avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle.
answerASource: The parking surface must be firm
answerBSource: You can lean the motorcycle against a lamppost if you put it into gear
answerCSource: You must switch on the dipped headlight
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni parkovka qilmoqchisiz. Nimani hisobga olishingiz kerak?
explanationTarget: Mototsikl ag‘darilib ketmasligi uchun parkovka yuzasi mustahkam va mototsikl og‘irligini ko‘tara oladigan bo‘lishi kerak.
answerBTarget: Mototsiklni uzatmaga qo‘yib, fonar ustuniga suyab qo‘yishingiz mumkin

questionTextSource: How do you secure your motorcycle against being used without permission?
explanationSource: The steering lock prevents steering in the event of unauthorised access. The engine cannot be started by removing the ignition key.
answerASource: - remove the ignition key
answerBSource: - close the steering lock
answerCSource: - disconnect the battery
questionTextTarget: Mototsiklingizni ruxsatsiz foydalanishdan qanday himoya qilasiz?
explanationTarget: Rul qulfi ruxsatsiz foydalanishda rulni boshqarishni oldini oladi. Kontakt kalitini olib qo‘yish orqali dvigatelni ishga tushirib bo‘lmaydi.

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when using a prop stand?
explanationSource: If the side stand is not fully retracted, it can cause a fall when cornering. If the slope is too great, the motorbike can slide forward because the side stand folds in on its own. If the ground is too soft, the motorbike may sink until it falls.
